title: exploration_agent: remove exploration_flutter/flutter_test/exploration_cli from pubspec + add no-Flutter guard
description:
## Problem

Once the three Flutter-importing tests are converted to pure-Dart, the Flutter
entries in `exploration_agent/pubspec.yaml` are dead:
- `exploration_flutter` under `dependencies:` — the production half of the
  circular dependency; no `lib/` code imports it.
- `flutter_test` under `dev_dependencies:` — only the (now converted) tests used it.
- `exploration_cli` under `dev_dependencies:` — already unused (no
  `package:exploration_cli` import anywhere in the package).

## Goal

Delete all three lines. Verify `dart test packages/exploration_agent` runs the
entire package green with one runner. Add a guard (a grep-based check, or wire
into the existing `tool/check_no_dart_io.dart` pattern) that fails if any file
under `lib/` or `test/` imports `package:flutter`, `package:flutter_test`,
`package:exploration_flutter`, or `dart:ui`. The exploration_agent ⇄
exploration_flutter and exploration_agent ⇄ exploration_cli cycles are gone.

title: exploration_agent: pure-Dart fake VmService for tests (replaces BindingVmServiceFake)
description:
## Problem

Three exploration_agent test files drive the agent loop through
`BindingVmServiceFake` (from `exploration_flutter/test_support/`), which wraps a
real Flutter `ExplorationBinding`. That is the sole reason exploration_agent
tests need Flutter and the sole reason exploration_flutter sits in
exploration_agent's pubspec.

## Goal

A pure-Dart test fake — in exploration_agent's OWN test tree — that satisfies the
VmService type the agent consumes and returns scripted responses for the
`ext.flutter.exploration.*` RPC surface the agent loop exercises (handshake,
`core.get_stable_observation`, namespaced tool dispatch). No `exploration_flutter`,
no `flutter_test`, no `dart:ui`. `package:vm_service` is itself pure Dart, so this
is achievable.

## Notes for the architect

- `package:vm_service`'s `VmService` is a concrete class. Decide subclass vs. a
  narrower agent-side interface vs. mock — check what `AgentDogfoodHarness` and
  `VmServiceClient` actually consume.
- Study `exploration_flutter/test_support/binding_vm_service_fake.dart` for the
  RPC surface that must be reproduced — the response shapes, not the binding.
- This is the foundation; C2/C3/C4 (the three test conversions) all build on it.

title: exploration_agent must be Flutter-free — break the exploration_agent ⇄ exploration_flutter circular dependency
description:
## Problem

`exploration_agent` and `exploration_flutter` declare each other in production
`dependencies:` — a hard circular dependency:

    exploration_agent  → exploration_flutter   (exploration_agent/pubspec.yaml)
    exploration_flutter → exploration_agent    (exploration_flutter/pubspec.yaml)

`exploration_agent` is the lower architectural layer: a Dart package that must
not depend on Flutter. `exploration_flutter` is the Flutter integration layer
built on top of it. The dependency must run one way only —
exploration_flutter → exploration_agent.

The cycle exists because `exploration_agent/pubspec.yaml` lists
`exploration_flutter` as a dependency that no `lib/` code imports — it is pulled
in solely by three test files that reach into `exploration_flutter`'s
`BindingVmServiceFake`. `flutter_test` is likewise a dev_dependency, and
`exploration_cli` is a stale, entirely unused dev_dependency.

## Goal

`exploration_agent` is free of Flutter — production AND dev. No
`exploration_flutter`, no `flutter_test`, no `dart:ui` anywhere in the package,
its tests included. `dart test packages/exploration_agent` runs the whole
package green with a single runner. The package dependency graph is acyclic.

## Out of scope

- Platform-support policy. `exploration_agent` simply does not depend on
  Flutter; which platforms the product targets is a documentation matter
  decided elsewhere, not a constraint this epic enforces or emphasises.
- `exploration_flutter`'s own tests, which legitimately exercise the real
  Flutter binding and stay where they are.
design:
## Decomposition

- C1 — Pure-Dart fake VmService for exploration_agent tests (foundation; no deps)
- C2 — Convert provider_loop_integration_test.dart to pure-Dart (deps: C1)
- C3 — Convert dogfood_e2e_test.dart to pure-Dart (deps: C1)
- C4 — Convert observation_fixture_e2e_test.dart to pure-Dart (deps: C1)
- C5 — Cut Flutter deps from exploration_agent/pubspec.yaml + acyclic/no-Flutter guard (deps: C2, C3, C4)
- C6 — CI: dart test packages/exploration_agent covers the package (deps: C5)
- C7 — Verify + install the dogfood LaunchAgent, finishing lenny-wisp-lm7 (deps: C5)

## Key decisions

- All three offending tests depend on `BindingVmServiceFake` + `ExplorationBinding`,
  both from `exploration_flutter`. The agent only ever talks to the binding through
  `package:vm_service` RPC, and `package:vm_service` is pure Dart. The replacement
  is a pure-Dart fake VmService with scripted responses for the
  `ext.flutter.exploration.*` surface — built once in C1.

- Fidelity tradeoff, accepted: `BindingVmServiceFake` routes RPC into a REAL
  `ExplorationBinding`. The pure-Dart fake returns scripted responses instead.
  Real-binding coverage does not leave the repo — it stays in exploration_flutter's
  own tests (binding_e2e_integration_test.dart), the correct layer for it.
  exploration_agent tests the agent against a scripted VmService;
  exploration_flutter tests the real binding. One-way layering.

- `exploration_cli` is an unused dev_dependency — no `package:exploration_cli`
  import exists anywhere in the package (only doc-comment mentions). C5 deletes
  the line; no code change needed.

- `exploration_agent/lib/` already imports zero Flutter. The coupling lives only
  in the pubspec and the three test files.

- `observation_fixture_e2e_test.dart`'s subject IS `BindingVmServiceFake`; C4
  re-points it at the pure-Dart fake's fixture-serving behaviour.

- C7 closes the loop on lenny-wisp-lm7: once C3 lands, run-dogfood.sh's existing
  `dart test` invocation is correct as written and the LaunchAgent can install.

title: Agent dogfood harness: --observation-fixture is decorative — fixture is never served as the binding's observation; agent operates blind
description:
## Problem

The agent dogfood harness accepts an `--observation-fixture <path>` flag and loads the fixture file into an `ObservationFixture` value, but **the fixture is never returned to the agent as the binding's observation**. The harness's `fixture` field dartdoc admits it (`packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/dogfood/agent_dogfood_harness.dart:125-130`):

> Currently informational — the binding fake's observation path is owned by the caller's [ExplorationBinding], not by the harness; the fixture is recorded in the trace header so prompt-tuning diffs can be reproduced.

What actually happens when the agent calls `core.get_stable_observation`:
1. Agent → `VmServiceClient.callExtension('ext.flutter.exploration.core.get_stable_observation', ...)`.
2. → `BindingVmServiceFake.callServiceExtension` → routes to `invokeServiceExtension` (since `get_stable_observation` isn't in `pluginRegistry.mergedTools`, the cx6.46-corrected fall-through path).
3. → `ExplorationBinding`'s real `core.get_stable_observation` handler runs, walks the (empty) Flutter widget tree, returns a near-empty observation.
4. The model sees an observation with `node_count: 0`, `route_stack: []` — **regardless of the fixture loaded**.

This makes the harness diagnostically useless for prompt tuning the *observation→tool-selection* path, which is the whole point. The model was picking coords like `(960, 108)`, `(850, 120)`, `(0.94, 0.08)` not because of the fixture's button positions but because it had nothing to anchor on and was guessing. The third-turn schema_exhausted in run 9 is the natural endpoint of trying to make a model decide with no input data.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Author a non-empty observation JSON file (e.g. login screen with a `routeStack: ["login"]` and a `core.nodes` map of 9 entries) and save as `/tmp/login_fixture.json`.
2. Run the dogfood harness: `dart run packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood.dart --observation-fixture /tmp/login_fixture.json --goal "sign in" ...` against any swift-infer endpoint.
3. Inspect the per-turn record in the trace file:

```json
"observation_summary": {
  "keys": ["core", "plugins", "stability"],
  "node_count": 0,
  "route_stack": []
}
```

Expected: `node_count: 9`, `route_stack: ["login"]` reflecting the fixture.
Actual: `node_count: 0`, `route_stack: []` — the binding's empty-tree response, fixture is ignored.

Confirmed in run 9 (`/tmp/lenny-dogfood/run9.jsonl`) post-cx6.47.

## Scope

Wire the fixture into the binding's observation path. Cleanest seam: intercept `core.get_stable_observation` calls in `BindingVmServiceFake` and return the fixture body wrapped in the binding's standard `{type: 'Observation', value: <bundle>}` envelope.

1. **`BindingVmServiceFake.callServiceExtension`** — when `method == 'ext.flutter.exploration.core.get_stable_observation'` AND a fixture is configured, return `{type: 'Observation', value: <fixture body>}` instead of calling `invokeServiceExtension`. The fixture is the source of truth; the real binding's empty-tree response is bypassed for this one method.

2. **Constructor wiring** — `BindingVmServiceFake` gains an optional `ObservationFixture? observationFixture` constructor parameter. When null, the current pass-through behavior is preserved (cvl.4 and cx6.41 callers don't set it). When non-null, observation calls return the fixture.

3. **`AgentDogfoodHarness.run()` / runner** — pass the harness's `fixture` to the `BindingVmServiceFake` constructor. Update the dartdoc on `AgentDogfoodHarness.fixture` to remove the "currently informational" caveat.

4. **Tests**:
   - Unit: `_support/binding_vm_service_fake_test.dart` — given a fixture, `core.get_stable_observation` returns the fixture's `core/plugins/stability` shape; without a fixture, behavior unchanged.
   - Unit: `dogfood/agent_dogfood_harness_test.dart` — a turn run with a fixture surfaces `observation_summary` with non-empty `route_stack` and `node_count > 0`.

5. **Mirror to inline clones** — until lenny-imr lands, the same constructor-param change must be replicated in:
   - `packages/exploration_flutter/test/binding_e2e_integration_test.dart` (cvl.4)
   - `packages/exploration_agent/test/integration/provider_loop_integration_test.dart` (cx6.41)
   
   ...but only the shared copy + harness path needs the fixture-injection wiring. The cvl.4/cx6.41 clones get the optional null-fixture pass-through behavior (constructor signature compat).

## Out of scope

- Dynamic observation updates after tool calls. v1 returns the SAME fixture every turn. The model can't "see" the result of its tap. Different bead — scriptable fixture chain (e.g. `--observation-sequence A.json,B.json,C.json`).
- Real Flutter widget tree mount. Still not happening; the fixture is the proxy.
- Plugin observation contributions (`router`, `riverpod`). Today the fixture's `plugins` block round-trips as-is; if the plugins themselves want to inject their own observation slices, that's a real binding concern.
- Captures of failed-turn model output (turn 2 schema_exhausted was opaque). Separate bead — capture the raw `tool_use{name, arguments}` the model emitted even when validation rejects, into `dogfood_turn`.

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_agent/test/_support/binding_vm_service_fake.dart` — the routing seam.
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/dogfood/agent_dogfood_harness.dart:125-130` — the dartdoc admission.
- `packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood_runner.dart` — the binding fake construction site.
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/dogfood/observation_fixture.dart` — `ObservationFixture.body` map (top-level JSON object).
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/binding/exploration_binding.dart` — the binding's `core.get_stable_observation` extension that emits `{type: 'Observation', value: <bundle>}` (the envelope shape we mirror).
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/session/observation_puller.dart:60-65` — the agent-side unwrap of the `value` envelope.

## Diagnosed via

Run 9 (`/tmp/lenny-dogfood/run9.jsonl`) post-cx6.47. The per-turn records now write but show `node_count: 0` and `route_stack: []` for every turn, despite a 9-node `login` fixture loaded. Inspection of the fixture field's dartdoc and tracing through the binding fake's routing confirmed the fixture is never read after the trace header.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `BindingVmServiceFake` constructor accepts an optional `ObservationFixture? observationFixture` parameter (named, defaults to null); null preserves current pass-through behavior.
- [ ] When constructed with a non-null `observationFixture`, calls to `ext.flutter.exploration.core.get_stable_observation` return `{type: 'Observation', value: <fixture.body>}` without invoking `_binding.invokeServiceExtension` for that method.
- [ ] All other extension calls (handshake, plugin-tool methods routed via `mergedTools`, other binding-owned extensions) continue to route exactly as before whether the fixture is null or non-null.
- [ ] `AgentDogfoodHarness.run()` (via the runner / e2e wiring) constructs `BindingVmServiceFake` with `observationFixture: fixture` so the loaded fixture is the observation the agent sees on every turn.
- [ ] The "Currently informational" dartdoc on `AgentDogfoodHarness.fixture` is removed/rewritten to state that the fixture is served by the binding fake on every `core.get_stable_observation` call.
- [ ] New unit test in `test/_support/binding_vm_service_fake_test.dart`: given a fixture, `core.get_stable_observation` returns the fixture body inside the `{type: 'Observation', value: …}` envelope; without a fixture, the existing fall-through to `invokeServiceExtension` is preserved (existing routing regression test still passes).
- [ ] New unit test in `test/dogfood/agent_dogfood_harness_test.dart` (or extension of an existing one): a harness run wired to a fixture with non-empty `core.routeStack` and `core.nodes` produces a `dogfood_turn` trace line whose `observation_summary` has `node_count > 0` and `route_stack` matching the fixture.
- [ ] Constructor signature mirrored in the inline clones at `packages/exploration_flutter/test/binding_e2e_integration_test.dart` and `packages/exploration_agent/test/integration/provider_loop_integration_test.dart` (optional null-fixture parameter only; no fixture-serving logic in clones). Both files compile and their existing tests still pass.
- [ ] `dart analyze` clean across `packages/exploration_agent` and `packages/exploration_flutter`.
- [ ] Full test suites pass: `dart test` in both packages.

title: BindingVmServiceFake routes core.* tools to invokeServiceExtension (always wrong); should use pluginRegistry.mergedTools to route plugin tools through invokePluginTool regardless of namespace
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All three `BindingVmServiceFake` copies (the shared file at `packages/exploration_agent/test/_support/binding_vm_service_fake.dart` plus the two inline `_BindingVmServiceFake` clones in `packages/exploration_flutter/test/binding_e2e_integration_test.dart` and `packages/exploration_agent/test/integration/provider_loop_integration_test.dart`) route methods by consulting `_binding.pluginRegistry.mergedTools()` rather than by the `core.` URL prefix.
- [ ] Methods whose `<ns>.<tool>` suffix is present in `mergedTools()` are dispatched to `_binding.invokePluginTool`; methods absent from `mergedTools()` (handshake, get_stable_observation, get_recent_errors, screenshot, diagnostics_warnings) fall through to `_binding.invokeServiceExtension`.
- [ ] Methods that do not start with `ext.flutter.exploration.` still throw `RPCError(method, -32601, 'Unknown method "$method"')`.
- [ ] The `corePrefix` constant (`'ext.flutter.exploration.core.'`) is removed from all three copies; only the generic `'ext.flutter.exploration.'` prefix remains.
- [ ] A new regression test in `packages/exploration_agent/test/_support/binding_vm_service_fake_test.dart` registers a plugin under namespace `core` whose `core.tap` tool sets a flag when invoked, drives `BindingVmServiceFake.callServiceExtension('ext.flutter.exploration.core.tap', args: {...})`, and asserts the flag was set (proving the call reached `invokePluginTool`, not `invokeServiceExtension`).
- [ ] The same regression test asserts that `ext.flutter.exploration.core.get_stable_observation` (a binding-owned extension that is NOT in `mergedTools()`) still routes through `invokeServiceExtension` — i.e. the fall-through path is exercised.
- [ ] `flutter test packages/exploration_flutter/test/binding_e2e_integration_test.dart` continues to pass (`_SampleEchoPlugin('sample', ['echo'])` still resolves via the registry path).
- [ ] `flutter test packages/exploration_agent/test/integration/provider_loop_integration_test.dart` continues to pass (`_RouterEchoPlugin('router', ['navigate'])` still resolves via the registry path).
- [ ] `dart analyze packages/exploration_agent packages/exploration_flutter` reports zero new diagnostics.

title: Agent dogfood harness: core.* tools crash the loop (no stand-in for core namespace); harnessError outcomes drop the exception text from the JSONL footer
description:
## Problem

Two related gaps in the agent dogfood harness surfaced by running it post-cx6.44:

### 1. `core.*` tool calls crash the loop

`tool/agent_dogfood_runner.dart` line ~154:

```dart
final Set<String> namespaces =
    toolNames.map((String t) => t.split('.').first).toSet()
      ..remove('core');
final ExplorationBinding binding = ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized(
  plugins: <ExplorationPlugin>[
    for (final String ns in namespaces) _StandInPlugin(ns, toolNames),
  ],
)!;
```

`core` is explicitly removed from the stand-in namespaces because `ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized` auto-loads the real `CorePlugin`. So when the model picks a `core.*` tool (e.g. `core.tap` — which it does naturally because most goals involve interacting with the UI), the real CorePlugin handler runs, finds no widget tree (the harness doesn't mount a real app), and the call fails — surfacing as `HarnessError.connectionLost` to the harness.

This is the most common case for prompt tuning: the user gives a goal like "turn on dark mode," the model picks `core.tap` with screen coordinates, and the harness crashes. The whole point of the harness is to let the model exercise tool selection; today it can only exercise non-core tools, which is useless because every realistic goal involves `core.*`.

### 2. `harnessError` outcomes don't carry the error text in the footer

When `LoopDriver` returns a `SessionTermination` with `harnessError: HarnessError.connectionLost`, the harness logs `outcome=typedException ... harnessError: HarnessError.connectionLost` to stdout (line `[dogfood] end ...`), but the JSONL `dogfood_footer` record shows `"exception": null`. The error TYPE is captured but the error TEXT isn't preserved anywhere durable.

To diagnose post-mortem, you have to either re-read scroll-back stdout (gone after the shell closes) or correlate with swift-infer's trace store. The whole point of the JSONL trace is to be the durable record; missing the actual exception message defeats that.

## Confirmed run (msg_8B2A1F4247E)

Run 5 against live swift-infer with `--max-turn-budget-ms 180000`:

- swift-infer responded in 73s with: `tool_calls=[{name:"core_tap", arguments:{x:"0.94", y:"0.08"}}]` — model selected `core.tap` at top-right coordinates (a plausible "settings gear" tap for a "turn on dark mode" goal).
- Agent decoded `core_tap` → `core.tap`, threaded it through `LoopHost.executeAction`.
- `core.tap` dispatched to real `CorePlugin` → no widget tree → exception → wrapped as `HarnessError.connectionLost`.
- Harness reported `outcome=typedException`, `termination=SessionTermination(SessionOutcome.harnessError, harnessError: HarnessError.connectionLost, finalSummary: null)`.
- JSONL footer: `{"type":"dogfood_footer","outcome":"typedException","ended_at":"..."}` — **`exception` field empty**.

## Scope

1. **Replace the real CorePlugin with a stand-in for harness runs.** Either:
   - Boot the binding without the default CorePlugin (whatever seam supports that), then register `_StandInPlugin('core', ...)` alongside the non-core stand-ins. Both `core.*` and plugin tools become no-op `{ok: true, value: args}`. OR
   - Use `_StandInPlugin('core', ...)` AND override `PluginRegistry` to allow the dup-namespace insertion in test/dogfood mode. OR
   - Boot a minimal sample widget tree so the real CorePlugin actually has something to tap. (Costly; loses the "Flutter-agnostic harness library" property cx6.43 paid for.)

   Architect picks. The cheapest viable approach is preferred — the harness is for prompt tuning, not real interaction.

2. **Persist the error text on `harnessError` outcomes.** When `harness.run()` returns a `DogfoodRunResult` whose `termination.harnessError != null`, the JSONL `dogfood_footer` must include:
   - `exception`: a human-readable string like `"HarnessError.connectionLost"` (or whatever `harnessError.toString()` produces).
   - Optionally `harness_error`: the enum value name (`connectionLost`, etc.) as a separate field for filterability.

   This is symmetric to the `recoveryError` field added in cx6.44 — same shape, same place.

## Out of scope

- Per-turn verbose logging (cx6.43 specced it; cx6.43's bitsmith only implemented start/end). File as a separate sibling bead — it's diagnosability scope, not completability.
- Real Flutter widget tree integration for actual visual tap behavior. Different shape, different bead.
- Tool input schema quality (empty `{type: object}` schemas mean the model has no clue what args each tool takes). Already mentioned as out-of-scope in cx6.44; still a separate bead.

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood_runner.dart` line ~149-163 (the `..remove('core')` + `ensureInitialized` block).
- `packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood_runner.dart` `_StandInPlugin` / `_NoopTool` classes (lines 65-103) — the pattern to extend to core.
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/registry.dart` — `PluginRegistry`; whatever guards against duplicate-namespace registration.
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/binding/exploration_binding.dart` — `ensureInitialized` and the auto-CorePlugin wiring.
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/dogfood/agent_dogfood_harness.dart` line ~215-245 — the run() outcome-emitting block. Footer construction in `DogfoodTraceWriter.writeFooter` at `lib/src/dogfood/trace_writer.dart`.

## Diagnosed via

Two harness runs post-cx6.44 (`/tmp/lenny-dogfood/run4.jsonl`, `run5.jsonl`) and one swift-infer trace lookup (`msg_8B2A1F4247E`). The harness now correctly reports outcomes without masking, but cannot reach a `tool_executed` outcome on any realistic goal because of (1), and even when it does fail informatively, (2) hides the actual error in the durable trace.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Build swift-infer and a Qwen-class model that emits tool calls.
2. `dart run packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood.dart --goal "turn on dark mode" --tools core.tap,core.scroll --endpoint http://127.0.0.1:8080 --model qwen3.6-27b --max-turn-budget-ms 180000 --trace-out /tmp/run.jsonl` (post-cx6.44 with the writer-header fix in place).
3. Observe the JSONL trace at `/tmp/run.jsonl`:
   - The footer line shows `"outcome":"typedException"` but `"exception"` is absent.
   - No `tool_executed` outcome is reachable on any goal that invokes `core.*` because the real `CorePlugin` dispatches without a widget tree and surfaces `HarnessError.connectionLost`.

**Expected:** `core.*` tool calls are dispatched to a no-op stand-in (just like non-core namespaces), and any `harnessError`-shaped termination has its error text persisted on the footer in `exception` (and the enum value in a new `harness_error` field).

**Actual:** `core.*` crashes the loop with `HarnessError.connectionLost`; the footer's `exception` field is `null`.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC1 `ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized` gains a `@visibleForTesting` named param `installCorePlugin` (default `true`); when `false`, no `CorePlugin` is constructed or registered.
- [ ] AC2 With `installCorePlugin: false`, a caller-supplied plugin whose namespace is `core` registers successfully (no `StateError: duplicate plugin namespace: core`).
- [ ] AC3 `tool/agent_dogfood_runner.dart` registers a `_StandInPlugin('core', toolNames)` alongside non-core stand-ins by calling `ensureInitialized(plugins: …, installCorePlugin: false)`; the line `..remove('core')` is gone.
- [ ] AC4 `_StandInPlugin('core', toolNames).tools` exposes one `_NoopTool` per `core.<tool>` entry in `toolNames`, each returning `ToolResult(ok: true, value: args)` on call.
- [ ] AC5 `DogfoodTraceWriter.writeFooter` gains optional named param `String? harnessError`; when supplied, the emitted `dogfood_footer` JSON line carries `"harness_error": "<value>"`.
- [ ] AC6 `AgentDogfoodHarness.run()` populates `capturedException` with a non-null `Object` whose `toString()` includes the `HarnessError` value name whenever `SessionTermination.harnessError != null` (covers both `connectionLost` and `agentStuck`), so the emitted footer's `exception` field is non-null and the run's `DogfoodRunResult.exception` field carries the same string.
- [ ] AC7 When `SessionTermination.harnessError != null`, the emitted `dogfood_footer` additionally carries `"harness_error": "<wireName>"` (e.g. `connection_lost`, `agent_stuck`).
- [ ] AC8 Existing dogfood harness/trace tests still pass without modification.
- [ ] AC9 New unit test exercises an in-process binding initialized with `installCorePlugin: false` plus a user `core` plugin and asserts the merged tool map contains the user plugin's tool and no real `core.tap`.
- [ ] AC10 New unit test exercises `AgentDogfoodHarness.run()` with a `VmService` fake that drives the loop to a `harnessError`-shaped `SessionTermination`, asserts `r.exception.toString()` contains the `HarnessError` value name, and asserts the JSONL footer carries `"exception"` and `"harness_error"` fields with matching values.

title: Agent dogfood harness: diagnosability — call writeHeader on LoopDriver's TrajectoryWriter, set captureBodies=true, preserve original exception in failure footer
description:
## Problem

The agent dogfood harness (lenny-cx6.43) is **diagnostically blind in exactly the cases the user built it for**. Two distinct flaws conspire to mask the original failure:

### 1. LoopDriver's `TrajectoryWriter` never gets `writeHeader()`

The harness constructs an `AgentDogfoodHarness` that wires a `TrajectorySink` and writes a `dogfood_header` via `DogfoodTraceWriter`. But the `LoopDriver` instantiated inside the harness has its own `TrajectoryWriter` (writing the agent's session-trajectory records: header/turn/event). The harness never calls `writeHeader()` on that LoopDriver-owned writer.

Result: any turn failure inside `LoopDriver.runTurn` enters one of three failure branches (`TurnTimeoutError`, `InvalidActionExhausted`, `SchemaExhausted`), each of which calls `_writeFailedTurn` → `_writer.writeTurn(rec)` → `_ensureHeader()` → `StateError: writeHeader must precede turns/events`. The **original** exception (the one that actually caused the failure) is swallowed; the StateError is what the harness reports. Confirmed three times in a row tonight against live swift-infer.

The exception that bubbles up to the harness footer is always the same: `Bad state: writeHeader must precede turns/events`. Useless for diagnosis. The real failure (whichever of the three classes triggered _writeFailedTurn) is invisible.

### 2. Harness's `SwiftInferConfig` does not set `captureBodies: true`

`tool/agent_dogfood_runner.dart:174-178` constructs `SwiftInferConfig` with `baseUrl + model + bearerToken` only. `captureBodies` defaults to `false`. So when the harness fires a request at swift-infer, the server's admin API returns a trace row with metrics but **no captured request/response bodies** — visible in `GET /v1/trace/<id>` as `tool_calls: null, content: None, thinking_content: None`. The `debug-inference` skill copied into lenny in this very session cannot diagnose harness runs.

This is self-inflicted blindness in the one place we most need ground-truth. The panel's `SwiftInferUiConfig` defaults `captureBodies: true` for exactly this reason; the harness inherited the agent-side `SwiftInferConfig` default (false) instead.

## Confirmed runs (3 tonight)

| run | --max-turn-budget-ms | total wall | swift-infer request_id | swift-infer dur | swift-infer body capture | harness exception |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 30000 | 30s | (not captured) | — | none | Bad state: writeHeader must precede turns/events |
| 2 | 120000 | 47s | msg_4B8F1134FD8 | 45s | none | Bad state: writeHeader must precede turns/events |
| 3 | 60000 | 54s | msg_0320A6615F0 | 52s | none | Bad state: writeHeader must precede turns/events |

Three runs, three identical useless errors. The harness ships diagnostically inert until both issues are fixed.

## Scope

1. **In `AgentDogfoodHarness.run()`** (or wherever the harness constructs the `LoopDriver`'s `TrajectoryWriter`): call `writeHeader()` on the loop driver's writer with the harness's run metadata before invoking `LoopDriver.runSession`. If the harness shares one `TrajectorySink` between the `DogfoodTraceWriter` and the `LoopDriver`'s `TrajectoryWriter`, the header records must coexist (one `dogfood_header` + one session-trajectory header) without each writer's `_headerWritten` flag overriding the other's invariant. Architect decides: two writers writing to the same sink, or two separate sinks for the two layers.
2. **In the harness's `SwiftInferConfig` construction** (`tool/agent_dogfood_runner.dart:174-178` and the matching path inside `lib/src/dogfood/agent_dogfood_harness.dart` if any): set `captureBodies: true` by default. Optionally expose a `--no-capture-bodies` CLI flag for opt-out.
3. **Failure-recovery cleanup**: when `_writeFailedTurn` itself throws, the harness's catch should still emit the **original** exception in the footer (with the secondary error appended as a note), not just the secondary. Today the harness emits `outcome=typedException exception=<StateError>`; it should emit `outcome=typedException exception=<original> recovery_error=<StateError>`.

## Out of scope

- Empty tool input schemas (the harness passes `{'type': 'object'}` for every tool; model has no clue what args to provide). Sibling bead — separate concern about input quality.
- Per-turn budget vs. total-run budget separation. The current single timeout is fine; refining the budget hierarchy is a separate concern.
- Anthropic / OpenAI provider equivalents.

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/dogfood/agent_dogfood_harness.dart` (harness construction + run loop)
- `packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood_runner.dart` (CLI shim — line 174 SwiftInferConfig construction)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/loop_driver/loop_driver.dart` (lines 130-160 runTurn failure branches; line 321 _writeFailedTurn)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/trajectory/writer.dart` (writeHeader/writeTurn/_ensureHeader invariant)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/dogfood/trace_writer.dart` (DogfoodTraceWriter — has its own header/footer)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_config.dart` (captureBodies default)

## Diagnosed via

Three runs of `dart run packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood.dart` against live swift-infer at localhost:8080. Traces at `/tmp/lenny-dogfood/run{1,2,3}.jsonl`. swift-infer admin API confirmed both real requests fired but bodies were not captured. This is the harness eating its own dogfood and reporting back that its diagnostic layer is broken.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Boot swift-infer locally on `localhost:8080` with a Qwen3.6-class model and a valid `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN`.
2. Run the dogfood harness against it:
   ```
   dart run packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood.dart \
     --goal 'tap any button' \
     --tools core.tap,core.done \
     --endpoint http://localhost:8080 \
     --token $SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN \
     --model qwen3.6-27b \
     --max-turns 3 \
     --max-turn-budget-ms 30000
   ```
3. Observe: the harness consistently exits 1 with `outcome=typedException exception=Bad state: writeHeader must precede turns/events`, regardless of what the model actually did. The real failure (timeout / invalid action / schema rejection) is masked.
4. Inspect swift-infer admin: `curl http://localhost:8080/v1/trace/<msg-id>` returns metrics but `content`, `thinking_content`, and `tool_calls` are all `null` because the harness did not request body capture.

## Expected

Failure path should surface the **actual** turn-level failure mode (turn_timeout / invalid_action_exhausted / schema_exhausted) and swift-infer's admin trace should contain the captured request/response bodies for downstream debug-inference analysis.

## Actual

Three consecutive runs all surface the same `StateError: writeHeader must precede turns/events`. Body capture is empty in swift-infer's admin API.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `AgentDogfoodHarness.run()` calls `writeHeader()` on the LoopDriver's `TrajectoryWriter` before `driver.runSession()`, with a `SessionHeader` whose `goal`, `modelIdentifier`, `harnessVersion`, and `config` reflect harness state (other fields default to empty/`''`).
- [ ] When a turn inside `LoopDriver.runTurn` fails (e.g. `TurnTimeoutError`), the harness no longer reports `StateError: writeHeader must precede turns/events`; verified by a unit test that wires a `TurnFailingLoopHost` (always throws `TurnTimeoutError` on `observe()`) and asserts the final outcome is `typedException` or `budgetExceeded` with the original failure mode reflected, not a `StateError`.
- [ ] `tool/agent_dogfood_runner.dart` constructs `SwiftInferConfig` with `captureBodies: true` by default; verified by reading the runner file's `SwiftInferConfig(...)` call and confirming `captureBodies: true` is passed.
- [ ] `tool/agent_dogfood.dart` exposes a `--no-capture-bodies` flag (default off, i.e. capture stays on) that, when passed, threads `capture_bodies: false` through `DOGFOOD_ARGS_JSON` to the runner; runner respects it.
- [ ] When the harness's recovery path (`writeFooter`) is itself triggered by a secondary exception from inside the `try` (e.g. simulated by injecting a writer that throws on `writeTurn`), the `dogfood_footer` JSON's `exception` field carries the **original** exception message, and a new `recovery_error` field carries the secondary error message.
- [ ] The existing test `budgetExceeded on hanging VmService within total budget` continues to pass with the new code path (no regression on the session.start() timeout case).
- [ ] All existing unit tests under `packages/exploration_agent/test/dogfood/` and `packages/exploration_agent/test/loop_driver/` pass: `dart test packages/exploration_agent/test/dogfood/ packages/exploration_agent/test/loop_driver/` → 0 failures.

title: agent ↔ binding handshake: rename to core.handshake, add plugins manifest, fix -32601 method-not-found code
description:
Child of lenny-cvl. The smallest slice that unblocks the DevTools Prompt tab.

## Problem

`VmServiceClient.handshake()` calls `ext.flutter.exploration.handshake` but `ExplorationBinding` registers `ext.flutter.exploration.core.handshake` → JSON-RPC -32601 ("Unknown method"), surfaced in the panel as "Binding not detected". Even past the name, `handshake()` would `StateError` because the binding's payload has no `contractVersion` or `plugins` array (it returns `protocolVersion`/`bindingType`/`flutterMode`/`pluginCount`), and the absent-extension translation keys off the wrong code (`_kMethodNotFound = 110`, not the real `-32601`).

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Build/run the dogfood DevTools target against `sample_app` (which calls `ExplorationBinding.run(SampleApp())` with the router/riverpod/dio plugins).
2. Open the DevTools "Prompt" tab.
3. Observe: "Binding not detected: ext.flutter.exploration.handshake: (-32601) Unknown method 'ext.flutter.exploration.handshake'." instead of the live plugin manifest.

(Equivalently, in pure Dart: a `VmServiceClient` pointed at a real `ExplorationBinding` throws `RPCError(-32601)` from `handshake()`; even if the name matched, `handshake()` would throw `StateError` on the missing `contractVersion`.)

## Scope

1. Point `VmServiceClient.handshake()` at `ext.flutter.exploration.core.handshake`.
2. Extend the binding's `core.handshake` payload additively with `plugins: [{namespace, tools:[<bare tool names>]}, ...]` built from the registered plugins (incl. the `core` namespace).
3. Make `handshake()` parse `protocolVersion` as the contract version, tolerate the extra fields, and read the new `plugins` array; translate the absent extension off `-32601`.

Out of scope (sibling beads): observation rename/encoding, action-routing, the end-to-end integration test.

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/vm_service_client.dart` (lines ~17-26 constants, ~90-124 handshake, ~150-160 _safeCall)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/binding/exploration_binding.dart` (`_registerCoreExtensions` ~346-357 handshake registration)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/registry.dart` (`PluginRegistry`)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin.dart` (`ExplorationPlugin.namespace`, `ExplorationTool.name`)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/types.dart` (`HandshakeResult`, `PluginManifestEntry`)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/errors.dart` (`BindingNotInitializedError`)
- `packages/exploration_agent/test/vm_service_client_test.dart` (existing handshake tests assert the OLD names — must be updated)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/test/binding_handshake_test.dart`
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `VmServiceClient.handshake()` invokes `ext.flutter.exploration.core.handshake` (verified: a fake `VmService` records `lastMethod == 'ext.flutter.exploration.core.handshake'`)
- [ ] `VmServiceClient` translates an absent handshake extension off the real JSON-RPC method-not-found code `-32601` (an `RPCError` with code `-32601` from the handshake call throws `BindingNotInitializedError`; the old `110` constant is gone)
- [ ] An `RPCError` with code `-32601` from a *non*-handshake call still rethrows unchanged (not translated)
- [ ] `VmServiceClient.handshake()` treats `protocolVersion` as the contract version, tolerates the binding's extra fields (`bindingType`, `flutterMode`, `pluginCount`), and parses a `plugins: [{namespace, tools:[...]}]` array; a response carrying `protocolVersion` instead of `contractVersion` no longer throws `StateError`
- [ ] `ExplorationBinding`'s `ext.flutter.exploration.core.handshake` response includes `plugins: [{namespace, tools:[<bare tool names>]}, ...]` built from the registered plugins (the `core` namespace plus every user plugin, in registration order), alongside its existing `protocolVersion`/`bindingType`/`flutterMode`/`pluginCount` fields
- [ ] `PluginManifestEntry.tools` is documented as carrying bare tool tokens (not namespaced)
- [ ] `dart test packages/exploration_agent` and `flutter test packages/exploration_flutter` pass; `dart analyze packages/exploration_agent packages/exploration_flutter` is clean
- [ ] Manual: in the dogfood DevTools target against `sample_app`, the Prompt tab shows the live plugin manifest (`core`, `router`, `riverpod`, `dio`) instead of "Binding not detected"

title: agent ↔ ExplorationBinding wire contract is incompatible — handshake/observation/action method names & response shapes diverge (panel 'Binding not detected'; agent loop can't drive the app)
description:
## Symptom

The DevTools Prompt tab now connects (the `Platform._version` web crash from
lenny-dzh is gone) but shows **"Binding not detected: ext.flutter.exploration.handshake:
(-32601) Unknown method 'ext.flutter.exploration.handshake'."** against `sample_app`,
which *does* call `ExplorationBinding.run(SampleApp())` with plugins.
Screenshot: `~/Screenshots/Screenshot 2026-05-12 at 10.58.10 PM.png`.

## Root cause

`packages/exploration_agent`'s `VmServiceClient` (the agent/panel side) and
`packages/exploration_flutter`'s `ExplorationBinding` (the app side) were built
against **different, incompatible wire contracts** and were never integration-tested
end to end. Divergences found:

| What | `VmServiceClient` calls (`vm_service_client.dart`) | `ExplorationBinding` registers (`exploration_binding.dart:_registerCoreExtensions`) |
|---|---|---|
| Handshake method | `ext.flutter.exploration.handshake` | `ext.flutter.exploration.**core**.handshake` — missing the `.core.` segment → -32601 |
| Handshake response | client expects `{contractVersion: String, plugins: [{namespace, tools:[...]}]}` (`handshake()` throws `StateError` if `contractVersion` absent) | binding returns `{protocolVersion:"1", bindingType, flutterMode, **pluginCount**:int}` — no `plugins` array, no `contractVersion` |
| Stable observation | `ext.flutter.exploration.core.**getStableObservation**` (and `ObservationPuller` uses a `get_stable_observation` constant — two paths) | `ext.flutter.exploration.core.**get_stable_observation**` (snake_case), debug-only, decodes request from flat string params |
| Action / tool exec | single dispatcher `ext.flutter.exploration.core.**executeAction**` with `{name, args}` | **no such endpoint** — tools are individual extensions: `ext.flutter.exploration.core.<tool>` (core_plugin) and `ext.flutter.exploration.<ns>.<tool>` (plugin extensions via `PluginContext.registerExtension`) |

So even past the handshake name, `handshake()` would `StateError` on the missing
`contractVersion`/`plugins`, observation would 404 on the camelCase name, and the
agent loop's `executeAction` calls would 404 entirely. The panel's prompt tab can't
load the plugin manifest, and the Start button (`session.run` → observation + action)
can't drive the app.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Build/run the dogfood DevTools target against `sample_app`.
2. Open the DevTools "Prompt" tab and press Start.
3. Observe: the Prompt tab shows "Binding not detected: ext.flutter.exploration.handshake: (-32601) Unknown method ..." (handshake name mismatch), and — past that — the agent loop's observation and `executeAction` calls would 404 against the binding's actual extension surface.

## Approach (recommendation — confirm/decompose in spec)

Treat the **binding's** convention as canonical (it's the more developed side: `.core.`
prefix, snake_case suffixes, per-namespace/per-tool extensions, the screenshot /
semantics / errors / diagnostics extensions, the `ExplorationPlugin` contract with
`namespace` + `List<ExplorationTool>`). Bring the agent client in line, and extend the
binding *additively* where the agent needs more than it currently emits:

1. **Handshake (unblocks the panel — do first):**
   - `VmServiceClient.handshake()` → call `ext.flutter.exploration.core.handshake`.
   - Extend the binding's `core.handshake` payload to include
     `plugins: [{namespace: <ns>, tools: [<tool.name>, ...]}, ...]` built from
     `_plugins` (each `ExplorationPlugin.namespace` + `tools.map((t) => t.name)`),
     keeping the existing `protocolVersion`/`bindingType`/`flutterMode`/`pluginCount`
     fields (additive — non-breaking per PRD §7.7).
   - Make `handshake()` parse `protocolVersion` (treat as the contract version),
     tolerate the extra fields, and accept `plugins` in that shape. Map the
     `BindingNotInitializedError` translation to the *real* JSON-RPC method-not-found
     code (`-32601`), not the current `110` (`_kMethodNotFound` is wrong).
2. **Observation:** rename the client call to `ext.flutter.exploration.core.get_stable_observation`,
   send the request via the param encoding the binding's `_decodeObservationRequest`
   expects, and collapse the two observation paths (`VmServiceClient.getStableObservation`
   vs `ObservationPuller`) into one.
3. **Action / tool execution:** replace `VmServiceClient.executeAction(name, args)` with a
   call to `ext.flutter.exploration.<namespace>.<tool>` (or `.core.<tool>`), splitting the
   fully-qualified `namespace.tool` name. `LoopHost.executeAction` already receives the
   qualified name, so the routing lives in `VmServiceClient`/`DefaultLoopHost`.
4. Add an **integration test** that boots `ExplorationBinding` (or a minimal binding fake
   that registers the *real* extension names/shapes) and drives handshake → observation →
   one tool call through `VmServiceClient` — the gap that let this ship.

Likely wants **decomposition**: step 1 (handshake) is the e2e-demo unblocker and is
small; steps 2–3 are a deeper reconciliation of the agent loop; the integration test
spans both. The architect should split accordingly.

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/vm_service_client.dart` (lines 17-26, 76-105, 137-155)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/session.dart` (`getStableObservation` ~132, `executeAction` ~203)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/session/observation_puller.dart`
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/loop_driver/default_loop_host.dart` (`executeAction` ~118)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/binding/exploration_binding.dart` (`_registerCoreExtensions` ~346-435, `kExplorationExtensionPrefix` ~27)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin.dart` (`ExplorationPlugin.namespace` / `ExplorationTool.name`)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin_context.dart` (`registerExtension` → `ext.flutter.exploration.<ns>.<suffix>`)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/core_tools/core_plugin.dart` (~108: per-tool extension registration)
- Discovered while verifying the lenny-dzh fix in the dogfood DevTools target.
design:
## Implementation Plan

This bug spans four packages and three logically separable wire-contract reconciliations,
so per the approved discovery recommendation it is decomposed into child beads. The parent
epic tracks the umbrella; builders work the children (each child carries its own concrete
steps, touches, and validation plan).

| Child | Scope | Depends on |
|---|---|---|
| lenny-cvl.1 | Handshake: `VmServiceClient.handshake()` → `ext.flutter.exploration.core.handshake`; binding payload gains `plugins:[{namespace,tools}]` additively; absent-extension translation keys off `-32601`. **The panel unblocker — do first.** Specified and sent to committee. | — |
| lenny-cvl.2 | Observation: agent calls `ext.flutter.exploration.core.get_stable_observation`; align the `policy` wire token (`StabilityPolicy.wireName` vs `kStabilityPolicyWireNames`); collapse `VmServiceClient.getStableObservation` and `ObservationPuller` into one path. | lenny-cvl.1 |
| lenny-cvl.3 | Action exec: replace `VmServiceClient.executeAction(name,args)` with a call to `ext.flutter.exploration.<ns>.<tool>` (split the qualified name); no `executeAction` endpoint; namespace tool tokens in `buildPluginTools`. | lenny-cvl.1 |
| lenny-cvl.4 | End-to-end integration test: boot a real-extension binding (or fake) and drive handshake → observation → one tool call through `VmServiceClient`/`ExplorationSession`. | lenny-cvl.1, lenny-cvl.2, lenny-cvl.3 |

Sequencing: lenny-cvl.1 first (and standalone); lenny-cvl.2 and lenny-cvl.3 can proceed in
parallel after .1 lands; lenny-cvl.4 last.

## Touches

Aggregate of the children's touches (see each child's `## Touches` for specifics):

**Files:**
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/vm_service_client.dart` — modified (lenny-cvl.1/.2/.3)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/session.dart`, `.../session/observation_puller.dart`, `.../loop_driver/default_loop_host.dart` — modified (lenny-cvl.2/.3)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/types.dart`, `.../loop_driver/plugin_tools.dart` — modified (lenny-cvl.1/.3)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/binding/exploration_binding.dart`, `.../contract/registry.dart`, `.../observation/observation_request.dart` — modified (lenny-cvl.1/.2)
- test files across `exploration_agent` / `exploration_flutter` — modified/added (all children); new integration test (lenny-cvl.4)

**Symbols added/exposed:**
- `PluginRegistry.manifest` (lenny-cvl.1) — see lenny-cvl.1 `## Touches`.

## Validation Plan

- [ ] All four children reach `closed`.
- [ ] After lenny-cvl.1: in the dogfood DevTools target against `sample_app`, the Prompt tab shows the live plugin manifest (`core` / `router` / `riverpod` / `dio`) instead of "Binding not detected".
- [ ] After lenny-cvl.4: `dart test packages/exploration_agent`, `flutter test packages/exploration_flutter`, `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools`, `dart test packages/exploration_cli` green; `dart analyze` clean across touched packages.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Bead decomposed into children lenny-cvl.1 (handshake), lenny-cvl.2 (observation), lenny-cvl.3 (action exec), lenny-cvl.4 (integration test), wired with deps (1 ← 2, 1 ← 3, {1,2,3} ← 4)
- [ ] lenny-cvl.1 closed: handshake method name, payload, and method-not-found code reconciled; Prompt tab shows the live manifest against `sample_app`
- [ ] lenny-cvl.2 closed: observation method name + policy wire token reconciled; the two observation paths collapsed to one
- [ ] lenny-cvl.3 closed: tool/action calls route to `ext.flutter.exploration.<ns>.<tool>`; no call to a non-existent `executeAction` endpoint
- [ ] lenny-cvl.4 closed: integration test boots a real-extension binding/fake and exercises handshake → observation → one tool call through `VmServiceClient`
- [ ] `dart test packages/exploration_agent`, `flutter test packages/exploration_flutter`, `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools`, `dart test packages/exploration_cli` green; `dart analyze` clean across touched packages
notes: Real blocker for the e2e dogfood demo (PRD §23 success-criteria run). lenny-dzh fixed the web transport; this fixes what's spoken over it. May produce child beads via a 'decompose' verdict.
close_reason: All four children landed (cvl.1 handshake, cvl.2 observation, cvl.3 action exec, cvl.4 e2e integration test). Wire contract reconciled and regression-guarded by the new flutter_test.

comments.author: architect
comments.text: architect: decomposed into lenny-cvl.1 (handshake/panel unblocker), lenny-cvl.2 (observation), lenny-cvl.3 (action exec), lenny-cvl.4 (integration test); deps wired (1←2, 1←3, {1,2,3}←4). lenny-cvl.1 specified and sent to committee. Parent left in in_spec as the umbrella tracker — not itself convened. Approved-design directive (description §Approach) called for the split; AC '(if decomposed)' clause covered.

title: DevTools Prompt tab dead on web: manifest probe / session connect pull in dart:io (vm_service_io) → 'Unsupported operation: Platform._version'
description:
## Symptom

In the real DevTools / Chrome environment (dogfood target: `sample_app` + the
`package:exploration` extension), the **Prompt** tab renders the binding-missing
banner with the text **"Binding not detected: Unsupported operation:
Platform._version"**. Screenshot: `~/Screenshots/Screenshot 2026-05-12 at 9.14.01 PM.png`.
The Thinking/Timeline tabs are unaffected (they don't probe the VM service), but
the Prompt tab — and therefore Start-the-agent — is dead on web.

## Root cause

`packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/vm_service_client.dart` imports
`package:vm_service/vm_service_io.dart` and `VmServiceClient.connect(Uri)` calls
`vmServiceConnectUri(...)` from it. `vm_service_io.dart` is the **IO** variant —
it transitively pulls in `dart:io`. The DevTools extension is a Flutter **web**
build, where `dart:io`'s `Platform.version` (and `WebSocket` / `HttpClient`)
throw `Unsupported operation`. Chain:

`ExplorationPanelHostState.refreshManifest` → `widget.manifestProbe` =
`defaultManifestProbe` (`manifest_probe.dart`) → `VmServiceClient.connect(uri)` →
`vmServiceConnectUri` → `dart:io` `Platform._version` throws → caught by
`refreshManifest`'s `catch (e)` → `ManifestProbeFailed("Unsupported operation:
Platform._version")` → `_PromptTabBody` shows the "Binding not detected" banner.

The same failure hits `ExplorationSession.connect` (`session.dart:52-53`, also via
`VmServiceClient.connect`) — so `ExplorationPanelHostState.ensureSession` /
the Start button would crash the same way even if the probe were stubbed.

This is **known debt**: `vm_service_client.dart:49-53` literally says *"story .21
will swap this entrypoint for a conditional import / DevTools-supplied
VmService"* — but `lenny-cx6.21` (closed) never did it, and `lenny-8rw` wired
`defaultManifestProbe` on top of the still-broken `connect()`. The test suites
never caught it: `panel_host` tests inject a stub `ManifestProbe`, and
`VmServiceClient`/`ExplorationSession` tests use `forTest(...)` with a fake
`VmService` — nothing exercises the real web connect path.

## Approach

The DevTools extension already holds a live, **web-safe** `VmService` —
`devtools_extensions`' `serviceManager.service` (it talks to the target over a
`package:web` JS WebSocket, not `dart:io`). The panel should reuse that
connection, not open its own. (Opening a second connection from the extension
was always wrong; this just makes the breakage visible.)

1. **`exploration_agent`** — add public, web-compatible factories that wrap an
   already-connected `VmService`:
   - `VmServiceClient.fromVmService(VmService vm, String isolateId)` (promote the
     `@visibleForTesting forTest` to a real API, or add alongside it).
   - `ExplorationSession.fromVmService(VmService vm, String isolateId)` mirroring it.
   Both stay `dart:io`-free — `package:vm_service/vm_service.dart` (the core lib)
   is web-compatible; only `vm_service_io.dart` is not.
2. **`exploration_devtools`** — in the DevTools wiring (`exploration_shell.dart` /
   `main.dart`, which already touch `serviceManager`), pass a `manifestProbe`
   that builds `VmServiceClient.fromVmService(serviceManager.service!, mainIsolateId)`
   where `mainIsolateId = serviceManager.isolateManager.mainIsolate.value?.id`.
   Guard nulls (`service` / `mainIsolate` not yet available) → `ManifestProbeBindingMissing`
   (or a `ManifestProbeLoading` retry once `connectedState` flips), not `ManifestProbeFailed`.
   Make `ExplorationPanelHostState.ensureSession`'s session creation injectable the
   same way (a `sessionFactory` callback, defaulting to `ExplorationSession.connect`
   for non-DevTools callers) so the panel calls `ExplorationSession.fromVmService`.
   Keep `panel_host.dart` itself free of `serviceManager` (it must still compile in
   pure-VM widget tests) — the `serviceManager` reads stay in `exploration_shell.dart`.
3. **Keep `VmServiceClient.connect(Uri)`** (the `vm_service_io.dart` path) for the
   **CLI** (`exploration_cli` runs on the Dart VM — `dart:io` is fine there).
   Optionally (robust fix, decide in spec): move `connect()` + the `vm_service_io.dart`
   import into a CLI-only seam (e.g. `vm_service_client_io.dart`) so `exploration_agent`'s
   web-safe core no longer imports it at all, and extend `tool/check_no_dart_io.dart`
   (or add a check) to flag `vm_service_io.dart` imports in web code — the current
   guard only catches *direct* `dart:io` imports, so it never tripped on this.

## Notes / unknowns for the spec

- Confirm the `serviceManager` API surface in the pinned `devtools_extensions` /
  `devtools_app_shared` version: `serviceManager.service` (the `VmService?`),
  `serviceManager.isolateManager.mainIsolate` (a `ValueListenable<IsolateRef?>`),
  and whether there's a ready-to-await "connected" future. Wire the probe to
  re-run on `connectedState` / `mainIsolate` changes (the shell already listens to
  `serviceManager.connectedState`).
- In the simulated standalone-web env (`lenny-93o`'s `exploration_devtools
  (standalone web)` config), `serviceManager.service` is the fake VM service; the
  handshake extension won't exist, so the Prompt tab should show "Binding not
  detected" there — which is correct, and notably *not* a `dart:io` crash anymore.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Build/serve the `package:exploration` DevTools extension (the `exploration_devtools` package) as a Flutter **web** build.
2. Run `sample_app` (`packages/exploration_flutter/example/sample_app`) with `ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized(...)` in `main()`, attach DevTools in Chrome, open the **Exploration** extension's **Prompt** tab.
3. Observe: the tab renders the binding-missing banner reading **"Binding not detected: Unsupported operation: Platform._version"** instead of the live plugin manifest. (Thinking/Timeline tabs are unaffected.) Pressing **Start** would crash the same way via `ExplorationSession.connect(Uri)`.
design:
## Design summary (forks resolved)

- **vm_service_io.dart → CLI-only seam?** *Resolved: NO, not in this bead.* Moving `connect()`/`vm_service_io.dart` out of `vm_service_client.dart` changes `exploration_cli` import sites and `ExplorationSession.connect`'s home — a separate refactor with no AC dependency. The web *crash* is fixed by making the DevTools code paths stop *calling* `connect`. File a follow-up bead and link it as a dependent (step 9). `tool/check_no_dart_io.dart` is unchanged here.
- **Injectable seam for panel session creation.** *Resolved:* the panel layer stops plumbing a `Uri`. `ExplorationShell` and `ExplorationPanelHost` take a **required** `ManifestProbe` (`Future<List<PluginManifestEntry>> Function()`, parameterless) and a **required** `SessionFactory` (`Future<ExplorationSession> Function()`, parameterless). `main.dart` builds both as closures over `serviceManager`. `VmServiceUriResolver` / `vmServiceUri` is deleted from the panel layer (it lived only there).
- **serviceManager API surface (devtools_extensions ^0.4.0 / devtools_app_shared 0.4.0).** Confirmed: `serviceManager.service` is `VmService?`; `serviceManager.isolateManager.mainIsolate` is `ValueListenable<IsolateRef?>` (`devtools_app_shared/.../isolate_manager.dart:52`), `IsolateRef.id` is `String?`; `serviceManager.connectedState` is `ValueListenable<ConnectedState>`. No connected-future is needed — `main.dart` re-triggers the probe via `Listenable.merge([serviceManager.connectedState, serviceManager.isolateManager.mainIsolate])`.

## Implementation Plan

1. **`exploration_agent` — public `VmServiceClient.fromVmService`** — `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/vm_service_client.dart`. Promote the `@visibleForTesting` `forTest` factory to a real public `factory VmServiceClient.fromVmService(VmService vm, String isolateId) => VmServiceClient._(vm, isolateId);`. Keep `forTest` as a thin (still `@visibleForTesting`) alias delegating to it so existing tests compile unchanged. Update the class doc to mention DevTools-supplied `VmService`.
   Test: `dart test packages/exploration_agent --plain-name "VmServiceClient"` → PASS
   Commit: `feat(exploration_agent): public VmServiceClient.fromVmService`

2. **`exploration_agent` — public `ExplorationSession.fromVmService`** — `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/session.dart`. Add `factory ExplorationSession.fromVmService(VmService vm, String isolateId) => ExplorationSession._(VmServiceClient.fromVmService(vm, isolateId));` next to `connect`/`forTest`. Add `import 'package:vm_service/vm_service.dart' show VmService;` (currently only imports `vm_service_client.dart`). Both factories pull in no `dart:io` (only `package:vm_service/vm_service.dart`).
   Test: `dart test packages/exploration_agent --plain-name "ExplorationSession"` → PASS
   Commit: `feat(exploration_agent): public ExplorationSession.fromVmService`

3. **`exploration_agent` — verify guard + add the agent-side regression test** — exports unchanged (`exploration_agent.dart` already exports `VmServiceClient` (line 91) and `ExplorationSession` (line 53); factories ride along). Run `dart run packages/exploration_agent/tool/check_no_dart_io.dart` → `OK: no dart:io in exploration_agent/lib`. Add `packages/exploration_agent/test/vm_service_client_fromvmservice_test.dart`: reuse the `_FakeVmService` pattern from `vm_service_client_test.dart`, build `VmServiceClient.fromVmService(fake, 'iso-1')`, assert `(await client.handshake()).plugins` is empty, then `ExplorationSession.fromVmService(fake, 'iso-1').start('goal', ExplorationConfig.defaults())` succeeds — proving the web path works without `vm_service_io.dart`.
   Test: `dart test packages/exploration_agent test/vm_service_client_fromvmservice_test.dart` → PASS
   Commit: `test(exploration_agent): fromVmService web-path regression test`

4. **`exploration_devtools` — `manifest_probe.dart`: parameterless `ManifestProbe` + `probeManifest`** — `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/manifest_probe.dart`. Add `import 'package:vm_service/vm_service.dart' show VmService;`. Change the typedef to `typedef ManifestProbe = Future<List<PluginManifestEntry>> Function();` (the closure owns its connection). Add a free function `Future<List<PluginManifestEntry>> probeManifest(VmService vm, String isolateId)` that does `final c = VmServiceClient.fromVmService(vm, isolateId); return (await c.handshake()).plugins;` — no `.dispose()` (the connection is owned by `serviceManager`). Delete `defaultManifestProbe`. `ManifestProbeResult` variants and `BindingNotInitializedError` handling are unchanged (the host still maps that error → `ManifestProbeBindingMissing`).
   Test: `dart analyze packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/manifest_probe.dart` → no errors
   Commit: `refactor(exploration_devtools): parameterless ManifestProbe + probeManifest`

5. **`exploration_devtools` — `panel_host.dart`: drop `vmServiceUri`, require `manifestProbe` + `sessionFactory`** — `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panel_host.dart`. Remove `VmServiceUriResolver`, the `vmServiceUri` field and all its uses. `manifestProbe` and a new `sessionFactory` (`SessionFactory` re-used from `prompt_panel_controller.dart` via its import) become required ctor params with no defaults. `refreshManifest`: drop the `widget.vmServiceUri()` null branch; body is `_manifest.value = const ManifestProbeLoading(); try { final plugins = await widget.manifestProbe(); ... ManifestProbeLoaded(plugins); } on BindingNotInitializedError { ... ManifestProbeBindingMissing(); } catch (e) { ... ManifestProbeFailed(e.toString()); }` (keep the `_probeGen`/`mounted` stale-result guards). `ensureSession`: `final created = await widget.sessionFactory(); if (!mounted) { await created.end(); throw StateError('Panel host disposed during connect'); } setState(() => _session = created); return created;`.
   Test: `dart analyze packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panel_host.dart` → no errors
   Commit: `refactor(exploration_devtools): panel host takes manifestProbe + sessionFactory, drops vmServiceUri`

6. **`exploration_devtools` — `prompt_panel_controller.dart`: parameterless `SessionFactory`** — `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/prompt_panel_controller.dart`. `typedef SessionFactory = Future<ExplorationSession> Function();`. Drop the `vmServiceUri` ctor field; `factory` becomes a required ctor param (remove the `?? ExplorationSession.connect` default). In `start()`, `_factory(vmServiceUri)` → `_factory()`. Update the typedef/ctor doc comments ("production wires a closure over `serviceManager.service` + main isolate id").
   Test: `dart analyze packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/prompt_panel_controller.dart` → no errors
   Commit: `refactor(exploration_devtools): SessionFactory is parameterless; controller requires it`

7. **`exploration_devtools` — thread the closures through mount → shell → main** —
   - `panels/prompt_tab_mount.dart`: `controllerFactory` type → `PromptPanelController Function()?`, but since `PromptPanelController.factory` is now required, make `controllerFactory` a **required** `PromptTabMount` ctor param (the shell always supplies it). `_ensureController()` drops the `Uri uri` param and calls `widget.controllerFactory()`. `_onStart`: drop `host.widget.vmServiceUri()` / `Uri.parse`; just `final c = _ensureController(); await c.start(cfg, providerCfg: _state.value.config);` then the existing `runFuture.whenComplete` teardown.
   - `exploration_shell.dart`: ctor params → `required ManifestProbe manifestProbe`, `required SessionFactory sessionFactory`, `Listenable? probeRetrigger` (renamed from `vmServiceUriListenable`); remove `vmServiceUri`. Pass `manifestProbe` + `sessionFactory` to `ExplorationPanelHost`. Build `PromptTabMount(controllerFactory: () => PromptPanelController(factory: widget.sessionFactory), store: ..., catalog: ..., trajectorySink: ...)`. Rename `_onUriChanged` → `_onRetrigger`, listening to `probeRetrigger`.
   - `main.dart`: inside the existing `Builder`, build `ManifestProbe probe = () async { final vm = serviceManager.service; final id = serviceManager.isolateManager.mainIsolate.value?.id; if (vm == null || id == null) throw BindingNotInitializedError(); return probeManifest(vm, id); };` and `SessionFactory session = () async { final vm = serviceManager.service; final id = serviceManager.isolateManager.mainIsolate.value?.id; if (vm == null || id == null) throw StateError('VM service / main isolate not available'); return ExplorationSession.fromVmService(vm, id); };`, then `ExplorationShell(manifestProbe: probe, sessionFactory: session, probeRetrigger: Listenable.merge([serviceManager.connectedState, serviceManager.isolateManager.mainIsolate]))`. Add `import 'package:exploration_agent/exploration_agent.dart' show BindingNotInitializedError, ExplorationSession;` and the `manifest_probe.dart` / `panels/prompt_panel_controller.dart` imports for the typedefs/helpers.
   Test: `dart analyze packages/exploration_devtools/lib` → no errors
   Commit: `feat(exploration_devtools): wire serviceManager VmService into manifest probe + session factory`

8. **`exploration_devtools` — update tests for the new seams + add the web-wiring regression test** —
   - `test/panel_host_manifest_test.dart`: `_host(...)` drops `vmServiceUri`, adds `manifestProbe: () async => [...]` and `sessionFactory: () async => _FakeSession()` (trivial `implements ExplorationSession` stub); probe stubs `(Uri _) async => ...` → `() async => ...`. Delete the "null vmServiceUri publishes ManifestProbeBindingMissing" case (replaced by step-8 regression file).
   - `test/shell_test.dart`: `ExplorationShell(vmServiceUri: ..., manifestProbe: _staticProbe(...))` → `ExplorationShell(manifestProbe: () async => [...], sessionFactory: () async => _FakeSession())`; `_staticProbe`/`_throwingProbe` drop the `Uri` param; the "loading state" probe `(Uri _) => completer.future` → `() => completer.future`.
   - `test/thinking/thinking_tab_test.dart`: `ExplorationShell(vmServiceUri: () => null)` → `ExplorationShell(manifestProbe: () async => const [], sessionFactory: () async => throw StateError('no session'))`.
   - `test/panels/prompt_panel_controller_test.dart` + `test/panels/prompt_tab_mount_test.dart`: drop `vmServiceUri: Uri.parse('ws://x')`; `factory: (_) async => fake` → `factory: () async => fake`.
   - **New `packages/exploration_devtools/test/web_wiring_test.dart`**: (1) build a `_FakeVmService` responding to `ext.flutter.exploration.handshake` with one plugin (`router`); `probe = () => probeManifest(fake, 'iso-1')`; pump `MaterialApp(home: ExplorationShell(manifestProbe: probe, sessionFactory: () async => throw StateError('x')))`; `await tester.pumpAndSettle()`; `expect(find.byKey(const Key('prompt.plugin.router')), findsOneWidget)` — exercises `VmServiceClient.fromVmService` with no `vm_service_io`. (2) `missing = () async { const VmService? vm = null; if (vm == null) throw BindingNotInitializedError(); return probeManifest(vm, 'iso-1'); }`; pump `ExplorationShell(manifestProbe: missing, sessionFactory: () async => throw StateError('x'))`; `expect(find.byKey(const Key('prompt.bindingNotDetected')), findsOneWidget)`.
   Test: `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools` → PASS; `dart test packages/exploration_agent` → PASS; `dart test packages/exploration_cli` → PASS
   Commit: `test(exploration_devtools): cover serviceManager-wired probe + session seams`

9. **File the follow-up bead + link it** — `bd create -t bug -p 3 -d "Confine vm_service_io.dart to a *_io seam (move connect()/vm_service_io import out of the web-safe vm_service_client.dart + ExplorationSession.connect into a CLI-only library; extend tool/check_no_dart_io.dart to also flag vm_service_io.dart imports in web code). Discovered while fixing lenny-dzh." "exploration_agent: vm_service_io.dart still imported by the web-safe lib"`, then `bd dep add <new-id> lenny-dzh` (the new bead depends on this one). No code/test — housekeeping the fork resolution promised.

**Files:**

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `exploration_agent` exposes a public (non-`@visibleForTesting`) `VmServiceClient.fromVmService(VmService vm, String isolateId)` and `ExplorationSession.fromVmService(VmService vm, String isolateId)`; both are synchronous wrappers over an already-connected `VmService` and pull in no `dart:io` (`dart run packages/exploration_agent/tool/check_no_dart_io.dart` stays green)
- [ ] The DevTools panel's manifest probe AND the Prompt-tab Start-button session creation obtain their `VmService` + isolate id from `serviceManager` (`serviceManager.service`, `serviceManager.isolateManager.mainIsolate.value?.id`) — neither code path calls `vmServiceConnectUri` / `VmServiceClient.connect(Uri)` / `ExplorationSession.connect(Uri)`
- [ ] When `serviceManager.service` is null or `mainIsolate.value?.id` is null, the manifest probe resolves to `ManifestProbeBindingMissing` (never an uncaught `dart:io` exception surfaced as `ManifestProbeFailed`); a session-creation attempt under the same condition throws a clear `StateError` rather than an `Unsupported operation` crash
- [ ] In the dogfood DevTools/Chrome environment the Prompt tab no longer renders "Unsupported operation: Platform._version"; with `ExplorationBinding` initialised in the target it shows the live plugin manifest (possibly empty), and "Binding not detected" appears only when the handshake extension is genuinely absent (verified manually against `sample_app` + `package:exploration`)
- [ ] `exploration_cli` is unchanged at the source level and still connects via `ExplorationSession.connect(Uri)` / `vm_service_io` (CLI runs on the Dart VM)
- [ ] A `flutter test` regression test pumps `ExplorationShell` with a `manifestProbe` built from `probeManifest(fakeVmService, isolateId)` and asserts the manifest loads — exercising the `VmServiceClient.fromVmService` web path without `vm_service_io.dart`; a second test asserts a null-`service` probe closure yields `ManifestProbeBindingMissing`
- [ ] `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools`, `dart test packages/exploration_agent`, and `dart test packages/exploration_cli` are green; `dart analyze` is clean on all three packages
notes: Found while dogfooding the e2e demo (sample_app + DevTools extension). Highest-priority blocker for any DevTools-driven exploration run. Anchors: vm_service_client.dart:11-12,49-55; manifest_probe.dart:53-61; panel_host.dart:75-110; session.dart:46-54; exploration_shell.dart (serviceManager wiring); main.dart:26,32.

AC4 (dogfood Chrome screenshot) is the remaining manual check. Follow-up: lenny-sw6 (vm_service_io CLI-only seam).
