title: Plugin contract: types, registry, and PluginContext
description: The load-bearing v0.5 design artifact. Define ExplorationPlugin, ExplorationTool, BusyState, ObservationContext, ExecutedAction, ToolResult, JsonSchema. Implement the plugin registry: registration order preserved, namespace uniqueness enforced (^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$), tool names mandatorily prefixed (<namespace>.<tool>), collision rejection at startup. Implement PluginContext with registerErrorHandler, registerExtension (namespaced under ext.flutter.exploration.<namespace>.<suffix>), registerFrameCallback. Lifecycle: initialize() in registration order, dispose() at session end. Per-method exception isolation; auto-disable a plugin after 3 consecutive failures. PRD §7.
design:
## Approach
Implement the v0.5 plugin contract (PRD §7.1–§7.8) in `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/`: `ExplorationPlugin`/`ExplorationTool` interfaces, value types, `PluginContext` with auto-namespaced VM extensions, and a `PluginRegistry` enforcing `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$` namespaces, mandatory `<ns>.<tool>` prefixing, registration-order preservation, per-method exception isolation, and per-(plugin × method) 3-strikes auto-disable. `initialize()` failures are non-fatal. Source carries doc comments capturing PRD §7.7 versioning posture and PRD §8 v2 extension points.

## Constraints
- v1 contract marked EXPERIMENTAL in source. cx6.8/.9/.17/.25/.26/.27/.35 consume these names verbatim.
- `_entries` is a `List` so registration order survives every dispatch (PRD §7.8).
- Auto-disable is per-(plugin × method); `initialize` failure flips `initFailed` once and short-circuits all later calls (PRD §17).
- Auto-disable log line emitted exactly once per (plugin, method): `[exploration] plugin <ns> auto-disabled after 3 failures in <method>`.

## Ruled out
- Auto-discovery from `pubspec.yaml` (PRD §18); plugin-to-plugin hard deps (PRD §21); subclassing the binding for extensibility (PRD §7.5); standalone error-chaining bead (collapsed into `registerErrorHandler`).

## Implementation Plan

1. Value types — `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/types.dart`

   Five `const`-constructible classes with final fields:
   - `JsonSchema(Map<String,Object?> raw)`
   - `ToolResult({required bool ok, Object? value, String? error})`
   - `BusyState({required bool isBusy, String? reason, Duration? estimatedDuration})` + `static const idle = BusyState(isBusy:false)`
   - `ObservationContext({required int turn, required Duration sinceLastAction})`
   - `ExecutedAction({required String toolName, required Map<String,Object?> args, required ToolResult result})` (toolName fully qualified)

   Test: `dart analyze exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/types.dart` → clean.

   Commit: `git commit -am "feat(contract): value types"`

2. Plugin & tool interfaces — `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin.dart`

   Imports `types.dart`, `plugin_context.dart`. Two abstract classes:
   - `ExplorationTool` — getters `String name` (bare token), `String description`, `JsonSchema inputSchema`; method `Future<ToolResult> call(Map<String, Object?> args)`.
   - `ExplorationPlugin` — getters `String namespace` (regex `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$`), `List<ExplorationTool> tools`; methods `Future<void> initialize(PluginContext)`, `Future<Map<String, Object?>?> observe(ObservationContext)`, `Future<BusyState> busyState()`, `Future<void> onActionExecuted(ExecutedAction)`, `Future<void> dispose()`.

   Class-level dartdoc on `ExplorationPlugin` MUST contain (verbatim — validation greps for these phrases): `EXPERIMENTAL — v1 plugin contract (PRD §7).`, `Versioning (PRD §7.7): adding tools, fragment fields, or busy heuristics is NOT breaking. Unknown fragment fields are passed through opaquely.`, `v2 extension (PRD §8): List<ExplorationResource> get resources and List<ExplorationPrompt> get prompts may be added with default empty implementations without breaking existing plugins.`

   Test: `dart analyze exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin.dart` → clean.

   Commit: `git commit -am "feat(contract): ExplorationPlugin and ExplorationTool with v2 docs"`

3. PluginContext — `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin_context.dart`

   Imports `dart:developer as developer`, `package:flutter/foundation.dart`, `package:flutter/scheduler.dart`.
   - `typedef ErrorHandler = bool Function(FlutterErrorDetails);`
   - `typedef ExtensionHandler = Future<developer.ServiceExtensionResponse> Function(String method, Map<String, String> parameters);`
   - `class PluginContext` — fields `final String namespace`, `final SchedulerBinding _scheduler`, `final List<ErrorHandler> errorHandlers = []`. Ctor `PluginContext({required this.namespace, required SchedulerBinding scheduler}) : _scheduler = scheduler;`. Static `@visibleForTesting String buildExtensionMethodName(String ns, String suffix) => 'ext.flutter.exploration.$ns.$suffix';`. Methods: `registerErrorHandler` appends to `errorHandlers`; `registerExtension(suffix, h)` calls `developer.registerExtension(buildExtensionMethodName(namespace, suffix), h)`; `registerFrameCallback(cb)` calls `_scheduler.addPostFrameCallback(cb)`.

   Test: `dart analyze exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin_context.dart` → clean.

   Commit: `git commit -am "feat(contract): PluginContext with auto-namespaced VM extensions"`

4. PluginRegistry — `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/registry.dart`

   Internals: `enum _Method { observe, busyState, onActionExecuted }`; per-plugin `_Entry { plugin; context; bool initFailed=false; Map<_Method,int> failures; Map<_Method,bool> disabled; }` (failure counters init 0, disabled init false for every `_Method`). State: `List<_Entry> _entries = []`, `bool _finalized = false`, `_nsRe = RegExp(r'^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$')`. Ctor `PluginRegistry({required SchedulerBinding scheduler})`.

   Public API:
   - `register(ExplorationPlugin p)` — throws `StateError` if `_finalized`; `ArgumentError.value(p.namespace, 'namespace', 'must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*\$')` on regex failure; `StateError('duplicate plugin namespace: ${p.namespace}')` on duplicate; else appends `_Entry(p, PluginContext(namespace: p.namespace, scheduler: _scheduler))`.
   - `mergedTools()` (alias `finalize()`) — for each entry/tool: throw `ArgumentError.value(t.name, 'tool.name', 'must be bare token (no ".")')` on dotted name; `fq = '${ns}.${t.name}'`; throw `StateError('tool name collision: $fq')` on duplicate; insert in registration order. Set `_finalized = true`; return `Map.unmodifiable(out)`.
   - `initializeAll()` — sequential per-plugin try/catch; on throw set `e.initFailed = true` and `debugPrint('[exploration] plugin ${e.plugin.namespace} initialize failed: $err\n$st')`; never rethrows.
   - `_guard<R>(_Entry e, _Method m, Future<R> Function() body, R fallback)` — return `fallback` if `e.initFailed || e.disabled[m]!`. Else `try` body, reset `failures[m]=0` on success. On throw increment, `debugPrint('... ${m.name} threw ...')`, and on first transition to `>=3` set `disabled[m]=true` and emit one `'[exploration] plugin <ns> auto-disabled after 3 failures in <method>'`; return `fallback`.
   - `observeAll(ctx)` — guarded, fallback `null`; inserts only non-null fragments under `e.plugin.namespace` in registration order.
   - `busyStateAll()` — guarded, fallback `BusyState.idle`; returns `List<MapEntry<String, BusyState>>`.
   - `onActionExecutedAll(a)` — guarded, fallback `null`.
   - `disposeAll()` — sequential, per-plugin try/catch; runs every plugin even if earlier ones throw; `debugPrint`s on throw.
   - `dispatchError(FlutterErrorDetails d)` — outer entries (skip `initFailed`); inner `e.context.errorHandlers` in order; first returning `true` short-circuits → `true`; per-handler exceptions are `debugPrint`ed and treated as not-claiming; returns `false` if none claimed.

   Test: `dart analyze exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/registry.dart` → clean.

   Commit: `git commit -am "feat(contract): PluginRegistry with validation, prefixing, isolation, 3-strikes"`

5. Public barrel — `exploration_flutter/lib/contract.dart`

   ```dart
   export 'src/contract/types.dart';
   export 'src/contract/plugin.dart';
   export 'src/contract/plugin_context.dart';
   export 'src/contract/registry.dart' show PluginRegistry;
   ```

   Test: `dart analyze exploration_flutter/lib/contract.dart` → clean.

   Commit: `git commit -am "feat(contract): public barrel"`

6. Registry tests — `exploration_flutter/test/contract/registry_test.dart`

   In-file helper fakes:
   - `_FakeTool` — `name` captured; `description=''`; `inputSchema=const JsonSchema({})`; `call` returns `const ToolResult(ok:true)`.
   - `_OrderPlugin({namespace, log, toolNames=const []})` — appends `'init:$ns'/'obs:$ns'/'busy:$ns'/'act:$ns'/'dispose:$ns'` to `log`; `observe` returns `{'k':1, 'unknown_future_field':'opaque'}`.
   - `_ThrowOnObserve` extends `_OrderPlugin` — `observe` logs then throws `StateError('boom')`.
   - `_ThrowOnDispose` extends `_OrderPlugin` — `dispose` logs then throws.

   Setup in `main()`: `TestWidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized(); final scheduler = SchedulerBinding.instance;`. Each test builds a fresh `PluginRegistry(scheduler: scheduler)`.

   Test cases (names verbatim):
   - `'rejects bad namespace'` — register `_OrderPlugin('Bad-NS')` → `throwsArgumentError`.
   - `'rejects duplicate namespace'` — register `'a'` twice → second `throwsStateError`.
   - `'prefixes tool names and rejects dotted names'` — `_OrderPlugin('router', toolNames:['navigate_to'])` → `mergedTools().keys` contains `'router.navigate_to'`. Fresh registry with dotted tool name `'a.b'` → `throwsArgumentError` from `mergedTools()`.
   - `'rejects intra-plugin tool collision'` — `_OrderPlugin('x', toolNames:['t','t'])` → `mergedTools()` `throwsStateError`.
   - `'preserves registration order across all dispatches'` — register `'a'` then `'b'`; run all dispatches with `const ObservationContext(turn:0, sinceLastAction:Duration.zero)` and `const ExecutedAction(toolName:'core.tap', args:{}, result:ToolResult(ok:true))`; expect `log == ['init:a','init:b','obs:a','obs:b','busy:a','busy:b','act:a','act:b','dispose:a','dispose:b']`.
   - `'observe fragment passes unknown fields through'` — `out['a']` equals `{'k':1, 'unknown_future_field':'opaque'}`.
   - `'per-method exception isolation and 3-strikes auto-disable'` — register `_ThrowOnObserve('x')` and `_OrderPlugin('y')`; loop `observeAll` 5×; each turn `out` lacks `'x'` and contains `'y'`; assert `log.where((s)=>s=='obs:x').length == 3`.
   - `'dispose isolation runs every plugin'` — register `_ThrowOnDispose('a')` then `_OrderPlugin('b')`; `disposeAll`; expect `log == ['dispose:a','dispose:b']`.

   Test: `flutter test exploration_flutter/test/contract/registry_test.dart` → all PASS.

   Commit: `git commit -am "test(contract): registry validation, ordering, isolation, auto-disable"`

7. PluginContext tests — `exploration_flutter/test/contract/plugin_context_test.dart`

   Helper `_HandlerPlugin extends ExplorationPlugin({namespace, handlers})` — `initialize` registers each via `c.registerErrorHandler`; remaining methods no-op. Setup: same as step 6.

   Test cases:
   - `'extension method is auto-namespaced'` — `expect(PluginContext.buildExtensionMethodName('router','ping'), 'ext.flutter.exploration.router.ping')`.
   - `'error handlers run in order; first true short-circuits'` — register three `_HandlerPlugin`s with handlers `[a→false, b→true, c→true]` that append `'a'/'b'/'c'` to `calls`; after `initializeAll`, `r.dispatchError(FlutterErrorDetails(exception: StateError('x')))` returns `true` and `calls == ['a','b']`.
   - `'frame callback forwards to scheduler'` — build `PluginContext(namespace:'a', scheduler: SchedulerBinding.instance)`, register `(_) { fired = true; }`; call `SchedulerBinding.instance.scheduleFrame(); await Future<void>.delayed(const Duration(milliseconds:16));` and assert `fired == true`.

   Test: `flutter test exploration_flutter/test/contract/plugin_context_test.dart` → all PASS.

   Commit: `git commit -am "test(contract): error chaining, frame forwarding, extension namespacing"`

## Touches

**Files (all created):**
- `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/{types,plugin,plugin_context,registry}.dart`
- `exploration_flutter/lib/contract.dart` (public barrel)
- `exploration_flutter/test/contract/{registry_test,plugin_context_test}.dart`

**Symbols exposed via `package:exploration_flutter/contract.dart`:**
- Interfaces: `ExplorationPlugin`, `ExplorationTool`
- Value types: `JsonSchema`, `ToolResult`, `BusyState` (+ `BusyState.idle`), `ObservationContext`, `ExecutedAction`
- `PluginContext` — `{namespace, registerErrorHandler, registerExtension, registerFrameCallback, buildExtensionMethodName}`
- Typedefs: `ErrorHandler`, `ExtensionHandler`
- `PluginRegistry` — `register`, `mergedTools`/`finalize`, `initializeAll`, `observeAll`, `busyStateAll`, `onActionExecutedAll`, `disposeAll`, `dispatchError`

## Validation Plan

- [ ] Types + barrel compile → `dart analyze exploration_flutter/` → `No issues found!`
- [ ] All registry/context tests pass → `flutter test exploration_flutter/test/contract/` → 11 tests PASS, mapping 1:1 to acceptance criteria as follows:
  - `rejects bad namespace` → namespace regex enforcement.
  - `rejects duplicate namespace` → uniqueness enforcement.
  - `prefixes tool names and rejects dotted names` → mandatory `<ns>.<tool>` prefixing + bare-token rule.
  - `rejects intra-plugin tool collision` → tool-name collision rejection at startup.
  - `preserves registration order across all dispatches` → registration-order across `mergedTools`/`observe`/`busyState`/`onActionExecuted`/`dispose`.
  - `observe fragment passes unknown fields through` → PRD §7.7 opaque pass-through.
  - `per-method exception isolation and 3-strikes auto-disable` → per-method isolation + auto-disable + log line.
  - `dispose isolation runs every plugin` → exception isolation in dispose path.
  - `extension method is auto-namespaced` → `ext.flutter.exploration.<ns>.<suffix>` rule.
  - `error handlers run in order; first true short-circuits` → chained error handler API.
  - `frame callback forwards to scheduler` → frame callback hook.
- [ ] `initializeAll` exception isolation → verified by code review of `registry.dart` (try/catch around `e.plugin.initialize` sets `initFailed` and continues; subsequent `_guard` calls short-circuit).
- [ ] v2 extensibility documented → `grep -q "v2 extension" exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin.dart` → match.
- [ ] Versioning posture documented → `grep -q "Versioning (PRD §7.7)" exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin.dart` → match.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Public types compile in `exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/`: `ExplorationPlugin` (abstract), `ExplorationTool`, `BusyState`, `ObservationContext`, `ExecutedAction`, `ToolResult`, `JsonSchema`, `PluginContext`, `ErrorHandler`, `ExtensionHandler`
- [ ] Library barrel `exploration_flutter/lib/contract.dart` re-exports the public contract surface; `dart analyze` is clean
- [ ] `PluginRegistry.register()` rejects a namespace that does not match `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$` with `ArgumentError`
- [ ] `PluginRegistry.register()` rejects a duplicate namespace with `StateError` carrying the offending namespace
- [ ] Tools are auto-prefixed: a plugin whose `namespace = "router"` and tool `name = "navigate_to"` exposes `router.navigate_to` in the merged tool list; `PluginRegistry.mergedTools()` rejects a tool whose name is not a bare token (contains a `.`) with `ArgumentError`
- [ ] Two plugins contributing the same fully-qualified tool name cause `PluginRegistry.finalize()` to throw `StateError` at startup
- [ ] Registration order is preserved across `mergedTools()`, `observeAll()`, `busyStateAll()`, `onActionExecutedAll()`, `disposeAll()` — verified by an order-tracking fake plugin test
- [ ] `PluginContext.registerExtension("ping", handler)` registers the VM service extension as `ext.flutter.exploration.<namespace>.ping`; the underlying `developer.registerExtension` receives the fully-qualified name
- [ ] `PluginContext.registerErrorHandler` stores handlers in registration order; `PluginRegistry.dispatchError(details)` calls them in order and stops at the first handler that returns `true`
- [ ] `PluginContext.registerFrameCallback` forwards to `SchedulerBinding.addPostFrameCallback` (validated via fake binding capturing the call)
- [ ] `PluginRegistry.initializeAll()` calls each plugin's `initialize()` in registration order; an exception in one plugin's `initialize()` is logged and the plugin is marked failed but the session continues
- [ ] Each per-method dispatch (`observe`, `busyState`, `onActionExecuted`) wraps the call in try/catch — an exception is logged with the plugin namespace and the call returns the per-method "no contribution" sentinel (null fragment, `BusyState.idle`, no-op respectively) without propagating
- [ ] After 3 consecutive failures of the same method, the plugin is marked auto-disabled for that method for the rest of the session and a single `[exploration] plugin <ns> auto-disabled after 3 failures in <method>` log line is emitted; further calls are skipped
- [ ] `PluginRegistry.disposeAll()` is exception-isolated and runs every plugin's `dispose()` even if earlier ones throw
- [ ] Unfamiliar fields in a plugin's observation fragment are passed through unchanged (host does not strip unknown keys) — verified by a test asserting structural equality on a fragment containing future keys
- [ ] `ExplorationPlugin` is shaped so adding `List<ExplorationResource> get resources => const []` and `List<ExplorationPrompt> get prompts => const []` would be source-compatible (default-implemented getters); a documentation comment in the source records this constraint

title: ExplorationBinding skeleton, lifecycle, and conflict detection
description: Host binding extends WidgetsFlutterBinding. ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized(plugins: [...]) called from main(), kDebugMode/profile gating, hard error if another custom binding (IntegrationTestWidgetsFlutterBinding etc.) is already installed, registers the 'ext.flutter.exploration.*' core namespace and a handshake/ping extension. Defines the VM service extension naming convention used by core and namespaced for plugins. PRD §6.1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `ExplorationBinding extends WidgetsFlutterBinding` and installs itself as the singleton `WidgetsBinding.instance` when `ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized(plugins: [...])` is called from `main()` in debug or profile mode
- [ ] `ensureInitialized` throws `StateError` with a message naming the conflicting binding's runtime type if a non-`ExplorationBinding` `WidgetsBinding` is already installed (e.g. `IntegrationTestWidgetsFlutterBinding`)
- [ ] `ensureInitialized` is a no-op (returns without installing the binding, returns `null`, and does NOT register any VM service extensions) outside debug/profile mode (when both `kDebugMode` and `kProfileMode` are false)
- [ ] The reserved namespace `ext.flutter.exploration.*` is documented in the package README with the format `ext.flutter.exploration.<core_or_plugin_namespace>.<suffix>` and the rule that `core` is reserved for host-owned extensions
- [ ] A handshake VM service extension is registered at `ext.flutter.exploration.core.handshake` and returns `{protocolVersion: '1', bindingType: 'ExplorationBinding', flutterMode: 'debug' | 'profile', pluginCount: <int>}`
- [ ] The user-supplied `plugins` list is stored verbatim on the binding (preserves order, no de-duplication) and is exposed to the registry via an internal accessor that cx6.3 will consume
- [ ] Calling `ensureInitialized` twice in the same process returns the same instance and does NOT re-register extensions or throw

title: Repo & multi-package scaffolding
description: Lay down the v1 package layout and CI baseline. Packages: exploration_flutter (host binding), exploration_agent (harness library, web-compatible — NO dart:io), exploration_cli (CLI frontend, where dart:io may live), exploration_devtools (DevTools extension, Flutter web app), exploration_router (reference plugin), exploration_riverpod (reference plugin), exploration_dio (reference plugin). Shared lints/analysis_options, baseline CI (analyze + test) across the workspace. Blocks every downstream story.
design:
## Design Summary
Dart 3.6+ workspace with seven packages — `exploration_flutter` (Flutter host binding), `exploration_agent` (pure-Dart web-compatible harness), `exploration_cli` (Dart CLI, may use `dart:io`), `exploration_devtools` (Flutter web app on `package:devtools_extensions`), and three reference plugins (`exploration_router`/`_riverpod`/`_dio`). Shared root `analysis_options.yaml` included by every package. Baseline GitHub Actions CI runs `dart analyze`, an `exploration_agent` `dart:io`-free grep gate, and per-package tests on PRs.

Key invariants: SDK floors Dart >= 3.11.0 (all) and Flutter >= 3.41.0 (Flutter packages); `exploration_agent/lib/` MUST be `dart:io`-free per PRD §6.3 / §22 (harness runs in DevTools web app); `exploration_devtools` pins `devtools_extensions: ^0.4.0` per §22 (DevTools version-skew). No code beyond green analyze + one trivial test per package — reference plugins are scaffolds only (real implementations land in cx6.25/.26/.27). Single-package layout, pubspec auto-discovery, and Melos are explicitly ruled out (PRD §6/§18; native Dart workspaces suffice). `dart:io` usage belongs in `exploration_cli` or behind DTD in `exploration_devtools` (cx6.21).

## Implementation Plan

1. Root workspace + lints — `pubspec.yaml`, `analysis_options.yaml`

   `pubspec.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   name: lenny_workspace
   publish_to: none
   environment: { sdk: ^3.11.0 }
   workspace:
     - packages/exploration_flutter
     - packages/exploration_agent
     - packages/exploration_cli
     - packages/exploration_devtools
     - packages/exploration_router
     - packages/exploration_riverpod
     - packages/exploration_dio
   ```

   `analysis_options.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   include: package:lints/recommended.yaml
   analyzer:
     language: { strict-casts: true, strict-inference: true, strict-raw-types: true }
   linter:
     rules: [prefer_single_quotes, sort_pub_dependencies, unawaited_futures, avoid_print]
   ```

   Test (paired with step 2): `dart pub get` → exit 0.

   Commit: `chore: initialize Dart workspace with shared lints`

2. `exploration_agent` (pure Dart, web-compatible) — `packages/exploration_agent/`

   `pubspec.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   name: exploration_agent
   description: Web-compatible harness library.
   publish_to: none
   resolution: workspace
   environment: { sdk: ^3.11.0 }
   dependencies: { meta: ^1.16.0 }
   dev_dependencies: { lints: ^5.1.0, test: ^1.25.0 }
   ```

   `analysis_options.yaml`: `include: ../../analysis_options.yaml`.

   `lib/exploration_agent.dart`:
   ```dart
   /// Web-compatible harness library. MUST NOT import dart:io.
   library;
   const String packageName = 'exploration_agent';
   ```

   `test/smoke_test.dart`:
   ```dart
   import 'package:exploration_agent/exploration_agent.dart';
   import 'package:test/test.dart';
   void main() {
     test('package loads', () => expect(packageName, equals('exploration_agent')));
   }
   ```

   Test: `dart pub get && dart analyze packages/exploration_agent && dart test packages/exploration_agent` → analyze `No issues found!`, `+1: All tests passed!`.

   Commit: `feat(exploration_agent): scaffold web-compatible harness package`

3. `exploration_flutter` (Flutter host binding) — `packages/exploration_flutter/`

   `flutter create --template=package --no-pub packages/exploration_flutter`. Overwrite `pubspec.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   name: exploration_flutter
   description: Host WidgetsBinding for the Flutter Exploration Agent.
   publish_to: none
   resolution: workspace
   environment: { sdk: ^3.11.0, flutter: ">=3.41.0" }
   dependencies:
     flutter: { sdk: flutter }
     exploration_agent: any
     meta: ^1.16.0
   dev_dependencies:
     flutter_test: { sdk: flutter }
     lints: ^5.1.0
   ```

   Replace `lib/exploration_flutter.dart`:
   ```dart
   library;
   const String packageName = 'exploration_flutter';
   ```

   Replace `test/exploration_flutter_test.dart` with a `flutter_test` test asserting `packageName == 'exploration_flutter'` (`import 'package:flutter_test/flutter_test.dart';`). Add `analysis_options.yaml`: `include: ../../analysis_options.yaml`.

   Test: `dart pub get && flutter test packages/exploration_flutter` → `+1: All tests passed!`.

   Commit: `feat(exploration_flutter): scaffold host binding package`

4. `exploration_cli` (Dart CLI) — `packages/exploration_cli/`

   `pubspec.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   name: exploration_cli
   description: CLI frontend for the Flutter Exploration Agent.
   publish_to: none
   resolution: workspace
   environment: { sdk: ^3.11.0 }
   dependencies: { exploration_agent: any, args: ^2.6.0 }
   dev_dependencies: { lints: ^5.1.0, test: ^1.25.0 }
   ```

   `bin/exploration_cli.dart`:
   ```dart
   import 'dart:io';
   void main(List<String> arguments) {
     stdout.writeln('exploration_cli v0 — scaffold');
   }
   ```

   `lib/exploration_cli.dart`:
   ```dart
   library;
   const String packageName = 'exploration_cli';
   ```

   `test/smoke_test.dart`: same shape as step 2 asserting `packageName == 'exploration_cli'`. `analysis_options.yaml`: `include: ../../analysis_options.yaml`.

   Test: `dart pub get && dart analyze packages/exploration_cli && dart test packages/exploration_cli` → analyze clean, `+1: All tests passed!`.

   Commit: `feat(exploration_cli): scaffold CLI frontend package`

5. `exploration_devtools` (Flutter web, DevTools extension) — `packages/exploration_devtools/`

   `flutter create --template=app --platforms=web --no-pub packages/exploration_devtools`. Overwrite `pubspec.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   name: exploration_devtools
   description: DevTools extension for the Flutter Exploration Agent.
   publish_to: none
   resolution: workspace
   environment: { sdk: ^3.11.0, flutter: ">=3.41.0" }
   dependencies:
     flutter: { sdk: flutter }
     devtools_extensions: ^0.4.0
     exploration_agent: any
   dev_dependencies:
     flutter_test: { sdk: flutter }
     lints: ^5.1.0
   ```

   `extension/devtools/config.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   name: exploration
   issueTracker: https://example.invalid
   version: 0.0.1
   materialIconCodePoint: 0xe1bd
   ```

   Replace `lib/main.dart`:
   ```dart
   import 'package:devtools_extensions/devtools_extensions.dart';
   import 'package:flutter/widgets.dart';
   const String packageName = 'exploration_devtools';
   void main() => runApp(const DevToolsExtension(child: SizedBox.shrink()));
   ```

   Replace generated widget test with a `flutter_test` smoke test asserting `packageName == 'exploration_devtools'`. Add `analysis_options.yaml`: `include: ../../analysis_options.yaml`.

   Test: `dart pub get && flutter test packages/exploration_devtools` → `+1: All tests passed!`.

   Commit: `feat(exploration_devtools): scaffold DevTools extension package`

6. Three reference plugins — `packages/exploration_{router,riverpod,dio}/`

   For each `<NAME>` in `{exploration_router, exploration_riverpod, exploration_dio}`:

   `pubspec.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   name: <NAME>
   description: Reference plugin (<NAME>) for the Flutter Exploration Agent.
   publish_to: none
   resolution: workspace
   environment: { sdk: ^3.11.0, flutter: ">=3.41.0" }
   dependencies:
     flutter: { sdk: flutter }
     exploration_flutter: any
   dev_dependencies:
     flutter_test: { sdk: flutter }
     lints: ^5.1.0
   ```

   `lib/<NAME>.dart`:
   ```dart
   library;
   const String packageName = '<NAME>';
   ```

   `test/smoke_test.dart`: `flutter_test` test asserting `packageName == '<NAME>'`. `analysis_options.yaml`: `include: ../../analysis_options.yaml`.

   Test: `dart pub get && flutter test packages/exploration_router && flutter test packages/exploration_riverpod && flutter test packages/exploration_dio` → each `+1: All tests passed!`.

   Commit: `feat(plugins): scaffold three reference plugin packages`

7. Web-compatibility gate — `tool/check_no_dart_io.sh`

   ```bash
   #!/usr/bin/env bash
   set -euo pipefail
   m=$(grep -rEn "^[[:space:]]*import[[:space:]]+['\"]dart:io['\"]" \
     packages/exploration_agent/lib || true)
   if [ -n "$m" ]; then
     echo "ERROR: exploration_agent/lib must not import dart:io" >&2
     echo "$m" >&2; exit 1
   fi
   echo "OK: exploration_agent/lib is dart:io-free"
   ```
   Then `chmod +x tool/check_no_dart_io.sh`.

   Test: `./tool/check_no_dart_io.sh` → prints `OK: exploration_agent/lib is dart:io-free`, exit 0.

   Commit: `chore(ci): add dart:io gate for exploration_agent`

8. Baseline CI — `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`

   ```yaml
   name: ci
   on:
     pull_request:
     push: { branches: [main] }
   jobs:
     analyze-and-test:
       runs-on: ubuntu-latest
       steps:
         - uses: actions/checkout@v4
         - uses: subosito/flutter-action@v2
           with: { flutter-version: '3.41.4', channel: 'stable' }
         - run: dart pub get
         - run: dart analyze
         - run: ./tool/check_no_dart_io.sh
         - run: dart test packages/exploration_agent
         - run: dart test packages/exploration_cli
         - run: flutter test packages/exploration_flutter
         - run: flutter test packages/exploration_devtools
         - run: flutter test packages/exploration_router
         - run: flutter test packages/exploration_riverpod
         - run: flutter test packages/exploration_dio
   ```

   Test (locally simulate): run each `run:` command in order from a fresh clone; each exits 0; `dart analyze` prints `No issues found!`.

   Commit: `ci: add baseline analyze + test workflow`

## Touches

**Files:**
- `pubspec.yaml`, `analysis_options.yaml`, `tool/check_no_dart_io.sh`, `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` — created (root)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/{pubspec.yaml,analysis_options.yaml,lib/exploration_flutter.dart,test/exploration_flutter_test.dart}` — created
- `packages/exploration_agent/{pubspec.yaml,analysis_options.yaml,lib/exploration_agent.dart,test/smoke_test.dart}` — created
- `packages/exploration_cli/{pubspec.yaml,analysis_options.yaml,lib/exploration_cli.dart,bin/exploration_cli.dart,test/smoke_test.dart}` — created
- `packages/exploration_devtools/{pubspec.yaml,analysis_options.yaml,lib/main.dart,extension/devtools/config.yaml,test/smoke_test.dart}` — created
- `packages/exploration_{router,riverpod,dio}/{pubspec.yaml,analysis_options.yaml,lib/exploration_<name>.dart,test/smoke_test.dart}` — created (one per plugin)

**Symbols added/exposed (consumed by sibling beads):**
- The seven package names — siblings import as `package:<name>/...`. cx6.2/.3/.4/.5/.6/.7/.8/.9/.10 build inside `exploration_flutter`; cx6.11–.19 inside `exploration_agent`; cx6.20 inside `exploration_cli`; cx6.21–.24 inside `exploration_devtools`; cx6.25/.26/.27 inside the matching plugin.
- `exploration_<name>.packageName` — `String` const in each library entry; trivial marker so siblings can `import` and reference something until their real APIs land. Removable in any sibling's first PR.
- `resolution: workspace` so siblings adding cross-package deps don't need path overrides.

## Validation Plan

- [ ] Workspace + 7 packages exist → `test -f pubspec.yaml && for p in exploration_flutter exploration_agent exploration_cli exploration_devtools exploration_router exploration_riverpod exploration_dio; do test -d "packages/$p" || exit 1; done && echo OK` → expect `OK`.
- [ ] Shared lints referenced everywhere → `for p in packages/*/analysis_options.yaml; do grep -q "../../analysis_options.yaml" "$p" || exit 1; done && echo OK` → expect `OK`.
- [ ] Workspace resolves → `dart pub get` → exit 0.
- [ ] Analyze green → `dart analyze` → `No issues found!`.
- [ ] Each package's trivial test passes → run all 7 `dart test`/`flutter test` commands from the CI workflow; each → `+1: All tests passed!`.
- [ ] `exploration_agent` is `dart:io`-free → `./tool/check_no_dart_io.sh` → `OK: exploration_agent/lib is dart:io-free`.
- [ ] `exploration_devtools` declares `devtools_extensions ^0.4.0` → `grep -E "devtools_extensions:\s*\^?0\.[4-9]" packages/exploration_devtools/pubspec.yaml` → match.
- [ ] SDK floors pinned in all 7 packages → `grep -lE "sdk:\s*\^3\.(11|1[2-9])" packages/*/pubspec.yaml | wc -l` → `7`.
- [ ] CI workflow is valid YAML → `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/ci.yaml'))"` → exit 0.

- [ ] `exploration_devtools` `pubspec.yaml` declares a dependency on `devtools_extensions` with a pinned minimum version (>= 0.4.0) and includes the `devtools_extensions` config block.
- [ ] `exploration_flutter` and `exploration_devtools` pin a minimum Flutter SDK consistent with the installed toolchain (>= 3.41.0) and Dart SDK (>= 3.11.0); other packages pin Dart SDK >= 3.11.0.
- [ ] GitHub Actions workflow `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` runs on PR, executes `dart pub get`, `dart analyze`, package tests, and the `exploration_agent` web-compatibility grep gate; the workflow passes on a fresh clone of the resulting tree.

title: Spike: verify mlx-vlm supports the qwen3_coder tool-call parser
description:
PRD v0.5 §16.3 + §22 risk: the local-inference path depends on mlx-vlm correctly parsing Qwen3.6-35B-A3B's tool-call format. vLLM and SGLang require '--tool-call-parser qwen3_coder'; whether mlx-vlm handles the same parser format is unknown. This is a pre-build spike — its outcome shapes the local provider design and may force a fallback to mlx-lm text-only (losing vision) or moving local inference to SGLang on Linux (losing the M3 Ultra setup).

## Alternatives Considered

(See `design` field's "Alternatives Considered" section for full rationale.)

- mlx-lm text-only as primary — rejected: loses vision (PRD §16.3 hard requirement).
- SGLang on Linux as primary — rejected upfront: loses the M3 Ultra setup; chosen only if mlx-vlm fails T2 unrecoverably.
- Custom parser shim wrapping mlx-vlm raw output — chosen if only T2 needs adapting.
- vLLM on M3 Ultra — rejected: no working CUDA-free Metal backend at spike time.
- Network-routed Linux inference — rejected for v0.5: extra hop and second machine.
design:
## Implementation Plan

1. Create spike scaffolding — `docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md` and `infra/mlx-vlm/`

   Create the doc skeleton and infra directory:

   ```bash
   mkdir -p docs/spikes infra/mlx-vlm/scripts
   cat > docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md <<'DOC'
   # Spike 18f — mlx-vlm + qwen3_coder tool-call parser

   ## Environment
   - Host: M3 Ultra, macOS <fill>, Python <fill>
   - mlx-vlm version: <pinned>
   - Model: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B @ 8-bit (HF id: <fill>)
   - Server invocation: see `infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/serve.sh`

   ## Smoke Tests
   ### T1 Text chat
   ### T2 qwen3_coder tool call
   ### T3 Vision image_url
   ### T4 preserve_thinking across turns
   ### T5 Structured output (JSON schema)

   ## CORS
   ## Sampling defaults
   ## Decision
   ## Pinned versions
   DOC
   ```

   **Test:**
   ```bash
   test -f docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md && test -d infra/mlx-vlm/scripts && echo OK
   ```
   Expected: `OK`

   **Commit:**
   ```bash
   git add docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md infra/mlx-vlm/
   git commit -m "docs(spike-18f): scaffold mlx-vlm tool-call spike"
   ```

2. Pin mlx-vlm and stand up the server — `infra/mlx-vlm/requirements.txt`, `infra/mlx-vlm/.env.example`, `infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/serve.sh`

   ```text
   # infra/mlx-vlm/requirements.txt
   mlx-vlm==<latest-at-spike-time>
   pillow>=10.0.0
   ```

   ```bash
   # infra/mlx-vlm/.env.example
   MLX_MODEL=mlx-community/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-8bit
   MLX_PORT=8080
   MLX_HOST=127.0.0.1
   MLX_CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN=http://localhost:5173
   ```

   ```bash
   # infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/serve.sh
   #!/usr/bin/env bash
   set -euo pipefail
   cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
   set -a; source .env; set +a
   exec python -m mlx_vlm.server \
     --model "$MLX_MODEL" \
     --host "$MLX_HOST" \
     --port "$MLX_PORT" \
     --temperature 1.0 --top-p 0.95 --top-k 20 \
     --repetition-penalty 1.0
   ```

   **Test:**
   ```bash
   chmod +x infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/serve.sh
   cp infra/mlx-vlm/.env.example infra/mlx-vlm/.env
   bash -n infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/serve.sh && echo OK
   ```
   Expected: `OK` (syntax check; full launch requires the box)

   **Commit:**
   ```bash
   git add infra/mlx-vlm/
   git commit -m "feat(spike-18f): pin mlx-vlm and add launch script"
   ```

3. Write smoke-test driver — `infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/smoke.py`

   Hits the OpenAI-compatible endpoint for T1-T5, captures raw transcripts to `docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/T{1..5}.json`.

   ```python
   import base64, json, os, pathlib, sys, time, urllib.request
   BASE = f"http://{os.environ['MLX_HOST']}:{os.environ['MLX_PORT']}/v1"
   OUT = pathlib.Path("docs/spikes/18f-transcripts"); OUT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   SAMPLING = {"temperature":1.0,"top_p":0.95,"top_k":20,
               "presence_penalty":1.5,"frequency_penalty":0.0}

   def post(path, body):
       req = urllib.request.Request(BASE+path,
           data=json.dumps(body).encode(),
           headers={"Content-Type":"application/json"})
       t0=time.time(); r=urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=120)
       return time.time()-t0, json.loads(r.read())

   def save(name, req, dt, resp):
       (OUT/f"{name}.json").write_text(json.dumps(
           {"request":req,"latency_s":dt,"response":resp}, indent=2))

   # T1
   req={"model":"qwen","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Say hi in 3 words."}],**SAMPLING}
   dt,resp=post("/chat/completions",req); save("T1",req,dt,resp)

   # T2 tool calls (qwen3_coder)
   tools=[{"type":"function","function":{"name":"get_weather",
     "parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"city":{"type":"string"}},"required":["city"]}}},
     {"type":"function","function":{"name":"add",
     "parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"a":{"type":"number"},"b":{"type":"number"}},"required":["a","b"]}}}]
   req={"model":"qwen","tools":tools,"tool_choice":"auto",
        "messages":[{"role":"user","content":"What is the weather in Paris and what is 2+3?"}],**SAMPLING}
   dt,resp=post("/chat/completions",req); save("T2",req,dt,resp)

   # T3 vision
   img=base64.b64encode(pathlib.Path("infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/fixture.png").read_bytes()).decode()
   req={"model":"qwen","messages":[{"role":"user","content":[
     {"type":"text","text":"Describe this image in one sentence."},
     {"type":"image_url","image_url":{"url":f"data:image/png;base64,{img}"}}]},],**SAMPLING}
   dt,resp=post("/chat/completions",req); save("T3",req,dt,resp)

   # T4 preserve_thinking across two turns
   msgs=[{"role":"user","content":"Think step by step. What is 17*23?"}]
   req={"model":"qwen","messages":msgs,"preserve_thinking":True,**SAMPLING}
   dt1,r1=post("/chat/completions",req)
   msgs.append(r1["choices"][0]["message"])
   msgs.append({"role":"user","content":"Now subtract 11 from that."})
   req2={"model":"qwen","messages":msgs,"preserve_thinking":True,**SAMPLING}
   dt2,r2=post("/chat/completions",req2)
   save("T4",{"turn1":req,"turn2":req2},dt1+dt2,{"turn1":r1,"turn2":r2,"turn2_latency_s":dt2})

   # T5 structured output
   schema={"type":"object","properties":{"city":{"type":"string"},"temp_c":{"type":"number"}},
     "required":["city","temp_c"],"additionalProperties":False}
   req={"model":"qwen","response_format":{"type":"json_schema",
        "json_schema":{"name":"weather","schema":schema,"strict":True}},
        "messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Return JSON for Paris weather, temp 18C."}],**SAMPLING}
   dt,resp=post("/chat/completions",req); save("T5",req,dt,resp)
   print("done")
   ```

   **Test:**
   ```bash
   python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/smoke.py').read())" && echo OK
   ```
   Expected: `OK`

   **Commit:**
   ```bash
   git add infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/smoke.py
   git commit -m "feat(spike-18f): add T1-T5 smoke-test driver"
   ```

4. Add image fixture and run the smoke battery — `infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/fixture.png`, `docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/`

   Use any 256x256 PNG (e.g. a screenshot of a Flutter sample app) committed as `fixture.png`. Then on the M3 Ultra:

   ```bash
   pip install -r infra/mlx-vlm/requirements.txt
   bash infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/serve.sh &   # background
   sleep 60   # wait for model load
   set -a; source infra/mlx-vlm/.env; set +a
   python infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/smoke.py
   ```

   **Test:**
   ```bash
   ls docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/T{1,2,3,4,5}.json
   ```
   Expected: all five files exist and are valid JSON (`python -m json.tool` on each)

   **Commit:**
   ```bash
   git add infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/fixture.png docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/
   git commit -m "test(spike-18f): record T1-T5 smoke-test transcripts"
   ```

5. Probe CORS from a browser-origin context — `docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/cors.txt`

   Send a preflight + actual request with `Origin` set, capture the response headers verbatim:

   ```bash
   set -a; source infra/mlx-vlm/.env; set +a
   {
     echo "=== OPTIONS preflight ==="
     curl -sS -i -X OPTIONS "http://$MLX_HOST:$MLX_PORT/v1/chat/completions" \
       -H "Origin: http://localhost:5173" \
       -H "Access-Control-Request-Method: POST" \
       -H "Access-Control-Request-Headers: content-type"
     echo
     echo "=== POST with Origin ==="
     curl -sS -i -X POST "http://$MLX_HOST:$MLX_PORT/v1/chat/completions" \
       -H "Origin: http://localhost:5173" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
       -d '{"model":"qwen","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"hi"}]}'
   } > docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/cors.txt
   grep -i 'access-control' docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/cors.txt || true
   ```

   If `mlx-vlm.server` lacks CORS support, document the wrapper used (e.g., a small FastAPI proxy or `caddy reverse_proxy` with `header Access-Control-Allow-Origin {origin}`) and commit its config under `infra/mlx-vlm/`.

   **Test:**
   ```bash
   test -s docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/cors.txt && echo OK
   ```
   Expected: `OK`, and the file contains either native CORS headers from mlx-vlm or evidence of the proxy returning them.

   **Commit:**
   ```bash
   git add docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/cors.txt infra/mlx-vlm/
   git commit -m "test(spike-18f): record CORS preflight + post probe"
   ```

6. Fill in the spike doc with results, decision, and pinned versions — `docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md`

   For each of T1-T5, paste a 5-15 line excerpt of the transcript, mark pass/fail, and note any deviations (e.g., tool calls returned as raw text instead of structured `tool_calls[]`, vision token errors, repetition under the spec's penalty values).

   Then write the Decision section using this exact decision matrix:

   ```markdown
   ## Decision
   Choice: <mlx-vlm-primary | mlx-lm-text-fallback | sglang-linux-fallback | parser-shim>

   Rationale (cite T1-T5):
   - T2 tool-call parsing: <observed>
   - T3 vision: <observed>
   - T4 preserve_thinking: <observed>
   - T5 structured output: <observed>
   - CORS: <native | proxied | blocker>

   If mlx-vlm-primary: requires no fallback; lenny-cx6.15 implements against this server config.
   If mlx-lm-text-fallback: lenny-cx6.15 sets capability vision=false; epic must reroute screenshot path.
   If sglang-linux-fallback: lenny-cx6.15 targets SGLang's OpenAI endpoint with --tool-call-parser qwen3_coder; M3 Ultra path is dropped.
   If parser-shim: lenny-cx6.15 ships a regex-based extractor that converts raw model output -> tool_calls[]; document the regex here.

   ## Pinned versions
   - mlx-vlm: <exact>
   - Model HF revision: <commit-sha>
   - Python: <X.Y.Z>
   ```

   **Test:**
   ```bash
   grep -E '^Choice: (mlx-vlm-primary|mlx-lm-text-fallback|sglang-linux-fallback|parser-shim)$' \
     docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md
   ```
   Expected: a single matching line (no `<...>` placeholder remaining).

   **Commit:**
   ```bash
   git add docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md
   git commit -m "docs(spike-18f): record results and decision"
   ```

7. File the decision into bead memory and unblock downstream — `bd remember` + bead comment

   ```bash
   CHOICE=$(grep -E '^Choice: ' docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md | head -1 | awk '{print $2}')
   MLXV=$(grep -E '^- mlx-vlm:' docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md | awk '{print $3}')
   bd remember "spike-18f decision: $CHOICE; mlx-vlm=$MLXV; see docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md" --tag spike-18f --tag local-inference
   bd comments add lenny-18f "Spike complete. Decision: $CHOICE. See docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md and infra/mlx-vlm/." --actor specify
   ```

   **Test:**
   ```bash
   bd remember list --tag spike-18f | grep -q 'spike-18f decision:' && echo OK
   ```
   Expected: `OK`

   **Commit:** none (bd state, not git).

## Touches

**Files:**
- `docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md` — created (spike report with T1-T5, CORS, decision, pinned versions)
- `docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/T1.json` … `T5.json` — created (raw smoke-test transcripts)
- `docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/cors.txt` — created (preflight + POST probe output)
- `infra/mlx-vlm/requirements.txt` — created (pinned mlx-vlm version)
- `infra/mlx-vlm/.env.example` — created (model, host, port, CORS origin)
- `infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/serve.sh` — created (one-command launch with sampling defaults)
- `infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/smoke.py` — created (T1-T5 driver)
- `infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/fixture.png` — created (vision-test image)

**Symbols added/exposed:**
- None (this is a decision spike; no application code is added).
- Decision artifact consumed by lenny-cx6.15: `docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md` (`Choice:` line + `## Pinned versions`) and `infra/mlx-vlm/` sample config.

## Validation Plan

- [ ] AC: spike doc + per-test transcripts exist → `ls docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/T{1,2,3,4,5}.json` → all listed
- [ ] AC: T1 text chat recorded → `python -m json.tool docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/T1.json | head -20` → valid JSON with `choices[0].message.content`
- [ ] AC: T2 tool call recorded → `python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/T2.json')); print(d['response']['choices'][0]['message'].get('tool_calls') or d['response']['choices'][0]['message'].get('content'))"` → either structured `tool_calls` array OR raw content (the spike doc states which)
- [ ] AC: T3 vision recorded → `python -m json.tool docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/T3.json | grep -i -E 'image|describe|content'` → response references image
- [ ] AC: T4 preserve_thinking recorded → `python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/T4.json')); print(d['response']['turn2_latency_s'])"` → numeric latency present
- [ ] AC: T5 structured output recorded → `python -c "import json,jsonschema; d=json.load(open('docs/spikes/18f-transcripts/T5.json')); c=json.loads(d['response']['choices'][0]['message']['content']); jsonschema.validate(c,{'type':'object','properties':{'city':{'type':'string'},'temp_c':{'type':'number'}},'required':['city','temp_c']})"` → no exception (or spike doc records why T5 failed)
- [ ] AC: decision recorded → `grep -E '^Choice: (mlx-vlm-primary|mlx-lm-text-fallback|sglang-linux-fallback|parser-shim)$' docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md` → one matching line
- [ ] AC: CORS documented → `grep -i 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md` → present
- [ ] AC: sampling defaults present in committed config → `grep -E 'temperature 1.0|top-p 0.95|top-k 20' infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/serve.sh` → all three matched, and spike doc T1 transcript shows non-empty, non-runaway output
- [ ] AC: sample server config runnable → `bash -n infra/mlx-vlm/scripts/serve.sh && test -f infra/mlx-vlm/.env.example && test -f infra/mlx-vlm/requirements.txt` → exit 0
- [ ] AC: bd remember entry filed → `bd remember list --tag spike-18f | grep -q 'spike-18f decision:'` → match

## Alternatives Considered

- **mlx-lm text-only as primary.** Rejected upfront: PRD §16.3 requires vision for the screenshot-driven observation loop. Falls back to this only if T3 (vision) is the sole failing test and T2 passes — then lenny-cx6.15 must drop screenshot capability and the epic must reroute.
- **SGLang on Linux as primary.** Rejected upfront: loses the M3 Ultra setup the user has standing. Becomes the choice if T2 (tool-call parsing) fails on mlx-vlm in a way no shim can fix and T3/T5 don't both pass either, since SGLang already supports `--tool-call-parser qwen3_coder` natively.
- **Custom parser shim wrapping mlx-vlm raw output.** Considered if T2 returns the qwen3_coder XML/JSON envelope as `content` rather than structured `tool_calls[]`. Cheaper than switching servers, but adds maintenance surface. Selected only if T1, T3, T4, T5 all pass and only T2 needs adapting.
- **vLLM on the M3 Ultra.** Rejected: vLLM does not have a working CUDA-free Metal backend for Qwen3.6-A3B at spike time; out of scope.
- **Routing all local inference through a Linux box over the network.** Rejected for v0.5: adds a network hop and a second machine to the deployment story; revisit only if SGLang-on-Linux becomes the chosen fallback.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `docs/spikes/18f-mlx-vlm-toolcall.md` exists and contains: environment, model, mlx-vlm version, server invocation, and one section per smoke test (T1-T5) with raw request/response transcripts
- [ ] T1 (text-only chat) recorded: pass/fail with model output excerpt
- [ ] T2 (qwen3_coder tool call with two simple tools) recorded: pass/fail, parsed `tool_calls[]` JSON shown, plus raw model output for comparison
- [ ] T3 (image_url base64 vision input) recorded: pass/fail with model output excerpt referencing image content
- [ ] T4 (preserve_thinking=true across two turns) recorded: pass/fail with second-turn latency and evidence of cache reuse or absence thereof
- [ ] T5 (structured output via JSON schema / response_format) recorded: pass/fail with returned JSON and schema-validation result
- [ ] Decision section in the doc states one of: `mlx-vlm-primary`, `mlx-lm-text-fallback`, `sglang-linux-fallback`, `parser-shim` — with one-paragraph rationale citing T1-T5 outcomes
- [ ] CORS section documents exact headers (`Access-Control-Allow-Origin`, `-Methods`, `-Headers`) needed for the DevTools panel and how they are set for the chosen path
- [ ] Sampling defaults (`temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=20`, `presence_penalty=1.5`, `repetition_penalty=1.0`) are present in the committed sample server config and the spike doc confirms a generation produced output without runaway repetition or empty completions
- [ ] `infra/mlx-vlm/` (or `infra/<chosen-path>/`) contains a runnable sample config: launch script, env file template, and README with one-command start
- [ ] `bd remember` entry filed under tag `spike-18f` summarizing the decision and the exact mlx-vlm (or fallback) version pinned
close_reason: Obviated — swift-infer is the local gateway, see comment

comments.author: Nico Spencer
comments.text: Closed as obviated. The spike scope ('verify mlx-vlm supports qwen3_coder tool-call parser') hedged against an unknown that doesn't apply: the user's local inference path is swift-infer, their own gateway, with tool-call routing they wrote and verified themselves. The risk this spike was guarding against (mlx-vlm parser support) is bypassed by the architecture choice. Standalone mlx-vlm support remains a hypothetical fallback (PRD §16.3) but is not on v1's critical path.

title: Verify + install the dogfood LaunchAgent (finish lenny-wisp-lm7)
description:
## Problem

lenny-wisp-lm7 shipped `scripts/launchd/run-dogfood.sh` + plist + INSTALL.md but
the LaunchAgent was never installed: run-dogfood.sh invokes
`dart test packages/exploration_agent/test/e2e/dogfood_e2e_test.dart`, which
failed because that test imported Flutter. Once the dogfood e2e test is pure-Dart,
the `dart test` invocation is correct as written — no script change needed.

## Goal

Run `scripts/launchd/run-dogfood.sh` end to end against live swift-infer, confirm
it goes green and that the bd set-state / notification path behaves. Then install
the LaunchAgent per `scripts/launchd/INSTALL.md` (create ~/.lenny-dogfood.env,
sed-substitute the plist, launchctl bootstrap). Confirm `launchctl print` shows
the agent waiting.

title: CI: dart test packages/exploration_agent covers the whole package
description:
## Problem

`.github/workflows/ci.yaml` runs `dart test packages/exploration_agent`. Until the
Flutter-importing tests are converted, that command fails to load three files
(dart:ui unavailable on the Dart VM) — CI for that step is broken.

## Goal

Once exploration_agent is Flutter-free, confirm `dart test packages/exploration_agent`
in CI runs the full package green. Remove any Flutter-test handling that was added
for this package. The runner split is now clean: dart test for exploration_agent,
flutter test for exploration_flutter.

title: exploration_agent: convert observation_fixture_e2e_test.dart to pure-Dart
description:
## Problem

`packages/exploration_agent/test/dogfood/observation_fixture_e2e_test.dart`
imports `package:exploration_flutter`, `flutter_test`, and `BindingVmServiceFake`.
Its stated subject is proving `BindingVmServiceFake` serves a supplied observation
fixture verbatim.

## Goal

Re-point the test at the pure-Dart fake VmService from the foundation bead: it
should prove the pure-Dart fake serves the observation fixture, using
`package:test`. No `exploration_flutter`, no `flutter_test`.

`packages/exploration_agent/test/e2e/dogfood_e2e_test.dart` (the live-swift-infer
dogfood test, lenny-cx6.43) imports `package:exploration_flutter`, `flutter_test`,
and `BindingVmServiceFake` — so it needs `flutter test`. All three scenarios
already use `ObservationFixture.empty()`, so the real binding contributes nothing
the pure-Dart fake cannot.

## Goal

Convert it to pure-Dart: swap `BindingVmServiceFake`/`ExplorationBinding` for the
pure-Dart fake VmService from the foundation bead, `flutter_test` for
`package:test`. The model side stays real swift-infer. After this, the file runs
under `dart test` — which is exactly what lenny-wisp-lm7's run-dogfood.sh already
invokes.

title: Frontier providers drop model thinking content: swift_infer_provider looks for <think>...</think> text, anthropic_provider returns Stream.empty(); both miss Anthropic-native thinking_delta events
description:
## Problem

Both frontier providers — `swift_infer_provider` and `anthropic_provider` — silently ignore the model's thinking content because they're wired for the wrong wire shape.

### What swift-infer actually emits (verified via raw SSE capture against `qwen3.6-moe`)

```
event: content_block_start
data: {"content_block":{"thinking":"","type":"thinking"},"index":0,"type":"content_block_start"}

event: content_block_delta
data: {"delta":{"thinking":"Here","type":"thinking_delta"},"index":0,"type":"content_block_delta"}

event: content_block_delta
data: {"delta":{"thinking":"'s","type":"thinking_delta"},"index":0,"type":"content_block_delta"}
...
```

This is Anthropic's native `thinking_delta` shape — `type:"thinking_delta"`, `delta.thinking:"<text>"`.

### What the agent's parsers look for

**`swift_infer_provider.dart:177-225`** (`_emitThinking`): scans `text_delta` events for `<think>...</think>` tags. The Qwen-native legacy format. Never matches because swift-infer's wire isn't using it.

**`anthropic_provider.dart:61`**: hardcoded `Stream<ThinkingDelta> thinking() => const Stream.empty();`. Returns an empty stream regardless of what the model emits.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start swift-infer locally on `localhost:8080`, model `qwen3.6-moe`.
2. Run the cx6.43 dogfood harness against it (any non-trivial goal, e.g. "tap Sign In").
3. Inspect the resulting trace: `dogfood_turn.decision.thinking_excerpt` is the empty string (`len 0`) on every turn.
4. Cross-reference the swift-infer admin trace for the same `message_id` (e.g. `msg_A280CAACEDA` on Run 11): the `thinking_content` field shows **2350 chars** of real reasoning ("The goal is to 'tap Sign In'. From the previous action summary... node id 8 is the Sign In button...").
5. Capture the raw SSE wire from the same request (`curl -N -H 'accept: text/event-stream' ... /v1/messages`): swift-infer emits `content_block_delta` events with `delta.type == 'thinking_delta'`, not `text_delta` with `<think>...</think>` tags.

The mismatch means every dogfood run is diagnostically blind to model reasoning — the most useful signal for prompt tuning is being silently dropped.

## Expected Behaviour

`ModelProvider.thinking()` emits a `ThinkingDelta` for every chunk of model reasoning the server streams. Both swift_infer (where reasoning is always present) and Anthropic (when extended thinking is enabled by the model) should surface the same `thinking_delta` wire shape through the same code path. The `dogfood_turn.decision.thinking_excerpt` field (capped at 2000 chars per cx6.47) should carry the reasoning text on every turn that emitted any.

## Out of Scope

- Carrying thinking across turns in the conversation history (`lenny-wisp-cl4`).
- Token-budget accounting for thinking (also `lenny-wisp-cl4`).
- OpenAI provider thinking — different wire shape, separate bead if needed.
- Surfacing thinking in the DevTools UI panel (`lenny-cx6.23` may cover the panel side).

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_provider.dart:131-225` (the SSE parser + `_emitThinking`).
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/anthropic/anthropic_provider.dart:61` (the empty-stream stub).
- `packages/exploration_agent/test/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_provider_test.dart` (the existing `<think>` test).
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/frontier/` (home for the new shared decoder).

## Diagnosed via

Raw SSE capture against `localhost:8080/v1/messages` (model `qwen3.6-moe`, agent token) showed Anthropic-native `thinking_delta` events. swift-infer admin trace endpoint cross-referenced with the harness's empty `thinking_excerpt` confirmed the wire mismatch. Filed mid-loop from a dogfood session that closed cx6.43–cx6.48 in sequence.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `provider/frontier/thinking_decoder.dart` exports `ThinkingSseDecoder` that takes a `StreamController<ThinkingDelta>` and exposes `void onEvent(Map<String,dynamic> evt)` plus `void onDone()`; on `content_block_delta` with `delta.type == 'thinking_delta'` it emits `ThinkingDelta(text: delta.thinking, isFinal: false)`; on `content_block_stop` for the thinking block index it emits `ThinkingDelta(text: '', isFinal: true)`.
- [ ] `swift_infer_provider.dart`'s SSE loop routes every parsed event through `ThinkingSseDecoder` so `thinking_delta` deltas flow to `_thinking`; the existing `<think>...</think>` text-delta fallback remains in place for legacy servers.
- [ ] `anthropic_provider.dart` issues a streaming request (`http.Request` + `_client.send`) with `stream: true` in the body and `accept: text/event-stream` in the headers, parses Anthropic SSE events (`content_block_start`, `content_block_delta`, `content_block_stop`, `message_stop`), uses `ThinkingSseDecoder` to surface thinking on a `StreamController<ThinkingDelta>` exposed via `thinking()`, and still returns the `ModelDecision` from the assembled `tool_use` block.
- [ ] `swift_infer_provider_test.dart` has a passing test that feeds an SSE stream containing `content_block_start{type:thinking}` + two `thinking_delta` deltas + `content_block_stop` and asserts the `thinking()` stream emits `['Here', "'s", '']` with the last delta `isFinal: true`.
- [ ] `swift_infer_provider_test.dart`'s legacy `<think>...</think>` text-delta test still passes unchanged (the fallback path is preserved).
- [ ] `anthropic_provider_test.dart` has a passing test that feeds an Anthropic SSE stream containing a `thinking_delta` block followed by a `tool_use` block and asserts (a) `decide()` returns the expected `ModelDecision`, and (b) `thinking()` emitted the expected non-empty `ThinkingDelta` sequence ending with `isFinal: true`.
- [ ] `anthropic_provider_test.dart`'s existing non-streaming tests are updated to use the streaming MockClient pattern (or removed and replaced) so all anthropic tests pass under the new SSE wire.
- [ ] `dart test packages/exploration_agent` passes locally with no skips added to existing tests.
- [ ] `dart analyze packages/exploration_agent` is clean (no new warnings or infos).

title: Plumb swift-infer request id into dogfood_turn.decision for debug-inference cross-reference
description: cx6.47 shipped per-turn dogfood records but the bitsmith dropped the 'swift_infer_request_id' field from the minimum-useful set. Without it, the dogfood JSONL can't cross-reference back to swift-infer's /v1/trace/:id endpoint — the operator has to time-correlate by hand. This bead closes the gap by plumbing the provider response id from the SSE wire through ModelDecision → TurnRecord → the dogfood_turn record.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `ModelDecision` exposes a nullable `providerRequestId` field constructed via the existing const constructor (no required parameter).
- [ ] `SwiftInferModelProvider.decide` captures `message.id` from the SSE `message_start` event and sets it on the returned `ModelDecision.providerRequestId`; the field is null when no `message_start` event is seen.
- [ ] `AnthropicModelProvider.decide` captures `message.id` from the SSE `message_start` event and sets it on the returned `ModelDecision.providerRequestId`; the field is null when no `message_start` event is seen.
- [ ] `TurnRecord` exposes a nullable `providerRequestId` field, round-trips through `toJson` / `fromJson` as the snake_case key `provider_request_id`, and the key is omitted from `toJson` when null.
- [ ] `LoopDriver._runTurn` (success path) populates `TurnRecord.providerRequestId` from `ModelDecision.providerRequestId`; the failed-turn path leaves it null.
- [ ] `_DogfoodInterceptingTrajectoryWriter.writeTurn` adds `provider_request_id` to the `dogfood_turn.decision` map when `TurnRecord.providerRequestId` is non-null, and omits the key entirely when it is null.
- [ ] No existing test in `packages/exploration_agent` regresses: `dart test` for the package passes after the change.

comments.author: fs
comments.text: Complete. Branch: fs/lenny-9am/plumb-swift-infer-request-id-into-dogfoo

title: Agent dogfood harness: writeTurn() is never called; multi-turn runs produce a trace with only header+footer, no per-turn diagnostic records
description:
## Problem

The agent dogfood harness ships `DogfoodTraceWriter.writeTurn()` at `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/dogfood/trace_writer.dart:64-90` but **`AgentDogfoodHarness.run()` never calls it**. The harness only writes `dogfood_header` (at start) and `dogfood_footer` (at end). Multi-turn runs produce a trace with exactly two lines regardless of how many turns ran or what happened in them.

Confirmed run 8 (`/tmp/lenny-dogfood/run8.jsonl`, `--max-turns 3`, MoE model):

```
{"type":"dogfood_header","goal":"turn on dark mode",...}
{"type":"dogfood_footer","outcome":"completedWithToolCall",...}
```

Two lines. The harness reported `toolCalls=2` over 3 max-turns — but the trace can't tell me:
- Which turn produced a tool call vs. which didn't.
- What prompt was sent each turn.
- What the model returned each turn (tool name, args, thinking).
- What observation the binding returned each turn.
- What the model's reasoning was across turns (carry-forward of state).

This makes multi-turn diagnosis impossible from the durable trace. The lessons cx6.44 surfaced (don't read scroll-back, use the JSONL) apply even more here.

cx6.43's spec promised "structured per-turn logging." The bitsmith implemented the writer but didn't wire it. cx6.44 added the start/end safety net. **The middle is still missing.**

## Scope

1. In `AgentDogfoodHarness.run()` (or wherever it consumes loop driver events): after each turn completes, call `DogfoodTraceWriter.writeTurn(...)` with the turn's prompt, response, tool call, executed action, observation diff (or full obs at high verbosity), and any error.

2. Wire the harness to the loop driver's per-turn event stream. The `LoopDriver` already emits `TurnEvent`s (`TurnStarted`, `TurnPromptBuilt`, `TurnResponse`, `TurnValidation`, `TurnComplete`, etc.) — `DefaultLoopHost` and `ExplorationSession` already plumb these. The harness should subscribe and accumulate per-turn state into a `DogfoodTurnRecord` it writes at `TurnComplete`.

3. Decide what each `dogfood_turn` record should contain. Minimum useful set:
   - `turn_index`
   - `prompt_summary` — last user message text (truncated to e.g. 2000 chars) so a reader can see what the model was asked.
   - `model_response` — `{tool_name, tool_args, thinking_excerpt}`.
   - `executed_action` — the qualified tool name actually dispatched (after `lookupTool` resolution).
   - `tool_result` — `{ok, value, error}` from the binding.
   - `observation_summary` — top-level keys + node count + route stack of the observation captured for the NEXT turn (or null on the last turn).
   - `failure?` — reason string when this turn failed validation, schema, timeout, etc.
   - `wall_ms` — turn wall-clock.
   - `swift_infer_request_id` — for cross-reference with the `debug-inference` skill.

4. Verbose-mode stdout: when `--verbose` is set, also `_log()` each turn with the same shape (one-line summary, not the full record).

## Out of scope

- Full prompt-as-sent vs. prompt-summary serialization. (Recording the *exact* bytes sent to swift-infer would be useful but adds significant size; defer to a sibling flag like `--full-prompts` if desired.)
- Per-event tracing (sub-turn TurnEvent records). The unit of value is the turn, not the event.
- Prompt construction quality improvements — that's `lenny-wisp-cl4`.
- Tool input schema quality — separate bead.
- Capturing thinking content fully — `thinking_excerpt` is fine; full thinking goes to the existing `model_response` thinking only if budget allows.

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/dogfood/trace_writer.dart:64-90` — `writeTurn()` already exists and takes the right shape.
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/dogfood/agent_dogfood_harness.dart` — the missing call site.
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/loop_driver/loop_driver.dart` — emits `TurnEvent`s; the harness's wiring entry point.
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/session/exploration_session.dart` and `default_loop_host.dart` — already plumb events through the session stream.
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/loop_driver/types.dart` — `TurnEvent`/`TurnComplete`/etc. types.

## Diagnosed via

Multi-turn run 8: `/tmp/lenny-dogfood/run8.jsonl` produced 2 lines (header + footer) for a 3-turn / 2-tool-call run. Run completed correctly post-cx6.46 — the harness can run multi-turn end-to-end now — but the per-turn diagnostic record the harness was built to provide isn't being written.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Build & install swift-infer; export `SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT` and `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN`.
2. Run the dogfood CLI against any live binding:
   ```bash
   dart run packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood.dart \
     --goal 'turn on dark mode' --max-turns 3 \
     --trace /tmp/lenny-dogfood/run.jsonl
   ```
3. Inspect the trace:
   ```bash
   wc -l /tmp/lenny-dogfood/run.jsonl
   jq -r .type /tmp/lenny-dogfood/run.jsonl | sort -u
   ```

**Observed:** exactly 2 lines: `dogfood_header` and `dogfood_footer`. Zero `dogfood_turn` records regardless of turn count or outcome.

**Expected:** N `dogfood_turn` records sandwiched between header and footer, one per LoopDriver turn (success or failed-turn path), each carrying the per-turn diagnostic shape `DogfoodTraceWriter.writeTurn` already accepts.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `AgentDogfoodHarness.run()` writes exactly one `dogfood_turn` JSONL line per `LoopDriver` turn (success **or** failed-turn paths), so an N-turn run produces 1 header + N `dogfood_turn` + 1 footer lines.
- [ ] Each `dogfood_turn` line is valid JSON with `type == "dogfood_turn"` and a strictly-increasing `index` starting at 0.
- [ ] Each `dogfood_turn.decision` carries `{tool, args, thinking_excerpt}` reflecting the action the LoopDriver chose (post-validation), with `thinking_excerpt` truncated to ≤ 2000 chars.
- [ ] Each `dogfood_turn.act_result` carries `{ok, value, error}` derived from `TurnRecord.executedAction['result']` for success turns and `{ok: false, error: <reason>}` for failed-turn paths (turn_timeout, invalid_action_exhausted, schema_exhausted).
- [ ] Each `dogfood_turn` carries `observation_summary` with `{keys, node_count, route_stack}` summarising the observation snapshot for that turn, OR `null` when `TurnRecord.observation` is empty (failed turn path with no observation).
- [ ] Each `dogfood_turn` carries `elapsed_ms` measured wall-clock around the turn (>= 0).
- [ ] On a failed turn, the line carries `error` with the `TurnRecord.validation['reason']` string (`turn_timeout` / `invalid_action_exhausted` / `schema_exhausted`).
- [ ] The harness still honours `DogfoodTraceWriter`'s `header → turns* → footer` ordering invariant (no `StateError` if turns precede the header — the harness writes header before `driver.runSession()` starts).
- [ ] Verbose mode (`verbose: true`) emits one `[dogfood] turn <index> tool=<name> ok=<bool> ms=<ms>` line per turn via the harness's `_log` callback, in addition to the JSONL record.
- [ ] The existing budgetExceeded / typedException / harnessError tests still pass — adding turn records does NOT alter the outcome classification or the footer's `exception` / `harness_error` fields.
- [ ] `DogfoodTraceWriter.writeTurn`'s positional signature (`index`, `prompt`, `decision`, `actResult`, `elapsedMs`, `error`) is preserved; no new required parameters. (`swift_infer_request_id` capture is deferred to a follow-up bead since `SwiftInferModelProvider` does not currently surface a request id.)

comments.author: fs
comments.text: Complete. Branch: fs/lenny-cx6.47/agent-dogfood-harness-writeturn-is-never
