comments.text: marshal: APPROVED. Dispatched and completed. Pending review.

title: exploration_agent: agent dogfood harness — CLI + e2e test sharing one library, drives real swift-infer for prompt tuning and regression gating
description:
## Problem

The lenny test suite lacks a real e2e for the agent ↔ inference-server contract. `lenny-cx6.41`'s `provider_loop_integration_test.dart` looks like e2e in name but mocks `http.Client` via `MockClient.streaming` — swift-infer-server is never exercised, the model never runs. Today's bug (qwen3.6-27b returning bare `navigate` from a tool_use) was discovered by *manual* dogfood through the DevTools UI loop, not by a test. Encoded into the integration test only after the fact.

But this bead is not just "add the missing test." It's a **harness** — a tunable tool for stressing the agent against a live model server AND tuning prompts. Single library + two entry points (CLI for ad-hoc tuning; test for CI gate) means the same code closes both the e2e gap and the prompt-engineering iteration loop.

The user explicitly framed: *"the whole point of this harness is to stress the client against the server. we will not use it just to prove the client out but to tune the prompts as well."*

## Goal

Build `packages/exploration_agent/{lib/src/dogfood,tool,test/e2e}/` — a shared harness library with a CLI entry point and a test entry point.

### Library — `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/dogfood/`

Private (not exported via `lib/exploration_agent.dart`) until the harness pattern proves itself. Holds:

- `AgentDogfoodHarness` class. Constructs a real `SwiftInferModelProvider` against a configurable `endpoint` and `token`, wires a real `ExplorationSession`, drives N loop iterations against a `_BindingVmServiceFake` (reused from cvl.4 — the binding-side fake is fine because the bug class this harness exists to catch lives on the model side).
- Observation fixture loader: reads a JSON file matching the binding's `core.get_stable_observation` response shape and returns it as the canned observation for the harness's binding fake.
- Trace writer: every run records its prompts / responses / tool calls / outcomes into a JSONL file (default `/tmp/agent-dogfood-<unix-ts>.jsonl`) so two runs after a prompt edit can be diffed.
- Verbose logger: structured `print` of each turn (prompt sent, SSE chunks received, tool call attempted, binding fake response, observation diff). On in CLI mode by default; off in test mode unless test fails.

### CLI — `packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood.dart`

Thin shim over the library. Args:

- `--goal "<string>"` (required): the goal text inserted into the system prompt.
- `--tools <comma-separated>` (required): qualified tool names. Validates each contains a `.` and is registered in the binding fake.
- `--endpoint <url>` (required, falls back to `SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT` env var).
- `--token <string>` (required, falls back to `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN` env var).
- `--model <id>` (default `qwen3.6-27b`): model id in the request body.
- `--observation-fixture <path>` (optional): path to a JSON file with a canned `Observation`. Without it, the binding fake returns a minimal empty observation (and the harness prints a warning that the model won't have much to work with).
- `--max-turns N` (default 3): hard cap on loop iterations.
- `--max-turn-budget-ms M` (default 30000): wall-clock budget per turn; harness aborts a turn that exceeds it. Total run budget is `N * M` plus a small slack.
- `--verbose` (default true in CLI): structured per-turn logging.
- `--trace-out <path>` (optional, default `/tmp/agent-dogfood-<unix-ts>.jsonl`): where to write the JSONL trace.

Exit codes:
- 0: loop completed with at least one tool call dispatched into the binding fake.
- 1: typed agent exception (`SchemaRejection`, `ArgumentError`, `BindingNotInitializedError`, ...). Exception type + `.toString()` printed.
- 2: budget exceeded (turn budget or total turns).
- 3: harness misconfiguration (missing required arg, unreadable fixture, ...).

### Test — `packages/exploration_agent/test/e2e/dogfood_e2e_test.dart`

Same library, test mode. Reads `SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT` + `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN` from `Platform.environment`; **skips with a clear message** if either is unset, so default `flutter test` runs are unaffected.

Initial canonical scenarios (small, will grow):

1. **happyPathDarkMode**: goal "turn on dark mode" on a fixture login screen with `[router.navigate, core.tap]` tools. Asserts the loop completes with at least one tool dispatched (does NOT assert *which* — the model can reasonably choose `navigate` or `tap`).
2. **unknownToolNameSurvives**: goal that historically caused qwen3.6-27b to drop the namespace prefix; asserts loop completes OR surfaces `SchemaRejection` (after cx6.40, the latter is the correct outcome — regression-lock for the bug we just fixed).
3. **emptyObservationDoesNotCrash**: drive a one-turn loop with no observation fixture; assert no unhandled exception.

Each scenario uses the harness library; test asserts on the typed outcome. On failure, the test prints the run's trace file path and the swift-infer `request_id` (from the SSE response headers) for `debug-inference` cross-reference.

## Stress dimensions (v1)

- **Prompt tuning**: edit the system prompt template / tool descriptions in agent source → rerun harness → diff traces. No code change needed in the harness for this — the harness exercises whatever prompt assembler the agent currently uses.
- **Tool-set matrix**: `--tools` flag varies the action surface.
- **Observation fixtures**: `--observation-fixture` replays captured / synthesized UIs.
- **Goal matrix**: `--goal` is free-form; scripting multiple runs is just a shell loop calling the CLI.

Out of scope for v1 (file later if needed):
- `--inject-malformed-sse` / `--inject-rate-limit` failure injection (the canned-fixture integration test cx6.41 already covers basic error paths).
- `--replay-trace <request_id>` to pull a past swift-infer trace and rerun.
- An LLM-graded "did the agent solve the goal?" judge.
- Multi-provider `--provider {anthropic|openai}` support (sibling bead later).
- Anthropic / OpenAI cloud provider e2e (cost-bearing).
- Nightly CI orchestration (file later as separate operations bead).

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_agent/test/integration/provider_loop_integration_test.dart` — structural template for the harness library; the fixture/scenario pattern is reusable. Drop the `MockClient` and canned SSE strings.
- `packages/exploration_flutter/test/binding_e2e_integration_test.dart` — `_BindingVmServiceFake` pattern. Reuse, don't reimplement. May warrant hoisting into a shared test-support library at this point (third call site) — architect's call.
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_provider.dart` — real provider wire.
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/loop_driver/` — the prompt assembler whose behavior the harness exercises and the user wants to tune.
- swift-infer admin API (`debug-inference` skill in `.claude/skills/`) — for trace cross-reference on failure.

## Reported by

User feedback 2026-05-15 calling out that `lenny-cx6.41` is an integration test, not e2e — followed by a reframe: this isn't just a test, it's a prompt-tuning harness. Companion to `lenny-cx6.42` (rename for honesty) and follows the test-taxonomy-discipline memory.

## Design decisions answered in discovery

- **Where**: `tool/` + `test/e2e/` + private library in `packages/exploration_agent/`. (Spinning off a new `exploration_dogfood` package was considered and rejected as premature.)
- **Provider**: swift-infer only for v1. Anthropic/OpenAI multi-provider is a sibling bead later.
- **Stress flags v1**: `--observation-fixture`, `--max-turns`, `--max-turn-budget-ms`. Failure-injection and trace-replay deferred.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Private library exists at `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/dogfood/` with an `AgentDogfoodHarness` class; nothing from this subtree is re-exported by `packages/exploration_agent/lib/exploration_agent.dart`.
- [ ] `AgentDogfoodHarness.run()` returns a typed `DogfoodRunResult` carrying `outcome` (enum: `completedWithToolCall`, `completedNoToolCall`, `typedException`, `budgetExceeded`), `exception` (Object? — only set when `outcome == typedException`), `tracePath` (String), `toolCallCount` (int), and `turnCount` (int).
- [ ] `AgentDogfoodHarness` takes a caller-supplied `VmService` (the harness library itself does NOT import `exploration_flutter` / `flutter_test` — Flutter-binding wiring is the caller's responsibility) and a caller-supplied `TrajectorySink`.
- [ ] `ObservationFixture.loadFromFile(String path)` reads a JSON file matching the `core.get_stable_observation` response shape and returns a typed value the harness installs into the binding fake; missing file throws `DogfoodConfigError` (subclass of `ArgumentError`).
- [ ] Per-turn budget: the harness aborts a turn whose wall-clock duration exceeds `maxTurnBudgetMs` and surfaces `outcome == budgetExceeded`; total run budget is `maxTurns * maxTurnBudgetMs + 5000ms` slack.
- [ ] Verbose logger: when `verbose == true`, the harness writes structured per-turn lines to a caller-supplied `void Function(String) log` sink (prompt assembled, tool descriptors offered, raw provider decision, action validated, binding-fake response, turn elapsed ms). When `verbose == false`, the sink is only called on terminal failure.
- [ ] Trace writer: every run writes one JSONL line per turn plus a header and footer to the supplied `TrajectorySink`. Header includes goal, model id, tool descriptors, and harness timestamp; footer includes `outcome` and `exception.toString()` when applicable.
- [ ] CLI entrypoint exists at `packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood.dart`. `dart run packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood.dart --help` prints usage covering every flag listed in the bead description and exits 0.
- [ ] CLI maps `--goal`, `--tools`, `--endpoint`, `--token`, `--model`, `--observation-fixture`, `--max-turns`, `--max-turn-budget-ms`, `--verbose`, `--trace-out`. `--endpoint` and `--token` fall back to `SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT` and `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN`; missing values exit 3 with a message naming the missing flag/env var.
- [ ] CLI validates each `--tools` entry contains a `.` separator and the namespace is registered with the in-process binding; rejects with exit 3 otherwise.
- [ ] CLI exit codes are exactly: 0 = `completedWithToolCall`, 1 = `typedException` (prints exception type + `.toString()` to stderr), 2 = `budgetExceeded`, 3 = configuration error (caught before harness runs).
- [ ] `packages/exploration_agent/test/e2e/dogfood_e2e_test.dart` skips every test with `skip: 'SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT and SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN must be set; run packages/exploration_agent/tool/agent_dogfood.dart for ad-hoc runs'` (single message, names both env vars and the CLI alternative) when either env var is missing. Default `flutter test packages/exploration_agent/` does NOT hang or attempt network IO.
- [ ] When both env vars ARE set, three test cases run: `happyPathDarkMode`, `unknownToolNameSurvives`, `emptyObservationDoesNotCrash`. Each asserts on `DogfoodRunResult.outcome` from the shared harness library — no MockClient, no canned SSE, real `SwiftInferModelProvider` with real `http.Client`.
- [ ] `happyPathDarkMode` asserts `outcome in {completedWithToolCall, completedNoToolCall, typedException}` AND `toolCallCount >= 0` AND (on failure) prints `tracePath` and the `request_id` extracted from the swift-infer SSE response headers.
- [ ] `unknownToolNameSurvives` asserts `outcome in {completedWithToolCall, completedNoToolCall, typedException}` — when `typedException`, requires it `isA<SchemaRejection>()` (regression-lock for cx6.40).
- [ ] `emptyObservationDoesNotCrash` runs with `maxTurns = 1` and no observation fixture; asserts the harness does not throw an unhandled exception (any `outcome` value is acceptable as long as the future completes).
- [ ] Shared `_BindingVmServiceFake` clone lives at `packages/exploration_agent/test/_support/binding_vm_service_fake.dart`, imported by BOTH `test/e2e/dogfood_e2e_test.dart` and `tool/agent_dogfood.dart`. A header comment names the three call sites (cvl.4, cx6.41, cx6.43) and links the deferred-hoist sibling bead by id.
- [ ] A sibling bead is filed before this bead's spec submits — title exactly: `test_support: hoist _BindingVmServiceFake to packages/exploration_flutter/test_support/ (4 call sites)`. The bead id is recorded in the comment header of `test/_support/binding_vm_service_fake.dart`.
- [ ] `flutter test packages/exploration_agent/test/e2e/` exits 0 with three skipped tests when env vars are unset.

title: exploration_agent: pure-Dart e2e provider integration test — drive ExplorationSession against a fake inference HTTP backend, assert the loop survives canned model misbehavior
description:
## Problem

Today the only way to exercise the full agent loop (`ExplorationSession.run` → provider → binding → loop driver → tool call → next observation) end-to-end is the DevTools dogfood path: rebuild the extension, restart `sample_app`, click Start in the panel, observe a minified JS exception when something breaks. The cycle is opaque (no source maps), slow (Flutter build + restart), and the failure signal is a stack of mangled symbols.

cvl.4 closed the *binding-side* gap with `binding_e2e_integration_test.dart`. The *provider-side* gap remains: there is no pure-Dart test that drives `ExplorationSession` against the actual `SwiftInferProvider` / `AnthropicProvider` / `OpenAIProvider` HTTP code paths with realistic SSE responses. Every contract bug between the agent and a model server (today: a model that drops the namespace prefix from a `tool_use` name; tomorrow: any malformed delta, partial JSON, missing `tool_use` block, mid-stream disconnect, etc.) currently has to be reproduced through the DevTools panel.

## Goal

Add `packages/exploration_agent/test/integration/provider_loop_integration_test.dart` (or similar) that:

1. Stands up a **fake `http.Client`** (using `package:http/testing.dart` `MockClient` or a small in-test class) that:
   - On `POST /v1/messages` (SSE stream), replays a canned sequence of `event: ` / `data: {...}` lines.
   - Responses are parameterized fixtures (one fixture per scenario — happy path, model emits unknown tool, malformed JSON line, no `tool_use` block, mid-stream `[DONE]`, etc.).
2. Wires that client into a real `SwiftInferProvider` (and parallel cases for Anthropic/OpenAI providers — same SSE shape, different headers).
3. Wires the provider into a real `ExplorationSession` whose `VmServiceClient` is the cvl.4-style binding fake.
4. Calls `session.start(goal, config)` then drives one loop iteration; asserts the typed outcome:
   - Happy path: tool was dispatched to the binding, observation returned, no exceptions.
   - Unknown tool name: `SchemaRejection` thrown (after lenny-cx6.40 ships). Pre-cx6.40 the assertion can be `ArgumentError` to lock the current bug, then flipped to `SchemaRejection` in cx6.40's validation step.
   - Malformed SSE line: `FormatException` surfaces with the offending line in the message.
   - No `tool_use`: `SchemaRejection(validationError: "no tool_use block in response")`.

## Why this matters

- Reproduces every model-misbehavior class **in seconds**, with **readable Dart stack frames**, with no browser.
- Becomes the validation harness for cx6.40 (catch unknown tool names) and any future provider-side fix.
- Forces every provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, swift-infer) to share the same wire-shape expectations, surfacing drift early.
- Mirrors cvl.4 for the binding side; together they cover the agent's entire wire surface (binding ↑ + inference server ↓).

## Scope (out of)

- The fixes themselves (cx6.40 is a separate bead).
- Real swift-infer/Anthropic/OpenAI traffic. The fixtures are canned; the test does not depend on a live server.
- Streaming-token UI behavior. This is loop-correctness, not UX.
- The recorded-trace replay pattern (record a real swift-infer response, then replay it in a test). Nice future enhancement; not in this bead.

## Diagnosed via

This bead is the meta-fix for the dogfood-loop frustration surfaced 2026-05-15 while triaging `swift-infer-msg_333DE0C006B` (qwen3.6-27b returned `"navigate"` without the `router_` prefix; the agent silently fell through and crashed cvl.3's qualified-name guard). The diagnosis itself required `debug-inference` + reading server-side trace bodies, which would have been one Dart `expect` if this test existed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New test file `packages/exploration_agent/test/integration/provider_loop_integration_test.dart` exists and runs under `dart test` from the package root.
- [ ] Test harness exposes a `SseFixture` (or equivalent) factory that builds canned SSE bodies from a named scenario; each scenario produces a `MockClient` via `package:http/testing.dart` (no hand-rolled `http.Client` subclasses).
- [ ] Test harness reuses `_BindingVmServiceFake` pattern from `packages/exploration_flutter/test/binding_e2e_integration_test.dart` to back `ExplorationSession.fromVmService` with a real `ExplorationBinding` + test plugin contributing one routable tool under namespace `router` (tool `navigate`, single string arg `route_name`).
- [ ] Scenario `happyPathSwiftInfer`: provider emits `tool_use{name: "router_navigate", input: {route_name: "settings"}}`; `provider.decide(...)` → `session.act(name: 'router.navigate', args: ...)` returns `{ok: true, ...}`; the binding-side test plugin records exactly one invocation with `route_name == 'settings'`.
- [ ] Scenario `unknownToolBareNavigate` (regression for swift-infer trace `msg_333DE0C006B`): provider emits `tool_use{name: "navigate", input: {route_name: "settings"}}`; `provider.decide(...)` returns a `ModelDecision` whose `action.tool == "navigate"` (bare); `session.act(...)` throws `ArgumentError` from the cvl.3 qualified-name guard. The expectation has a `// TODO(cx6.40): flip to throwsA(isA<SchemaRejection>()) once tool_helpers fails closed.` comment immediately above it.
- [ ] Scenario `malformedSseLine`: SSE stream contains one `data: {not valid json` line followed by otherwise-valid events; `provider.decide(...)` throws `FormatException` whose `.message` contains the offending fragment.
- [ ] Scenario `noToolUseBlock`: SSE stream contains only `text_delta` events and `message_stop` (no `content_block_start` of type `tool_use`); `provider.decide(...)` throws `SchemaRejection` with `validationError == 'no tool_use block in response'`.
- [ ] Scenario `midStreamDone`: SSE stream emits `content_block_start` then `data: [DONE]` before any `input_json_delta`; `provider.decide(...)` throws `SchemaRejection` (no tool_use args ever materialise → bubble through the same no-args path; assertion locks current behavior).
- [ ] The same `MockClient` factory + binding fake is parametrised over at least one frontier provider (`SwiftInferModelProvider`); the file documents (in a top-level dartdoc comment) that the same fixtures are reusable for `AnthropicProvider` / `OpenAIProvider` callers in cx6.40's validation step.
- [ ] All scenario assertions go through a single helper `expectScenario(ProviderUnderTest p, Scenario s, Matcher outcome)` (or similar) so cx6.40's validation step can add the post-fix `SchemaRejection` assertion in one line.
- [ ] `dart analyze --fatal-infos packages/exploration_agent` passes with the new file.
- [ ] `dart test packages/exploration_agent/test/integration/provider_loop_integration_test.dart` passes with all scenarios.

title: Frontier providers (anthropic/openai/swift-infer): catch unknown tool names from the model and emit structured SchemaRejection instead of falling through to executeAction
description:
## Problem

The `frontier/tool_helpers.dart::decodeToolName` / `lookupTool` flow currently swallows model misbehavior: when the model emits a `tool_use` block with a `name` that does not encode to any tool offered in `prompt.tools`, both `anthropic_provider.dart:115` and `swift_infer_provider.dart:170` fall through to the raw wire name (`?? wireName`). After lenny-cvl.3, `VmServiceClient.executeAction` requires a qualified `<namespace>.<tool>` and throws `ArgumentError` on unqualified names — so the loop crashes with an unhandled `ArgumentError` that the DevTools panel surfaces as a minified JS exception.

## Steps to Reproduce

Confirmed 2026-05-15 from dogfood (swift-infer trace `msg_333DE0C006B`, model `qwen3.6-27b`, mlx-local, 295 completion tokens, no truncation):

1. Launch a Flutter app that registers a routing plugin under namespace `router` exposing a `navigate` tool (so the tool manifest sent to the model includes the encoded wire name `router_navigate`).
2. Start the exploration agent against swift-infer with the qwen3.6-27b model and goal `"turn on dark mode"`. The first observation is a login-screen.
3. Observe the model's reply: `tool_calls: [{name: "navigate", arguments: "{\"route_name\":\"settings\"}"}]` — bare `"navigate"`, dropping the `router_` namespace prefix.
4. Inside `SwiftInferModelProvider.decide`, `lookupTool(prompt.tools, "navigate")` returns null (only the encoded forms `router_navigate`, `core_tap`, etc. are registered). The `?? wireName` fallback at line 170 lets the bare `"navigate"` propagate as the `dottedName`.
5. `ActionSchema.validate` is permissive enough (in the production tools list at the time of the trace) to let the envelope through.
6. `ExplorationSession.act` → `VmServiceClient.executeAction("navigate", {...})` → `ArgumentError` from lenny-cvl.3's qualified-name guard.
7. The loop driver does not catch `ArgumentError`; the unhandled future error propagates; the DevTools panel surfaces a minified `main.dart.js:6497 Uncaught Error` with `Object.h / VC.Mk / a8v.$0 / a8u.{ad_,vi,Mk}` frames.

Trace evidence: `debug-inference` skill, `GET /v1/trace/msg_333DE0C006B` against swift-infer at `127.0.0.1:8080`.

## Approach (recommendation — confirm/refine in spec)

The cvl.3 guard is correct (a bare `"navigate"` IS unroutable). The fix is upstream:

1. In each frontier provider (anthropic, swift_infer, openai), do not silently fall through when `lookupTool` returns null. **Fail closed** at `anthropic_provider.dart:115`, `swift_infer_provider.dart:170`, `openai_parse.dart:75`.
2. Throw `SchemaRejection(validationError: "model emitted unknown tool: <wireName>; available: [router_navigate, core_tap, ...]", rawOutput: <JSON-encoded tool_use payload>)`. The available-tools list is the diagnostic key — both the loop driver's retry policy and the panel banner need to know what *was* offered.
3. Centralise the message format in a new `unknownToolRejection` helper in `frontier/tool_helpers.dart` so all three providers share one canonical shape.

Per the cvl.4 e2e lesson: lenny-cx6.41's integration harness already has an `unknownToolBareNavigate` scenario gated by a `TODO(cx6.40)` marker. Flipping that scenario's matcher from `throwsArgumentError` to `throwsA(isA<SchemaRejection>())` is the regression-stopper validation for this bead.

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/frontier/tool_helpers.dart` (the `decodeToolName` / `lookupTool` helpers — the silent-fallthrough site).
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/anthropic/anthropic_provider.dart:113-115` (Anthropic decode site).
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_provider.dart:165-170` (swift-infer decode site).
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/openai/openai_parse.dart:67-81` (OpenAI decode site — already throws `SchemaRejection`; normalise the shape).
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/vm_service_client.dart` (cvl.3 guard that fires today — must NOT change; the guard is correct).
- `packages/exploration_agent/test/integration/provider_loop_integration_test.dart` (cx6.41 harness; `unknownToolBareNavigate` scenario carries the flip TODO).
- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/prompt_tab_mount.dart` (panel-side catch — out of scope for this bead; see follow-up note).

## Out of scope

- Touching the cvl.3 qualified-name guard. It is correct.
- Fixing the model's tool-name behavior (qwen3.6-27b drops namespace prefixes intermittently — that is the model's training; we cannot fix it from here, only handle it).
- Re-prompting / self-healing logic. This bead surfaces and types the error; retry/heal is a sibling on lenny-cx6.18.
- Panel-side catch in `prompt_tab_mount.dart` (banner for `SchemaRejection`). The pure provider fix is independently regression-stopping; the banner is a UX leg with its own placement/dismissal decisions — filed as a follow-up.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `lookupTool(tools, wireName) == null` in `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/anthropic/anthropic_provider.dart` (around line 115) throws `SchemaRejection` instead of falling through to `?? wireName`; the `validationError` matches exactly `model emitted unknown tool: <wireName>; available: [<comma-separated encoded names>]`; the `rawOutput` is the JSON-encoded `tool_use` block (`{"name": "<wireName>", "input": <args map>}`).
- [ ] Same fail-closed behaviour applied to `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_provider.dart` (around line 170), with the same `validationError` template and the same `rawOutput` shape.
- [ ] `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/openai/openai_parse.dart` already throws `SchemaRejection` for unknown tools (line 76-81); its `validationError` is normalised to the same `model emitted unknown tool: <name>; available: [<comma-separated dotted names>]` template, and `rawOutput` switches to a JSON-encoded `{name, arguments: <args map>}` payload so all three providers emit a structurally identical SchemaRejection on this code path.
- [ ] `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/frontier/tool_helpers.dart` exposes a single shared helper `unknownToolRejection(String wireName, List<ToolDescriptor> tools, {required Map<String, Object?> rawPayload})` that returns the formatted `SchemaRejection`; all three providers call it (no string template duplicated across providers).
- [ ] `decodeToolName(wire, tools)` in `frontier/tool_helpers.dart` is removed (its only use was the silent-fallback site that this bead closes); `lookupTool` is retained.
- [ ] Anthropic unit test `'unknown tool wire name → SchemaRejection (unknown tool, available list)'` added to `packages/exploration_agent/test/provider/anthropic/anthropic_provider_test.dart`: feeds a `tool_use{name: 'navigate'}` body against tools `[core.tap, router.navigate]`, asserts a `SchemaRejection` whose `validationError` equals `model emitted unknown tool: navigate; available: [core_tap, router_navigate]` and whose `rawOutput` parses back to `{name: 'navigate', input: <args>}`.
- [ ] Swift-infer unit test `'unknown tool wire name → SchemaRejection (unknown tool, available list)'` added to `packages/exploration_agent/test/provider/swift_infer/swift_infer_provider_test.dart` with the same assertions against the swift-infer SSE wire.
- [ ] OpenAI unit test `'unknown tool name → SchemaRejection (unknown tool, available list)'` added to `packages/exploration_agent/test/provider/openai/openai_provider_test.dart` with the equivalent assertions over the OpenAI chat-completions wire (dotted available list).
- [ ] Integration test `unknownToolBareNavigate` in `packages/exploration_agent/test/integration/provider_loop_integration_test.dart` is updated: the scenario drops the `_bareNavigateDescriptor` override (i.e. uses the default `[router.navigate]` tools), its matcher flips from `throwsArgumentError` to `throwsA(isA<SchemaRejection>().having((e) => e.validationError, 'validationError', startsWith('model emitted unknown tool: navigate')))`, and both `TODO(cx6.40)` markers (the dartdoc one and the inline one) are removed.
- [ ] The dartdoc table at the top of `provider_loop_integration_test.dart` updates the `unknownToolBareNavigate` row's `locks` column from referencing `ArgumentError` / `TODO(cx6.40)` to describing the post-fix `SchemaRejection` outcome; the surrounding "Today only `unknownToolBareNavigate` asserts a present-tense bug" paragraph is rewritten to reflect that all five scenarios are now forward-looking guards.
- [ ] `_bareNavigateDescriptor` is deleted from the integration test (no remaining references after the flip).
- [ ] No production code references `?? wireName` in any of the three providers after this bead lands (`grep -F '?? wireName' packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/provider/` returns empty).
- [ ] `dart analyze --fatal-infos packages/exploration_agent` is clean.
- [ ] `flutter test packages/exploration_agent` is green (the full agent suite, not just the three new unit tests and the flipped integration scenario).

title: ModelCatalog: surface CORS / network / 4xx failures with a panel-visible error state instead of a silent dropdown
description:
## Problem

When `ModelCatalog.fetch()` fails (network error, CORS preflight rejection, 401/403/404/5xx, malformed response), the model picker dropdown in the panel silently shows no models or stays in a loading state. The actual failure surfaces only in the **browser JavaScript console**, which DevTools embedded panels do not expose to the user — the user sees a panel that looks broken.

Concrete recent example (dogfood session, 2026-05-15): swift-infer-server was missing CORS `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` and `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` for the panel's `/v1/models` request. The panel showed no model options, no error, no hint. The only signal was a JS console message visible only by pressing Cmd-Option-J in Chrome and ignoring three layers of unrelated DevTools-host warnings — three iterations of screenshot triage to find it. Filed against the server as `swift-infer-74z`; this bead is the lenny-side defensive fix so the next misconfigured server is debuggable without leaving the panel.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure the Prompt panel with any provider whose `/v1/models` endpoint is unreachable due to CORS (e.g. swift-infer running locally without `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`), unreachable due to network failure (server down), or returning a 4xx (bad API key / 401 / 403).
2. Open the exploration_devtools DevTools panel and select that provider.
3. Observe the model picker dropdown.

Expected: a visible, actionable error banner explaining what failed and what to do (and, for swift-infer, a one-tap "use the configured fallback model" affordance).

Actual: the dropdown is empty / shows the loading spinner / shows a `Model list error: ClientException: Failed to fetch, uri=...` opaque string — the user has no signal that CORS is the likely cause and no recovery path inside the panel.

## Approach (recommendation — confirm/refine in spec)

In the model-picker UI widget (the consumer of `ModelCatalog.fetch()` — see `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/`), distinguish three observable states:
1. **loading** (existing).
2. **success(models)** (existing).
3. **error(detail)** — new: render a banner / inline error with:
   - one-line summary (e.g. `Could not load models from <baseUrl>/v1/models — <reason>`)
   - the failure category (CORS, network, HTTP <code>, malformed response)
   - a hint pointing to the README's CORS section (`packages/exploration_devtools/README.md:88-99`) when the failure looks CORS-shaped
   - the model fallback id from `SwiftInferUiConfig.defaultModelId` (which the gateway / non-`/v1/models` servers already use) so the user can press Start with the fallback even without a working catalog

`ModelCatalog.fetch()` already throws `ModelCatalogError` (see `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/model_catalog.dart:41, ~70-100`); the bead is mostly UI plumbing for the existing typed error.

### What CORS looks like from Dart in the browser

A CORS preflight rejection on a `package:http` `BrowserClient` request typically surfaces as a `ClientException` with message `"Failed to fetch"` (Chrome) / `"TypeError: NetworkError when attempting to fetch resource"` (Firefox) — **the same shape as plain offline / DNS / connection-refused**. The browser deliberately hides the CORS-specific information from the JS caller. So the panel cannot perfectly diagnose CORS vs network; what it CAN do is:
- Show the inability to reach `<baseUrl>/v1/models` clearly.
- Note that the most common cause for `localhost` / `127.0.0.1` targets is missing CORS headers.
- Link to the README so the user can verify.

The detection heuristic is fuzzy by necessity; the visibility is the win.

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/model_catalog.dart` (`ModelCatalogError` line 41, `fetch` flow lines 81-101, `_fetchSwiftInfer` and siblings lines 109-201).
- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/prompt_panel.dart` lines 201-213 — the existing error banner widget.
- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/prompt_tab_mount.dart` lines 86-106 — the catch site that populates `ModelCatalogState.error`.
- `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md` lines 87-104 — the existing CORS docs to link from the error UI.
- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/provider_config.dart` line 38 — `defaultModelId` field (named `defaultModelId`, not `fallbackModelId`) reused as the swift-infer fallback id.

## Out of scope

- Fixing swift-infer-server (tracked in `swift-infer-74z`).
- Adding new diagnostic endpoints in lenny.
- Auto-retry / circuit-breaker logic (separate concern).

## Reported by

Surfaced from a lenny dogfood session 2026-05-15 after the agent↔binding wire contract was reconciled (lenny-cvl epic). The wire reconciliation got the panel rendering; the next failure mode (model picker against a CORS-incomplete server) was silent and required reading minified JS console output across two screenshots and a /loop iteration to diagnose.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `ModelCatalogError` carries a `kind: ModelCatalogErrorKind` field with values `httpError`, `networkOrCors`, `malformedResponse`.
- [ ] `ModelCatalogError` carries a nullable `targetUrl: Uri?` field populated with the `/v1/models` URI that failed.
- [ ] `ModelCatalogError.toString()` for `networkOrCors` mentions both "CORS" and the README hint; for `httpError` includes the status code and target URL; for `malformedResponse` mentions the response did not match the `/v1/models` shape.
- [ ] `ModelCatalog._fetchAnthropic` classifies `http.ClientException` from `_client.get` as `ModelCatalogError(kind: networkOrCors, targetUrl: <models uri>)`.
- [ ] `ModelCatalog._fetchOpenAi` classifies `http.ClientException` from `_client.get` as `ModelCatalogError(kind: networkOrCors, targetUrl: <models uri>)`.
- [ ] `ModelCatalog._fetchAnthropic` and `_fetchOpenAi` classify a 4xx/5xx response as `ModelCatalogError(kind: httpError, statusCode: <code>, targetUrl: <models uri>)`.
- [ ] `ModelCatalog._fetchAnthropic` and `_fetchOpenAi` classify `FormatException` from JSON decoding as `ModelCatalogError(kind: malformedResponse, targetUrl: <models uri>)`.
- [ ] `ModelCatalog._fetchSwiftInfer` continues to return `_swiftInferFallback(cfg)` on 404 / 5xx / network failure (existing behavior preserved — keeps a usable dropdown).
- [ ] `ModelCatalog._fetchSwiftInfer` classifies a 4xx response other than 404 (e.g. 401/403) as `ModelCatalogError(kind: httpError, statusCode: <code>, targetUrl: <models uri>)` rather than falling back silently.
- [ ] `PromptPanel`'s error banner (`Key('prompt.modelsError')`) renders an `Icons.error_outline` icon plus the `state.error.toString()` text.
- [ ] When `state.config` is a `SwiftInferUiConfig` and `state.error != null`, the banner renders a tappable child with `Key('prompt.modelsError.useFallback')` whose label is `Use fallback model: <defaultModelId>`.
- [ ] Tapping the `prompt.modelsError.useFallback` widget invokes `PromptPanel.onUseFallback?.call(<defaultModelId>)`.
- [ ] `PromptTabMount` passes an `onUseFallback` callback to `PromptPanel` that installs a synthetic single-entry `ModelCatalogState(models: [ResolvedModel(id: <id>, label: <id>, usingFallback: true)], loading: false, error: null, config: <current cfg>)` so the dropdown becomes selectable.
- [ ] When `state.config` is `AnthropicUiConfig` or `OpenAiUiConfig`, the `prompt.modelsError.useFallback` widget is NOT rendered.
- [ ] `flutter test packages/exploration_devtools` passes (existing + new tests).
- [ ] `dart analyze packages/exploration_devtools` reports no new issues.
notes:
## Graph engagement

- **Parent epic** `lenny-cx6` (Flutter Exploration Agent — DevTools panel surface).
- **Depends on** `lenny-0wd` (the closed bead that introduced `ModelCatalog` and the existing error banner at `prompt_panel.dart:201-213`). This bead extends 0wd's typed error machinery and reuses its banner widget key — no parallel error type, no second banner.
- **Sibling scope-carving**:
  - `lenny-6rh` (open, P3 — persist last-used config across reloads). Touches `provider_config.dart` / `prompt_tab_mount.dart`. **No file overlap** with this bead's edits: 6rh changes the persistence layer (`ProviderConfigStore`-adjacent code), q05 changes error classification + banner + an `onUseFallback` callback. Same files at the function/widget level but disjoint edit regions. If 6rh lands first this bead rebases trivially; if this bead lands first 6rh does the same.
- **Out-of-graph reference**: `swift-infer-74z` (in a different project) is the swift-infer-side CORS fix. q05 is the lenny-side defensive UX so the next misconfigured server (any server, not just swift-infer) is debuggable without leaving the panel.
- **No ADRs / recorded decision beads cover this surface** — verified via `ls docs/adrs/` (no such dir) and `bd list --status all` filtered to `type=decision` (zero). The architectural choices in this spec (typed error kind, honest CORS ambiguity, no auto-OPTIONS probe, fallback affordance only for swift-infer where `defaultModelId` is documented) are made in the spec itself rather than downstream of a recorded decision.

title: exploration_agent: end-to-end integration test — boot a real-extension binding fake and drive handshake → observation → tool call through VmServiceClient
description:
Child of lenny-cvl. Depends on the handshake, observation, and action-routing children — it exercises all three reconciled surfaces.

## Problem

The agent/panel client and the binding were built against incompatible wire contracts and never integration-tested end to end — that gap is what let lenny-cvl ship. Close it with a test that registers the *real* extension names and response shapes (a binding fake, or the real `ExplorationBinding` under flutter_test) and drives handshake → observation → one tool call through `VmServiceClient` / `ExplorationSession`.

## Scope

A new test (in `packages/exploration_agent/test/` for the pure-Dart `VmServiceClient` path, or `packages/exploration_flutter/test/` if the real `ExplorationBinding` is used) that:
1. Stands up a binding (or fake) registering: `ext.flutter.exploration.core.handshake` returning the real payload incl. the `plugins` array; `ext.flutter.exploration.core.get_stable_observation`; at least one tool extension (`ext.flutter.exploration.core.<tool>` and/or `ext.flutter.exploration.<ns>.<tool>`).
2. Runs handshake → asserts the manifest is parsed (incl. `core` namespace + plugin namespaces).
3. Runs an observation → asserts the typed `Observation` deserializes.
4. Runs one tool call → asserts the `{ok, value, error}` result round-trips.

Out of scope: production code changes (those are the sibling beads).

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_agent/test/vm_service_client_test.dart`, `packages/exploration_agent/test/session_test.dart` (existing fakes to extend)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/binding/exploration_binding.dart` (`invokeServiceExtension` test helper, `_registerCoreExtensions`)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/test/binding_handshake_test.dart` (real-binding test patterns)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The `{ok, value, error[, trace]}` envelope shape that `CorePlugin.initialize` currently builds inline (`core_plugin.dart:115-134`) lives in exactly one place — a shared function `dispatchToolToEnvelope(ExplorationTool, Map<String, Object?>) -> Future<String>` — and `CorePlugin.initialize` calls it instead of duplicating the shape.
- [ ] `ExplorationBinding` exposes a `@visibleForTesting Future<String> invokePluginTool(String method, Map<String, String> params)` that resolves the tool via `pluginRegistry.mergedTools()['<ns>.<tool>']`, calls `tool.call(decodedArgs)`, and returns the JSON body produced by `dispatchToolToEnvelope` — wrapping any unexpected throw into the same `dispatch_failed` envelope `CorePlugin.initialize` produces.
- [ ] `invokePluginTool` throws `ArgumentError` when the method name does not start with `ext.flutter.exploration.`, is missing the `<ns>.<tool>` tail, or when no tool is registered for that qualified name.
- [ ] New integration test `packages/exploration_flutter/test/binding_e2e_integration_test.dart` boots a single `ExplorationBinding` via `setUpAll` with the existing `CorePlugin` plus a test-local `_SampleEchoPlugin` (namespace `sample`, one tool `echo`).
- [ ] The test wires an in-process `_BindingVmServiceFake extends VmService` whose `callServiceExtension` routes `ext.flutter.exploration.core.*` to `binding.invokeServiceExtension` and `ext.flutter.exploration.<ns>.<tool>` (for `<ns> != 'core'`) to `binding.invokePluginTool`, converting `Map<String, dynamic>? args` to `Map<String, String>` (jsonEncoding any non-string value).
- [ ] The test drives `VmServiceClient.fromVmService(fake, 'isolate-0').handshake()` and asserts `contractVersion == '1'` and that the returned `plugins` manifest contains both `core` and `sample` namespaces, with `sample.tools` containing `echo`.
- [ ] The test drives `ExplorationSession.start('test goal', const ExplorationConfig())` followed by `session.observe()` and asserts the returned typed `Observation` is non-null.
- [ ] The test drives `session.act(<String, dynamic>{'name': 'sample.echo', 'args': <String, dynamic>{'text': 'hello'}})` and asserts the response satisfies `r['ok'] == true && r['value'] == 'hello' && r['error'] == null`.
- [ ] The test includes a negative guard: a second `VmServiceClient` whose fake raises `RPCError` with code `-32601` for `ext.flutter.exploration.core.handshake` causes `handshake()` to throw `BindingNotInitializedError` (regression guard for the original "Binding not detected" symptom).
- [ ] `flutter test packages/exploration_flutter` is green (the envelope refactor must not regress any existing `CorePlugin`/binding test).
- [ ] `dart analyze packages/exploration_flutter packages/exploration_agent` reports "No issues found!".

comments.author: architect
comments.text: architect: BLOCKED/design-flaw. The approved design's bridging fake calls binding.invokeServiceExtension(method, params) for the tool-call step, but per-tool extensions registered via PluginContext.registerExtension (used by CorePlugin.initialize and any user plugin like the proposed _SampleEchoPlugin) call dart:developer.registerExtension *directly* — they never populate ExplorationBinding._extensionCallbacks. invokeServiceExtension looks up only _extensionCallbacks (exploration_binding.dart:639-647) and throws 'No extension registered' for anything plugin-registered. The handshake/observation steps work (those go through the binding's _registerExtension into _extensionCallbacks), but the tool-call assertion (session.act → ext.flutter.exploration.sample.echo) cannot be dispatched in-process via this helper. core_plugin_registration_test.dart:82-86 already documents this constraint. Three viable resolutions for the discoverer: (a) add a small additive production change so PluginContext.registerExtension also feeds _extensionCallbacks (or a parallel test-only map), tracked as a sibling spec this bead depends on; (b) drop 'real binding' and build a VmService fake that simulates the per-tool wire shape directly (still catches wire-contract drift, loses real-extension fidelity); (c) add a new @visibleForTesting binding helper that resolves any ext.flutter.exploration.<ns>.<tool> via pluginRegistry.mergedTools() and applies the same {ok,value,error} envelope. Returning to discover for a design revision rather than picking unilaterally.

title: agent ↔ binding action exec: route tool calls to ext.flutter.exploration.<ns>.<tool>; remove the non-existent executeAction endpoint
description:
Child of lenny-cvl. Depends on the handshake child.

## Problem

`VmServiceClient.executeAction(name, args)` calls a single dispatcher `ext.flutter.exploration.core.executeAction` with `{name, args}` — but the binding has no such endpoint. Tools are individual extensions: `ext.flutter.exploration.core.<tool>` (CorePlugin) and `ext.flutter.exploration.<ns>.<tool>` (plugin extensions via `PluginContext.registerExtension`), and each takes the tool args directly as flat string params (JSON-encoded per value), returning `{ok, value, error}`. So every `executeAction` call 404s.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Boot `sample_app` against the dogfood DevTools target after lenny-cvl.1 (handshake) and lenny-cvl.2 (observation) have landed — the panel reaches the agent loop's action phase.
2. Let the agent decide on a tool call (e.g. `router.go` or `core.tap`).
3. Observe: `_safeCall('ext.flutter.exploration.core.executeAction', {name, args})` throws `RPCError(-32601, "method not found")` because the binding registers each tool as its own extension (`ext.flutter.exploration.<ns>.<tool>`), never an `executeAction` dispatcher.
4. Alternatively, run the existing unit test `dart test packages/exploration_agent test/vm_service_client_test.dart --plain-name "routes executeAction to the core extension"` — it documents the wrong wire shape (asserts the non-existent dispatcher name).

## Scope

1. Replace `VmServiceClient.executeAction(name, args)`'s single-dispatcher call with a call to `ext.flutter.exploration.<namespace>.<tool>`, splitting the fully-qualified `namespace.tool` name (`core` namespace → `...core.<tool>`). Args are passed directly (no `{name, args}` envelope) and JSON-encoded per value so the binding's `_decodeParams`/`_tryDecode` round-trips them.
2. `ExplorationSession.act()` and `DefaultLoopHost.executeAction()` already receive the qualified name — keep the splitting logic in `VmServiceClient` so callers are unchanged.
3. Handle a malformed/unqualified name (no `.`, leading dot, trailing dot) with an `ArgumentError`.

Out of scope: handshake (sibling lenny-cvl.1, closed), observation (sibling lenny-cvl.2, closed), end-to-end integration test (sibling lenny-cvl.4).

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/vm_service_client.dart` (`_extExecuteAction` ~24-25, `executeAction` ~126-135)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/session.dart` (`act` ~186-210)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/loop_driver/default_loop_host.dart` (`executeAction` ~117-124)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/core_tools/core_plugin.dart` (`initialize` ~105-138 per-tool extension registration, `_decodeParams`/`_tryDecode` ~156-172)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/contract/plugin_context.dart` (`registerExtension`, `buildExtensionMethodName`)
- `packages/exploration_agent/test/vm_service_client_test.dart`, `packages/exploration_agent/test/session_test.dart`, `packages/exploration_agent/test/loop_driver/default_loop_host_test.dart`
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `VmServiceClient.executeAction('router.go', {'route':'/home'})` calls VM service extension `ext.flutter.exploration.router.go` (NOT `...core.executeAction`), verified by a unit test asserting `fake.lastMethod`.
- [ ] `VmServiceClient.executeAction('core.tap', {'id':42})` calls VM service extension `ext.flutter.exploration.core.tap`, verified by a unit test asserting `fake.lastMethod`.
- [ ] On the wire, each arg value is a JSON-encoded string (so `_decodeParams`/`_tryDecode` on the binding round-trips it): for `{'count':42,'target':'home','payload':{'k':1}}` the unit test asserts `lastArgs['count'] == '42'`, `lastArgs['target'] == '"home"'`, `lastArgs['payload'] == '{"k":1}'`.
- [ ] `VmServiceClient.executeAction('tap', {})` (unqualified — no `.`) throws `ArgumentError` whose `name` is `'name'`, verified by a unit test.
- [ ] `VmServiceClient.executeAction('.tool', {})` (leading dot) throws `ArgumentError`, verified by a unit test.
- [ ] `VmServiceClient.executeAction('core.', {})` (trailing dot) throws `ArgumentError`, verified by a unit test.
- [ ] The constant `_extExecuteAction` and any reference to `'ext.flutter.exploration.core.executeAction'` is removed from `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/vm_service_client.dart`, verified by `grep -rn 'core\.executeAction\|_extExecuteAction' packages/exploration_agent/lib` returning zero matches.
- [ ] `ExplorationSession.act` and `DefaultLoopHost.executeAction` signatures are unchanged (still take qualified `<ns>.<tool>` name + args), verified by their existing call-site tests in `session_test.dart` and `default_loop_host_test.dart` continuing to pass with updated fakes.
- [ ] `dart test packages/exploration_agent` is green.
- [ ] `dart analyze packages/exploration_agent` is clean (no new warnings/errors).

title: agent ↔ binding observation: rename to core.get_stable_observation, fix policy wire token, collapse the two observation paths
description:
Child of lenny-cvl. Depends on the handshake child (shared corrected method-not-found constant; lands on a clean base).

## Problem

`VmServiceClient.getStableObservation()` calls `ext.flutter.exploration.core.getStableObservation` (camelCase); the binding registers `...core.get_stable_observation` (snake_case, debug-only) → 404. There are also two observation code paths: `VmServiceClient.getStableObservation` (raw map, used by `ExplorationSession.observe`) and `ObservationPuller` (typed, used by `ExplorationSession.pullObservation`/`observeWithDiff`), which call different things. And the policy wire token diverges: agent's `StabilityPolicy.wireName` is snake_case (`action_relative`) while the binding's `kStabilityPolicyWireNames` is kebab-case (`action-relative`).

## Steps to Reproduce

1. With the dogfood DevTools target connected to `sample_app` (post-lenny-cvl.1: handshake now succeeds and the manifest renders), press Start on the Prompt tab.
2. The agent loop reaches its first observation pull. `DefaultLoopHost.observe()` routes through `ExplorationSession.pullObservation()` → `ObservationPuller.pull()`, which sends `policy: 'action_relative'` (snake_case) to `ext.flutter.exploration.core.get_stable_observation`.
3. The binding's `_decodeObservationRequest._policyFromWire` throws `FormatException("Unknown stability policy: \"action_relative\"...")` — the wire contract requires kebab-case (`action-relative`).
4. Separately, any caller of `ExplorationSession.observe()` (raw-Map path) hits `VmServiceClient.getStableObservation`, which calls the camelCase method name `ext.flutter.exploration.core.getStableObservation` — not registered by the binding → JSON-RPC `-32601` "method not found".
5. Net effect: even past the lenny-cvl.1 handshake fix, the agent loop cannot observe the app — every pull either 404s on the camelCase name or `FormatException`s on the wrong policy token.

## Scope

1. Make all agent-side observation calls use `ext.flutter.exploration.core.get_stable_observation` with the request shape the binding's `_decodeObservationRequest` decodes (flat string params; `policy` as a wire token, `pluginBudgets` as a JSON-encoded string, etc.).
2. Align the policy wire token between `StabilityPolicy.wireName` (agent) and `kStabilityPolicyWireNames` (binding) — pick one casing and use it on both sides.
3. Collapse `VmServiceClient.getStableObservation` and `ObservationPuller` into a single path; `ExplorationSession.observe()` and `pullObservation()`/`observeWithDiff()` route through it.

Out of scope: handshake (sibling), action-routing (sibling), integration test (sibling).

## Anchors

- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/vm_service_client.dart` (`_extGetStableObservation` ~22-23, `getStableObservation` ~126-130)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/session.dart` (`observe` ~131-137, `pullObservation`/`observeWithDiff` ~140-180)
- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/session/observation_puller.dart` (whole file)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/binding/exploration_binding.dart` (`_decodeObservationRequest` ~471-499, `get_stable_observation` registration ~386-408)
- `packages/exploration_flutter/lib/src/observation/observation_request.dart` (`kStabilityPolicyWireNames` ~22-26, `ObservationRequest.fromJson`)
- `packages/exploration_agent/test/vm_service_client_test.dart`, `packages/exploration_agent/test/session_test.dart`
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The agent has exactly one observation method-name constant in `vm_service_client.dart`, and it is NOT the camelCase `core.getStableObservation`; the broken `VmServiceClient.getStableObservation` public method is gone.
- [ ] `ObservationPuller.pull(policy: ...)` sends a wire token that matches the binding's `kStabilityPolicyWireNames` for every `StabilityPolicy` value (`action-relative`, `quiet-frame`, `bounded-stability` — kebab-case).
- [ ] `ExplorationSession.observe()` returns a typed `Observation` (not `Map<String, dynamic>`) by routing through the same `ObservationPuller` that backs `pullObservation()` / `observeWithDiff()` — i.e. all three public observation entrypoints share one code path.
- [ ] The binding's `_decodeObservationRequest._policyFromWire` accepts the wire token the agent now sends without throwing `FormatException` (regression-guarded by an agent-side unit test asserting the wire token string for `StabilityPolicy.actionRelative` is `'action-relative'`).
- [ ] `dart test packages/exploration_agent` is green; the `VmServiceClient.getStableObservation / executeAction` group's *observation* assertions are removed (the executeAction assertions, owned by sibling lenny-cvl.3, remain), and `session_test.dart`'s `observe` expectations decode a typed `Observation`.
- [ ] `flutter test packages/exploration_flutter` is green (no behaviour change there beyond what existing tests cover).
- [ ] `dart analyze packages/exploration_agent packages/exploration_flutter` is clean ("No issues found!").

title: Dark-mode diagram fix + context-assembly visualization on the product site
description: The product site (lenny-n6h) shipped two mermaid SVGs rendered light-on-dark — the perception-action sequence diagram is illegible on the dark page (confirmed by screenshot). This bead (a) fixes the diagram readability and (b) adds a multi-part, dark-native, zero-JS 'what goes into each turn' visualization on the landing page that walks the prompt/context assembly per turn and embellishes the memory-tier structuring (accurate to PRD §10 'build prompt' and §13 'memory: summary vs diff', but with richer visual + prose framing). Scope is site/ only; docs/how-lenny-works.md and docs/build-story.html are out of scope.
design: ## Approved Design (from discovery) DECISIONS: (1) architecture.svg → re-render with a dark mermaid theme tuned to the site palette (light text, transparent bg), keep the .mmd source; (2) loop-turn → DROP the mermaid SVG/.mmd, rebuild the perception-action loop as a native HTML/CSS Astro component sharing the visual language of the new context viz; (3) new context-assembly viz → native HTML/CSS Astro components, zero JS, multi-part: prompt-stack + Turn-N→N+1 delta + illustrative running-summary-over-a-session; (4) ships through the factory. SITE PALETTE (existing CSS vars in site/src/styles/global.css): --bg #0c0f17, --ink #dbe2f1, --muted #8a93a8, --accent-green #6ee7b7, --accent-blue #7aa2f7, --rule #1c2230, --mono. Memory-tier colors to introduce: static=--muted (gray), narrative=amber #f7c873, episodic=--accent-blue, perception=--accent-green. Add a --tier-amber var. WORK ITEMS: 1. site/src/diagrams/architecture.mmd — change the light classDef fills to dark-friendly ones (host: fill:#15233f stroke:#7aa2f7 color:#dbe2f1; plug: fill:#10301c stroke:#6ee7b7 color:#dbe2f1). Re-render to architecture.svg with: mmdc -t dark -b transparent -p -i architecture.mmd -o architecture.svg . Then apply the SAME mermaid-token strip / id-rename post-processing the original build used (commit c52d177) so 'grep -ri mermaid site/dist' stays empty — rename SVG ids to lenny-* and drop class="mermaid"/aria 'mermaid' strings. Verify it renders legibly on #0c0f17 (light text, accent strokes). 2. DELETE site/src/diagrams/loop-turn.svg and site/src/diagrams/loop-turn.mmd. Remove the loopTurn import from site/src/pages/index.astro. 3. NEW site/src/components/LoopTurn.astro — the perception-action loop as a dark-native ordered step list (zero JS, scoped ``` Test: `grep -q 'href="#context"' site/src/components/LoopTurn.astro && grep -q 'Stabilize' site/src/components/LoopTurn.astro && grep -q '
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `site/src/diagrams/architecture.svg` renders legibly on the site dark bg: contains a light text fill (e.g. `#dbe2f1` or `#ECECFF`/similar light token from `mmdc -t dark`) and `background-color: transparent`; has no `fill:#333` text default and no `fill:#fff`/`#ffffff` on text nodes.
- [ ] `site/src/diagrams/architecture.mmd` classDefs use dark-friendly fills: `host` = `fill:#15233f,stroke:#7aa2f7,color:#dbe2f1`; `plug` = `fill:#10301c,stroke:#6ee7b7,color:#dbe2f1`.
- [ ] `grep -ri mermaid site/dist` after build returns nothing (architecture.svg post-processed: root id renamed to `lenny-architecture`, no `class="mermaid"`, no `aria-roledescription`/style tokens containing `mermaid`).
- [ ] `site/src/diagrams/loop-turn.svg` and `site/src/diagrams/loop-turn.mmd` no longer exist.
- [ ] `site/src/components/LoopTurn.astro` exists: a zero-JS ordered 10-step list of the perception-action loop (steps from PRD §10), each row has a step number, a color-coded actor pill (Harness/Host/Plugins/Model), a title, and a one-line detail; step 5 ("Build the prompt") is visually highlighted and contains an anchor link to `#context`; footer note states turn budget ≤ 30s wall clock and session budget ≤ 50 turns or 15 min. Styling is in a scoped `<style>` block.
- [ ] `site/src/components/PromptStack.astro` exists: a zero-JS top-to-bottom stack of region cards in prompt order, grouped Static / Narrative / Episodic / Live perception, each card showing region name (mono), tier tag, "authored by", "changes each turn", and a token budget; includes a caption about the budget asymmetry. Scoped `<style>`.
- [ ] `site/src/components/TurnDelta.astro` exists: a zero-JS Turn N → Turn N+1 strip showing each region's fate color-coded (unchanged=gray, rewritten=amber, slides=blue, fresh/recomputed=green) with a caption ending "The model's leverage over its own future is exactly one paragraph wide." Scoped `<style>`.
- [ ] `site/src/components/SummaryEvolution.astro` exists: a zero-JS set of three note cards labeled ILLUSTRATIVE showing the running summary at turn 1 / ~8 / ~20 of a "log in" session, plus a caption noting the harness never writes this and the text is illustrative. Scoped `<style>`.
- [ ] `site/src/pages/index.astro`: the loop-turn `<figure>` in `#how-it-works` is replaced by `<LoopTurn />`; the `loopTurn` import is removed; the architecture `<figure set:html={architecture}>` is unchanged. A new `<Section id="context" title="What goes into each turn — the agent's memory">` is inserted after `#how-it-works` and before `#surfaces`, containing an intro paragraph then `<PromptStack />`, `<TurnDelta />`, `<SummaryEvolution />` each with a brief lead-in line.
- [ ] `site/src/components/Nav.astro` has a "Memory" link pointing to `base + '/#context'`.
- [ ] `site/src/styles/global.css` defines `--tier-amber: #f7c873;` in `:root`.
- [ ] `npm --prefix site install && npm --prefix site run build` exits 0.
- [ ] `site/dist/index.html` exists and contains the strings `Running summary`, `AGENTS.md`, `Turn N`, and `illustrative` (case-insensitive for the last).
- [ ] `site/dist/getting-started/index.html` still exists.
- [ ] No `.js` files under `site/dist` (zero framework JS preserved): `find site/dist -name '*.js'` returns nothing.
- [ ] `.github/workflows/pages.yml` is unchanged by this bead.

title: Product site for Lenny, deployed to GitHub Pages
description: A small product/marketing+docs site for Lenny (the Flutter Exploration Agent), built with Astro, living in site/ at the repo root, deployed to GitHub Pages at https://nicholasspencer.github.io/lenny via a GitHub Actions workflow. Two pages in v1: a landing page and a Getting Started page. Honest dev-tool positioning — confident, names the v1-PoC status, links out to the existing docs/ (PRD, plugin authoring guide, how-lenny-works, build-story) rather than rebuilding them.
design:
## Approved Design (from discovery) STACK: Astro static site in `site/` (own package.json, npm). Zero-JS-by-default. Built-in Shiki for code blocks. `astro.config.mjs`: `site='https://nicholasspencer.github.io'`, `base='/lenny'`. DEPLOY: `.github/workflows/pages.yml` on push to main touching `site/**`. PAGES: (1) Landing (2) Getting Started — as detailed in the description. DIAGRAMS: pre-rendered SVGs of `docs/how-lenny-works.md` Diagram 1 (component graph) and Diagram 2 (one-turn sequence), embedded inline, no client-side mermaid. VISUAL: dark theme bg ~#0c0f17, ink #dbe2f1, accents green #6ee7b7 / blue #7aa2f7. RULED OUT: VitePress/Eleventy, Flutter-web build, custom domain, rebuilding the PRD/plugin-guide, client-side mermaid. Decision (spec-time, within the approved envelope): the two mermaid diagrams are **committed as hand-finished SVG files** under `site/src/diagrams/` (the "commit the SVGs" branch the design explicitly allows), with the extracted `.mmd` source committed beside each for maintainability. This avoids a headless-Chromium dependency in CI. `.astro` code blocks use `` from `astro:components` (Shiki under the hood); markdown is not used for page bodies. ## Implementation Plan 1. Scaffold `site/` package — `site/package.json` ```json { "name": "lenny-site", "type": "module", "version": "0.1.0", "private": true, "scripts": { "dev": "astro dev", "build": "astro build", "preview": "astro preview" }, "dependencies": { "astro": "^5.0.0" } } ``` Then run `npm --prefix site install` to generate `site/package-lock.json`. Test: `npm --prefix site install && test -f site/package-lock.json && echo OK` → expect `OK` Commit: `chore(site): scaffold Astro package` 2. ``` Test: `grep -q "astro:components" site/src/components/CodeBlock.astro && grep -q "

Watch an LLM drive a browser and you see the failure mode: it clicks, immediately reads a half-rendered DOM, and acts on the snapshot. It's racing animations, network calls, partial updates. A big chunk of agent failures are “I looked too early.”

  • Frame lifecycle. Flutter's scheduler knows when work is pending and when a frame committed — so “is the app still settling?” has a real answer.
  • Semantics tree. The screen-reader view gives interactable elements at a clean level, not raw pixels.
  • VM-service hook. A debug-mode app exposes a service the harness drives over the wire — observe, then act.

Put together: one trustworthy observation per turn.

The host is literally a custom WidgetsBinding — it claims the framework's lifecycle slot in main(), registers a handful of VM-service extensions, and otherwise gets out of the way. Outside debug mode it doesn't install at all. Plugins live in your app and contribute tools, observation fragments, and lifecycle hooks.

Every turn is the same shape — the perception-action loop:

  • Scriptable CLI — exploration_cli: headless, for CI and batch trajectory collection.
  • In-IDE DevTools panel — an Exploration tab with Prompt, Thinking, and Timeline views; runs the same loop in-panel and shows the model's reasoning live.

Neither reimplements anything — the loop is a library.

  • exploration_router — exposes the current route stack.
  • exploration_riverpod — reports which providers are loading.
  • exploration_dio — reports in-flight HTTP requests.

Know about your stack? Write a plugin — it ships in your app's pubspec.yaml, not in the harness.

  • qwen-mlx — a local Qwen MoE served by swift-infer (default).
  • claude — Anthropic.
  • openai — OpenAI.

This is a proof of concept. The architecture is real and the loop runs end to end, but APIs will move, coverage is partial, and you should expect rough edges. Read the PRD for where it's headed.

``` Test: `npm --prefix site run build && grep -q "Lenny" site/dist/index.html && grep -q "getting-started" site/dist/index.html && grep -q "how-it-works" site/dist/index.html && grep -q "exploration_router" site/dist/index.html && grep -q "qwen-mlx" site/dist/index.html && grep -qi " ExplorationAppConfig( plugins: [RouterPlugin(), RiverpodPlugin(), DioPlugin()], app: MaterialApp(home: HomeScreen()), ); } void main() => ExplorationBinding.run(MyExplorationApp());`; const cliCmd = `dart run exploration_cli --goal "open settings and toggle dark mode" --model qwen-mlx # or: --model claude | --model openai`; ---

  • Dart 3.11+.

title: DevTools: flutter_test coverage for top-level ExplorationDevToolsExtension (closes the lenny-g4u gap)
description:
**Coverage gap that allowed lenny-g4u to ship.** Existing shell_test.dart and friends pump `ExplorationShell` directly with stub VM-service-URI callbacks — they never pump `ExplorationDevToolsExtension` wrapping the real `DevToolsExtension` widget. So the build-phase ordering bug (parent-build reading `serviceManager.connectedState` before `DevToolsExtension.initState` runs) was invisible to CI.

## What this bead does

Add an integration-style flutter_test that:

1. Constructs the real `ExplorationDevToolsExtension` widget (not the inner `ExplorationShell`).
2. Stubs `devtools_extensions`'s `serviceManager` global with a fake that:
   - Returns a stub serviceUri
   - Exposes a fake `connectedState` ValueListenable
   - Throws (or records) on access **before** `DevToolsExtension.initState` would have run
3. Pumps the widget tree, asserts that initial frame renders without the 'serviceManager has not been initialized' error.
4. Optional bonus: covers reconnect path — drive `connectedState` to fire and assert a fresh manifest probe.

## Constraints / unknowns

`devtools_extensions` makes the `serviceManager` a top-level getter, so faking it requires either dependency injection (push a configurable getter through the package; PR upstream) or wrapping the production widget tree so the test doesn't go through DevToolsExtension at all (but then we're back to the same gap that allowed g4u).

If neither path is tractable in a single bead, ship a smaller win: a unit test on a new `MainExtensionRoot` widget that owns the `Builder`-wrapping pattern and is testable in isolation. The widget tree in main.dart is then a one-liner that uses `MainExtensionRoot`.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A test exists that would have failed against lenny-g4u's broken main.dart (direct property read above DevToolsExtension)
- [ ] The test passes against the current fixed main.dart
- [ ] If a real DevToolsExtension fake proves infeasible in this bead, the smaller-win factoring of main.dart into a testable widget is acceptable, with a note explaining the punt

title: DevTools: standalone web debug launch + workflow doc for iterating on the panel
description:
Today the only way to iterate on the exploration_devtools panel is to rebuild the extension bundle (tool/build_devtools_extension.sh), restart the dogfood target, and hard-reload Chrome. That's ~30s per iteration and burns the Dart hot-reload story entirely.

`devtools_extensions` ships a simulated DevTools environment: `dart run devtools_extensions serve` (from the package dir) launches the panel as a normal Flutter web app with a fake DTD + fake VM service. Full hot reload works there.

## What this bead does

1. Add an `exploration_devtools (standalone web)` config to `.vscode/launch.json` that runs the package via the simulated environment. Wire a VS Code task so it can be debugged with the Dart extension (breakpoints in .dart files, variable inspection).
2. Document the standalone vs in-DevTools workflow in `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md` (or `docs/development.md`):
   - When to use standalone (fast iteration on widgets, layout, form behavior — anything that doesn't depend on real DTD/VM behavior)
   - When to use in-DevTools (verifying real binding handshake, real /v1/models calls, real session.run end-to-end)
   - How to attach a debugger in each mode (Dart-side via VM service URI; browser-side via Chrome source maps)
3. Cross-reference from `MANUAL_TESTS.md` so reviewers know which mode each manual smoke targets.

## Why P2

Without this, every panel iteration requires the full rebuild loop, which makes lenny-g4u-style first-frame bugs expensive to find and fix. Filing this also unblocks the work for lenny-r8f (DtdProviderConfigStore) because that has to be tested in the in-DevTools mode, not standalone.
design:
## Approach

`devtools_extensions` supports running an extension's `lib/main.dart` directly as a
Flutter **web** app against a simulated DevTools environment (fake DTD + fake VM
service) by passing `--dart-define=use_simulated_environment=true`. Run that way the
panel is a normal `flutter run -d chrome` target: Dart-Code attaches its own VM
service, so breakpoints in `exploration_devtools/lib/**` and hot reload on save both
work — none of the bundle-rebuild / Chrome-hard-reload loop. Browser-side debugging is
just Chrome DevTools on the served app (debug builds ship source maps automatically).

### Decisions

- **launch.json**: add one config, `"exploration_devtools (standalone web)"`, type
  `dart`, `request: launch`, `program: lib/main.dart`, `cwd:
  ${workspaceFolder}/packages/exploration_devtools`, `deviceId: chrome`, `args:
  ["--dart-define=use_simulated_environment=true"]`. No `preLaunchTask` — the point is
  to skip the bundle rebuild. New presentation group `4_devtools`.
- **No tasks.json change.** The `dart`/`chrome` launch config is directly debuggable;
  Dart-Code owns the VM service. (Contrast: the dogfood `sample_app` configs need the
  `Build DevTools Extension` preLaunchTask because they serve the *compiled bundle*
  inside real DevTools.)
- **Doc location**: `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md`, section "Iterating on
  the panel" — not `docs/development.md` (which does not exist).
- **Cross-reference**: edit `packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md` (the
  package-local file; there is no repo-root MANUAL_TESTS.md) to point at the README
  section and label the existing smokes as in-DevTools mode.

### Out of scope

- Wiring `DtdProviderConfigStore` (lenny-r8f) — its own bead.
- Any change to the compiled-bundle build path or the dogfood `sample_app` configs.
- `docs/manual_smoke.md` — already cross-links MANUAL_TESTS.md; no edit needed.

## Implementation Plan

1. Add the standalone-web launch config — `.vscode/launch.json`

title: DevTools prompt panel: wire Start button to session.run() so the agent actually runs
description:
Closes a documented AC gap from lenny-0wd. After 0wd merged, the prompt panel builds a real `ModelProvider` from the configured provider/model and exposes it via `PromptPanelController.activeProvider`, but the controller's `start()` does not yet call `session.run()`. Pressing **Start** mints a config but doesn't actually contact the model.

## Why deferred

The 0wd spec referenced two pieces that don't exist in the agent package:

1. `TrajectoryWriter.noop()` — there's no no-op writer in `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/trajectory/writer.dart`. The panel needs one, or it needs to wire a real DTD-backed writer (DevTools panels can't write to disk).
2. `DefaultLoopHost.fromSession(...)` — exists, but expects the merged **core + plugin tool descriptors** as input. The panel currently has no way to obtain that merged map from the running app's binding.

The 0wd bitsmith chose to ship the provider+catalog plumbing and defer the actual session invocation rather than fabricate either of those.

## What this bead does

1. Add a no-op or DTD-backed writer in `exploration_agent` (or expose the panel's existing `DtdTrajectorySink` as the production target).
2. Surface a way for the panel to obtain the merged tool-descriptor map from the binding via VM service extension. Possible approach: extend the binding handshake (lenny-cx6.11) to return tool descriptors alongside the plugin manifest, OR expose a separate `ext.flutter.exploration.tools` extension.
3. Wire `PromptPanelController.start()` to call `session.run(host: DefaultLoopHost.fromSession(session, tools), provider: activeProvider, writer: ...)`. Hook `SessionStarted` / `SessionEnded` / `SessionProgressEvent` streams to drive the UI's running state (already partially wired in `prompt_tab_mount.dart`).
4. Manual AC: with a configured provider and a connected sample_app, pressing **Start** runs at least one turn end-to-end and the trajectory appears in the timeline panel.

## Touches

- `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/trajectory/writer.dart` — add no-op writer (or expose an alternative)
- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/prompt_panel_controller.dart` — call session.run
- `packages/exploration_agent` (potentially) — extend binding handshake or add tools extension
- Tests covering the new wiring
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `PromptPanelController.start()` invokes `ExplorationSession.run()` with the configured `ModelProvider` and the merged `DefaultLoopHost`; calling `start()` without a `providerCfg` throws `StateError` before any session.run is attempted.
- [ ] The controller wires a real `TrajectoryWriter` backed by a `BroadcastTrajectorySink` (in-memory) for v1; a code comment on the sink field names `DtdTrajectorySink` as the future on-disk successor.
- [ ] Tool descriptors handed to `DefaultLoopHost.fromSession` come from `session.handshake.plugins` (the live binding) intersected with `PromptPanelConfig.enabledPluginNamespaces` via a shared `buildPluginTools` helper — never a hardcoded list.
- [ ] `PromptPanelController.runFuture` resolves with a `SessionTermination` when the loop exits; `PromptTabMount` observes that completion and re-enables the form (existing `SessionEnded`/`SessionStarted` handling remains; new wiring covers natural loop termination too).
- [ ] `PromptPanelController.trajectory` yields each `TrajectoryRecord` produced by the live writer; `TimelinePanelMount` is wired to consume that stream through `ExplorationShell`.
- [ ] Manual: with a configured provider and a connected `sample_app`, pressing Start runs at least one turn end-to-end and at least one `TurnRecord` is rendered in the Timeline tab. (Documented in `packages/exploration_devtools/docs/manual_smoke.md`.)
