title: Memory tools, checkpoints, and compaction for chat-shape conversation
description: Follow-up to lenny-wisp-cl4 (chat-shape conversation rebuild). Once the spine ships, evidence-driven additions to the memory/tools surface: (a) `core.plan(text)` so the model can pin an explicit plan that survives trim and is queryable in trajectory; (b) `memory.remember(key, value)` / `memory.recall(key)` for exact-fidelity scratchpad recall of values the agent needs verbatim (confirmation codes, typed inputs, etc.); (c) mid-session checkpoints enabling save/resume; (d) compact — model-driven fold of an oldest trimmed block into a single synthetic user turn for when trim alone can't keep prompts under threshold. Deferred deliberately — build only when trim-only sessions show real overflow or a concrete failure mode demands a specific tool. Bundled because they share design surface (memory/state semantics) and likely a single test harness.

title: exploration_agent: vm_service_io.dart still imported by the web-safe lib
description: Confine vm_service_io.dart to a *_io seam: move connect()/the vm_service_io import out of the web-safe vm_service_client.dart (and ExplorationSession.connect) into a CLI-only library, and extend tool/check_no_dart_io.dart to also flag vm_service_io.dart imports in web code (the current guard only catches direct dart:io imports, so it never tripped on this). Discovered while fixing lenny-dzh.

title: (SPIKE) Recursive exploration + pure-Dart binding: can the agent explore non-Flutter Dart apps and itself?
description:
Two related questions surfaced during dogfooding:

1. **Recursive exploration**: can the exploration agent explore the exploration_devtools panel itself? (User-facing dogfood loop.)
2. **Non-Flutter Dart support**: `exploration_agent` (the harness) is pure Dart — depends only on http, json_schema, meta. `exploration_flutter` is the Flutter binding adapter. Is the architecture ready to support a hypothetical `exploration_dart` binding for pure-Dart Dart apps (CLI tools, dart:io servers, dart_frog backends, etc.)?

## Why this is one spike, not two

Both questions test the same hypothesis: that the Flutter coupling lives entirely in `exploration_flutter` and the agent + plugin contract are framework-neutral. If true, (a) we could build a Dart-VM binding without touching exploration_agent, and (b) such a binding could in principle target the panel's own Dart-web runtime.

## Scope: spike, not implementation

Time-box ~1 day. Deliverable is a written report (markdown in `docs/spikes/recursive-and-pure-dart.md` or a long bead note), not code. The report should answer:

### Recursive exploration sub-questions
- Can ExplorationBinding be installed alongside DevToolsExtension's binding, or are they mutually exclusive? (Hypothesis: mutually exclusive — Flutter allows one binding per process.)
- If mutually exclusive, is there a useful subset of agent functionality that works without ExplorationBinding? (e.g., agent-driven tool dispatch with a custom WebAdapter; loop-driver-only, no observation pull)
- What's the smallest viable demo: agent explores the panel's Provider Config form via DTD-based observation?
- Even if technically possible — is it actually useful, or is the panel too form-shaped (vs a rich app surface) for the screenshot+semantic-tree loop to add value?

### Non-Flutter Dart sub-questions
- Inventory `exploration_agent` for any Flutter assumptions (probably none, but verify; check trajectory writers, the loop driver, model providers, schema validation).
- Inventory the plugin contract: does `PluginManifestEntry` etc. carry Flutter-specific types? Plugins emit JSON; bindings consume it — should be neutral.
- For a hypothetical `exploration_dart` binding (no widget tree, no router, no rendering):
  - What's the observation surface? (Process state? Log tail? Open file handles? Active HTTP connections?)
  - What's the action surface? (CLI input? Programmatic API calls? File ops?)
  - Which existing plugins port directly (dio is already pure Dart — exploration_dio doesn't depend on Flutter, only on package:dio)? Which would need rewrites?
- Concrete proof-of-concept candidate: explore a dart_frog server's endpoints via a hypothetical dart-binding + dio-server-side plugin?

### Architectural decisions
- Is the binding-contract already abstract enough, or does `ExplorationBinding extends WidgetsFlutterBinding` leak into the agent's expectations via VM service extension names / handshake format?
- Should we rename `exploration_flutter` to `exploration_flutter_binding` to make room for `exploration_dart_binding` (preempt confusion before it ships)?
- PRD §22 is web-compat for the agent; does pure-Dart-host change that constraint?

## Out of scope (file separately if the spike justifies it)

- Building the dart binding.
- Building any new plugin.
- Refactoring exploration_agent to extract more shared surface.

## Why P3 not P2

PoC is shipping; the v0.5 PRD is Flutter-only by design. This spike is foundational research for the v1 architectural direction, not blocking any near-term user. Worth doing now because the answer shapes whether lenny-cx6's eventual successor epic is 'more Flutter' or 'more frameworks'.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Written report in docs/spikes/ or as a long bead note addressing every sub-question above
- [ ] A concrete recommendation: yes / no / yes-with-caveats on (a) recursive exploration, (b) pure-Dart binding as a follow-on architecture
- [ ] If yes on either, a follow-up bead skeleton (title + scope, no full spec) for the implementation work
- [ ] If no on either, the report records the blocking constraint so we don't re-litigate the question

title: DevTools: wire DtdProviderConfigStore in main.dart so provider config persists across reloads
description:
lenny-0wd merged with the prompt panel constructed from `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/main.dart` using the shell's default `InMemoryProviderConfigStore` — meaning provider config (Bearer tokens, base URLs, model ids) does NOT persist across DevTools reloads in production. The class `DtdProviderConfigStore` exists and is wired correctly behind read/write-string callbacks; main.dart just doesn't pass it.

## What this bead does

Construct `DtdProviderConfigStore` with read/write callbacks bound to DTD's actual workspace-property primitive (verify the dtd 4.x API surface — bitsmith found `IDEWorkspaceProperty` doesn't exist and used callbacks as a swap point). Pass the result to `ExplorationShell`. Also confirm that secrets (Bearer tokens, API keys) are stored in DTD's secret store, not its plain workspace properties — if DTD does not differentiate, document the limitation in MANUAL_TESTS.md.

## Touches

- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/main.dart` — instantiate DtdProviderConfigStore
- `packages/exploration_devtools/lib/src/panels/provider_config_store.dart` — verify callbacks bind to DTD
- `packages/exploration_devtools/MANUAL_TESTS.md` — document persistence behavior + any secret-storage caveats
- `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md` — fix the "In-DevTools" workflow wording (see drive-by below)

## Drive-by fixes folded in (from lenny-93o review)

1. **README "In-DevTools" wording.** The "Iterating on the panel" section added by lenny-93o tells the reader to launch the `Dogfood: sample_app + exploration_cli` compound "then open DevTools" — but that compound starts the app + CLI, not DevTools itself. Reword to make clear DevTools is opened separately (e.g. from the Flutter run's devtools URL, or `dart devtools`). One-line doc tweak in `packages/exploration_devtools/README.md`.
2. **Spec validation-plan comment-stripping regex (note, not a repo change).** lenny-93o's verbatim AC1–AC3 validation commands strip `//` line comments from `.vscode/launch.json` with a regex that also clobbers the pre-existing `"ws://127.0.0.1:0/ws"` default string, so those commands error on the *original* file too — they were never a valid jsonc check. If r8f's spec needs to validate launch.json/jsonc, use a string-aware jsonc parser (`dart pub global run`-able tool, or a tiny inline Dart snippet), not a `sed`/regex `//`-strip. No file in the repo changes for this item; it's guidance for whoever writes r8f's spec.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] main.dart constructs ExplorationShell with a real DtdProviderConfigStore (not the InMemoryProviderConfigStore default)
- [ ] Manual: configuring a provider, reloading DevTools, returning to the panel — config (including masked secrets) re-renders without re-entering
- [ ] If DTD has no separate secret store, MANUAL_TESTS.md documents that tokens are stored in DTD workspace properties (visible to anyone with workspace access)
- [ ] README 'In-DevTools' / 'Iterating on the panel' section makes clear DevTools is opened separately (not started by the Dogfood compound)
- [ ] No regression in shell_test.dart (which uses the InMemoryProviderConfigStore default)

title: DevTools prompt panel: persist last-used config across reloads
description:
Today every reload of the DevTools panel resets the prompt-panel form: goal cleared, max-turns back to 50, wall-clock budget back to 15min, model dropdown back to first entry, plugin toggles back to all-on. For dogfooding the agent through repeated turns / app restarts this is annoying — you re-type the goal every time.

## Architecture

Persist the last-used `PromptPanelConfig` (excluding the model id, which is owned by the provider-config layer in lenny-0wd). Storage backend: DTD if available (per-workspace, scoped), `window.localStorage` fallback. Restore on panel mount; overwrite on every `onStart`.

Goal field is restored verbatim. Max-turns / budget are restored. Plugin toggles are restored, but only for namespaces that still appear in the live manifest (lenny-8rw) — newly-removed namespaces are dropped, newly-added namespaces default to enabled.

## Out of scope

- A 'recent goals' history dropdown (file separately if useful — for v1 a single last-used slot is enough).
- Sharing the persisted config with the CLI.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Submitting the prompt panel persists the config (goal, max-turns, budget, enabled plugin namespaces)
- [ ] On panel reload, the form prefills from the persisted config
- [ ] Plugin toggles only restore for namespaces present in the current manifest; missing ones are dropped silently
- [ ] First-ever load (no persisted config) shows the existing defaults (50 turns / 15 min / all plugins enabled)
- [ ] No API key or other secret value is ever persisted via this code path

title: Exploration request API: job queue + MCP server
description:
Today the exploration agent is invoked one of two ways: directly from `exploration_cli` (lenny-cx6.20) or via an embedded `DefaultLoopHost` (lenny-0bd) inside whatever harness the developer wired up. There is no way for an outside requester — a human via REST/CLI, an IDE plugin, another Claude Code session, a CI job — to say 'explore this app for me with this goal' and get a result back. This bead is the placeholder to scope that surface.

Two related but separable surfaces:

1. Job queue
   - Durable submit / status / result API for exploration runs
   - Run record: id, app under test (workspace + entry point + plugin manifest), goal, model/endpoint, max-turns/budget, status (queued|running|succeeded|failed|cancelled), transcript artifacts, success-criteria evaluation
   - Backing store options to evaluate during /discover: sqlite, beads itself (each run = bead), filesystem queue, dolt, redis. Beads is interesting because we already track work durably and have a sync story.
   - Lifecycle: queued → claimed-by-worker → running → terminal. Reclaim on worker death.
   - At least one worker process that drains the queue against a real ExplorationSession. v1 can be one local worker; design should not preclude multi-worker / remote workers.

2. MCP server
   - A Model Context Protocol server exposing exploration as tools, so any MCP-aware client (Claude Code, Claude Desktop, IDEs) can natively call it
   - Likely tools (final shape from /discover):
     - `explore.submit` — start a run, returns run id
     - `explore.status` — poll
     - `explore.result` — fetch transcript / outcome / artifacts
     - `explore.cancel`
     - `explore.list` — recent runs, filterable
     - Optionally lower-level passthroughs (`session.tap`, `session.read_state`, etc.) so a driving Claude can do step-by-step exploration interactively rather than fire-and-forget
   - Sits on top of the job queue, not parallel to it — the queue is the durable substrate

Driving use cases (motivating; not exhaustive):
- Another Claude Code session working on a Flutter feature wants to verify 'logging in still works after my refactor' — submits an explore job with that goal and polls
- Developer in their editor invokes an MCP tool: 'find a path to the settings screen and toggle dark mode'
- CI step on PR: explore the success-criteria flows; fail the build if any drop below threshold
- A future supervisor / committee that wants to dispatch exploration the way it dispatches builds today

Open questions for /discover (not to answer here):
- v1 transport: stdio MCP only? + HTTP REST? + websocket for streaming transcript?
- Authentication / authorization model — local-only initially? token? share with bd's actor model?
- Multi-tenancy / app-isolation: one server per host app, or one server that knows how to bring up many?
- How does the requester specify 'which app' — workspace path + run command? prebuilt artifact id? attach to already-running app via VM service URI?
- Streaming vs. polling: does `explore.result` block until done or is there a separate `explore.tail` for live transcript?
- Relationship to lenny-qxx (native signals): does the MCP server expose those too, or only the Flutter-side primitives?
- Failure semantics for the MCP layer: how do partial / timeout / budget-exceeded outcomes surface as MCP responses?

Out of scope for this bead: actually building any of it. /discover should produce a small set of concrete child beads (probably: queue substrate + worker, MCP transport, schema for run records, one driving use case end-to-end).
notes: Filed at user request. Should sit in draft until /discover is run on it. Likely a small epic once decomposed.

title: Capture native-platform signals: platform channels + adb / iOS device bridge
description:
The exploration agent currently observes only Dart-side surfaces: widget tree, semantics, Riverpod state, Navigator, Dio HTTP traffic. A real Flutter app emits a second, parallel channel of behavior across the platform-channel boundary into native code, plus a third channel that's only visible at the OS/device level. Without these, the agent can't reason about anything that touches the platform — permissions dialogs, deep links, push notifications, in-app purchases, native pickers, plugin failures, ANRs/crashes, or any signal that lives in logcat / unified logs.

This bead is a placeholder to scope (in a follow-up /discover) what subset of those signals to capture and how. Three rough surfaces to consider — final selection is a discovery decision, not pre-committed:

1. Platform-channel tracing
   - Intercept MethodChannel / EventChannel / BasicMessageChannel calls in both directions
   - Capture channel name, method, args (with a redaction hook), result/error, latency
   - Likely seam: an ExplorationBinding-installed BinaryMessenger wrapper, similar to how DioTrackingInterceptor wraps Dio
   - Surfaces: which plugins fired, what args, what failed

2. Android device signals (adb-backed)
   - logcat stream filtered by app PID (parsed into structured events)
   - dumpsys snapshots: activity (current task/intent), input_method, package (permissions), connectivity
   - Input injection / observation: `adb shell input tap/swipe/text`, getevent
   - Screenshot / UI Automator dump for ground-truth comparison
   - Lifecycle: ANR traces, crash dumps from /data/anr and tombstones

3. iOS device signals (equivalents)
   - Simulator: `xcrun simctl` for io (screenshot/tap), spawn (logs), push, openurl, location, status_bar
   - Real device: idb (Facebook's iOS Device Bridge) or libimobiledevice for syslog, screenshot, input
   - Unified logging: `xcrun simctl spawn booted log stream --predicate 'subsystem == "<bundle-id>"'`
   - AccessibilityInspector / UI test framework as a parallel ground-truth channel

Open questions for /discover (not to answer here):
- Scope of v1: platform channels only? + Android only? all three?
- Where do these run: host-side process driven by exploration_cli, in-app shim, or both?
- How are device tools surfaced as agent tools (adb-tap, logcat-tail) vs. passive signals on the timeline?
- What's the privacy/redaction story for logcat lines that include user data?
- Is this packaged as a new `exploration_native` package or merged into exploration_flutter?

Out of scope for this bead: actually building any of it. This is the design-discovery entry point; a /discover pass should produce one or more concrete child beads with shipped APIs and bounded scope.
notes: Filed at user request while waiting on factoryskills-2wl. Should sit in draft until /discover is run on it.

comments.author: Nico Spencer
comments.text:
Stretch idea (separate workstream, file as child once shape is clearer): the adb/simctl/idb path treats the device as a black box from outside. That's the right v1 — fast to build, no platform code, works for any Flutter app. But longer term, for native iOS/macOS development specifically, we should look into building a first-class equivalent of ExplorationBinding inside the Swift runtime itself.

Sketch:
- A Swift package (`ExplorationKit` or similar) that links into a host app the way ExplorationBinding does for Flutter
- Hooks into UIKit/AppKit/SwiftUI lifecycle to capture: view hierarchy snapshots (with accessibility), responder chain events, NotificationCenter traffic, NSURLSession, UserDefaults reads/writes, navigation stack changes, scene transitions
- SwiftUI-specific: snapshot the rendered view tree via the reflection / mirror APIs, capture @State / @ObservedObject / @StateObject mutations
- Combine / async-await tracing — analog to how the Flutter side traces Riverpod and Streams
- Same VM-service-style RPC surface as the Flutter side so the agent loop is platform-agnostic at the protocol layer
- macOS too — same kit covers AppKit, useful for dogfooding (lenny itself is a candidate host)

Why first-class > extension (adb/simctl) long term:
- Symbol-level fidelity (you see VC names, view class names, accessibility identifiers, not pixel rectangles)
- Deterministic capture — no race against logcat / unified-logging buffer
- Same redaction / consent model across Flutter and native
- Testing-API-grade input injection without device-bridge fragility

This is bigger than lenny-qxx and probably wants to be its own epic once /discover on this bead lands. Filing here as a tracking note so we don't forget the second axis.

title: Plugin contract extension: resources & prompts (v2)
description: PRD §8: defer Resources (passive content the agent reads on demand) and Prompts (pre-authored multi-step workflows) to v2. The plugin contract should leave room: extension/exploration/config.yaml accommodates resources:/prompts: keys, ExplorationPlugin can grow getters in a backward-compatible way. Implement when (a) chat UI is on the roadmap or (b) a plugin author needs reference material exceeding the per-turn observation budget. Out of v1 scope.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ExplorationResource and ExplorationPrompt types defined
- [ ] Manifest schema extended
- [ ] Backward-compatible with v1 plugins
- [ ] Reuses MCP/Packaged-AI-Assets-shaped source files where possible

title: Marionette compatibility plugin (post-v1)
description: PRD §20.1: a MarionetteCompatibilityPlugin exposing Marionette's familiar tool set through the host. Three approaches: inheritance (preferred — ExplorationBinding extends MarionetteBinding, contingent on Marionette's class being open to subclassing), reimplementation (bounded work, loses free updates), or coexistence (not viable). Verify Marionette source for subclass-friendliness; if brittle, fall back to reimplementation. Explicitly post-v1 per PRD §18.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Marionette source reviewed for subclass-friendliness
- [ ] Path chosen (inheritance vs reimplementation) and documented
- [ ] Plugin ships exposing the §20.1 tool set

title: Plugin scaffolding generator (exploration_plugin create)
description: PRD §19 mentions a 'template package generator (exploration_plugin create my_plugin)'. Generates a new plugin package with the contract scaffolding, manifest, and minimal observe()/busyState() stubs. Nice-to-have, not v1-blocking — written manually until generator exists. Defer until contract has stabilized.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Generator produces a working plugin skeleton from a single command
- [ ] Generated package compiles and registers cleanly
- [ ] Generator covers config.yaml manifest

title: DevTools panel: mid-turn interrupt support
description: PRD §21 open question: should the DevTools panel allow the user to interrupt the agent mid-turn (cancel a long generation, inject a hint into the running summary)? Default position: yes, both, but only if cheap on top of existing session state. If it adds meaningful complexity, defer to v2. Defaults to v2 unless trivial during S22-S24 implementation.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Decide cheap vs meaningful complexity
- [ ] If cheap: implement cancel + hint injection
- [ ] If meaningful: document as v2 explicitly

title: Empirical tuning of action-relative stability default (800ms)
description: PRD §21 open question: the 800ms default still needs empirical tuning against real apps. Run sweeps against the sample app + 1-2 real apps measuring observation-completeness vs latency tradeoff. Adjust default if warranted. Not v1-critical — can ship with the 800ms guess.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tuning sweep run against ≥3 apps
- [ ] Default value justified or revised
- [ ] Findings documented in PRD addendum

title: Host diagnostic: warn when interactive widgets lack semantics
description: Connect-time host diagnostic only. Scan for interactable-looking widgets (GestureDetector, InkWell, custom buttons) that lack semantics annotations and surface a clear warning. The fix in v0.5 is to write a custom-widget plugin (the host no longer owns isInteractiveWidget knobs); the diagnostic message points the user at the plugin authoring guide. P3 because v0.5 deemphasizes host-side custom-widget handling — the plugin path is the canonical answer.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `_InteractiveSemanticsAuditor` walker identifies `GestureDetector` instances with non-null tap/long-press/double-tap callbacks that lack an enclosing `Semantics` annotation in their ancestor chain
- [ ] Walker identifies `InkWell` (and `InkResponse`) instances with non-null `onTap`/`onLongPress`/`onDoubleTap` that have no semantic label provided either inline (`InkWell.excludeFromSemantics == false` AND no parent `Semantics` with a label) or via an ancestor `Semantics`
- [ ] Walker identifies `MouseRegion` instances whose nearest `GestureDetector` ancestor has click handlers but no semantics
- [ ] Walker accepts a configurable list of additional class names (`extraInteractiveTypes: List<String>`) and flags any `Element` whose widget runtime type matches a name in that list and lacks an ancestor `Semantics`
- [ ] Diagnostic surfaces results as a JSON list of `{widget_type: String, location: String, suggested_fix_pointer: String}` records via VM service extension `ext.flutter.exploration.core.diagnostics_warnings`
- [ ] `location` is populated from `Element.debugGetCreatorChain(1)` when available; falls back to the widget's `runtimeType.toString()` when no creator info is present
- [ ] `suggested_fix_pointer` text is exactly: `"These widgets won't be visible to the agent. Add semantics annotations, or write a plugin that exposes them — see https://docs.example.com/exploration/plugin-authoring (cx6.35)."`
- [ ] Diagnostic runs exactly once on first call to the extension after binding `ensureInitialized`, results are cached, and subsequent calls return the cached results without re-walking
- [ ] If the walk throws, the extension returns `{ok: false, error: <message>, results: []}` and logs a single line via `debugPrint`; binding continues to function
- [ ] Diagnostic and extension registration are gated behind `kDebugMode || kProfileMode`; in release mode the extension is not registered and the auditor code is not executed

title: Nightly dogfood: launchagent runs swift-infer e2e on local Mac
description:
## Problem

`lenny-cx6.43` shipped an `AgentDogfoodHarness` that can drive real swift-infer
through the agent loop, but the corresponding e2e test
(`packages/exploration_agent/test/e2e/dogfood_e2e_test.dart`) skips by design
whenever `SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT` / `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN` are unset. CI never
exports either (and has no swift-infer to point at), so the live-model
regression signal the harness was built to provide is never actually exercised
on a schedule. The infrastructure is wired; no one pulls the trigger.

## Goal

A macOS launchd LaunchAgent on Nico's local Mac that runs the three checked-in
e2e scenarios against the user's local swift-infer gateway every 6 hours, files
the result as state on a persistent tracking bead, and pops a native macOS
notification on red transitions. The launchagent is the missing piece between
"the harness exists" and "the harness catches regressions in real time."

## Out of scope

- Broader scenario sweeps via the CLI. The matrix grows inside the test file
  as new bug classes are found; CLI stays for manual exploration only.
- CI integration on GitHub-hosted runners (no swift-infer there).
- Extending `fs notify --auto`'s precedence rules to recognise the
  `health:failing` label — separate factoryskills bead if/when desired.
- swift-infer healthcheck preflight — user's invariant: if the Mac is running,
  the server is running.
design:
## ADR Alignment

No ADR applies — verified via grep on keywords: launchagent, launchd, dogfood, swift-infer, notification, set-state. `bd list --type=decision --status=recorded --json` returned `[]`. No decision beads are recorded in this repository.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `scripts/launchd/run-dogfood.sh` exists, is executable, sources `~/.lenny-dogfood.env`, creates `~/Library/Logs/lenny-dogfood/` if absent, runs `dart test packages/exploration_agent/test/e2e/dogfood_e2e_test.dart` from the repo root, captures exit code, writes `healthy` or `failing` to `~/Library/Logs/lenny-dogfood/last_status`, calls `bd set-state lenny-g8y health=healthy|failing --reason "<summary>"`, fires a macOS notification via `osascript` only on a healthy→failing transition (read from `last_status` before overwriting it), and exits 0 on both green and flake-suppressed-red so launchd does not throttle.
- [ ] `scripts/launchd/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist` exists, references the script via absolute path using `$HOME`, sets `StartInterval=21600`, sets `StandardOutPath` and `StandardErrorPath` to `~/Library/Logs/lenny-dogfood/run.log`, and contains NO `EnvironmentVariables` block (secrets stay in `~/.lenny-dogfood.env`).
- [ ] A `scripts/launchd/README.md` (or `scripts/launchd/INSTALL.md`) documents the one-time bootstrap: copy plist to `~/Library/LaunchAgents/`, run `launchctl bootstrap gui/$UID ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist`, and create `~/.lenny-dogfood.env` with `SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT` and `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN`.
- [ ] `bd set-state lenny-g8y health=healthy` is confirmed to set the `health:healthy` label on the closed tracking bead without reopening it.
- [ ] Flake suppression: a single red run after a prior `healthy` does NOT trigger `bd set-state health=failing`; two consecutive red runs DO trigger it.
- [ ] Green recovery is immediate: a green run after any prior result always writes `bd set-state health=healthy` with no hysteresis.
- [ ] The macOS banner fires exactly once on the healthy→failing transition, not on the second consecutive red (which is a no-op set-state call because the label is already `health:failing`).
- [ ] The script is repo-root agnostic: it resolves the repo path from its own location via `$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)` so it works regardless of where `~/Library/LaunchAgents/` symlinks or copies land.

---

## Touches

- `scripts/launchd/run-dogfood.sh` (new)
- `scripts/launchd/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist` (new)
- `scripts/launchd/INSTALL.md` (new)
- `packages/exploration_agent/test/e2e/dogfood_e2e_test.dart` (read-only anchor; not modified)
- `packages/exploration_cli/README.md` (read-only anchor; not modified)

---

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1 — Create `scripts/launchd/` directory and launcher script

`scripts/launchd/run-dogfood.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Nightly dogfood runner for the lenny swift-infer e2e suite.
# Driven by com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist (StartInterval=21600).
# Secrets sourced from ~/.lenny-dogfood.env — never stored in the plist.
set -euo pipefail

# ── resolve repo root relative to this script ─────────────────────────────
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"

# ── source secrets (not inherited from login shell in launchd context) ─────
ENV_FILE="$HOME/.lenny-dogfood.env"
if [[ ! -f "$ENV_FILE" ]]; then
  echo "[dogfood] ERROR: $ENV_FILE not found. Create it with SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT and SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN." >&2
  exit 1
fi
# shellcheck source=/dev/null
source "$ENV_FILE"

# ── log dir ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LOG_DIR="$HOME/Library/Logs/lenny-dogfood"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
LAST_STATUS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/last_status"
TS="$(date +%s)"

echo "[dogfood] run start ts=$TS endpoint=${SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT:-<unset>}"

# ── run the three canonical e2e scenarios ─────────────────────────────────
# dart test exits non-zero if any test fails.
DART_EXIT=0
dart test \
  --timeout=5m \
  "$REPO_ROOT/packages/exploration_agent/test/e2e/dogfood_e2e_test.dart" \
  2>&1 || DART_EXIT=$?

# ── determine current run outcome ─────────────────────────────────────────
if [[ "$DART_EXIT" -eq 0 ]]; then
  CURRENT="healthy"
else
  CURRENT="failing"
fi

# ── read prior status for flake suppression ───────────────────────────────
PRIOR="healthy"
if [[ -f "$LAST_STATUS_FILE" ]]; then
  PRIOR="$(cat "$LAST_STATUS_FILE")"
fi

# ── write updated last_status ─────────────────────────────────────────────
echo "$CURRENT" > "$LAST_STATUS_FILE"

# ── state machine ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Green recovery: immediate, no hysteresis.
if [[ "$CURRENT" == "healthy" ]]; then
  bd set-state lenny-g8y health=healthy \
    --reason "All 3 e2e scenarios passed (ts=$TS)"
  echo "[dogfood] run complete: healthy"
  exit 0
fi

# current=failing branch
# Two-of-two-consecutive rule: only escalate when prior was also failing.
if [[ "$PRIOR" == "failing" ]]; then
  # Confirmed regression (2 consecutive failures).
  # Notification: only on the transition i.e. prior was failing AND
  # this is the run that confirmed it (second red). We always call
  # set-state; the banner fires only when prior was healthy (transition),
  # so we suppress the banner here because prior was already failing —
  # the label is already health:failing from the previous run's second-red
  # confirmation. Actually, to be precise: we fire the banner on the exact
  # call that first writes health=failing, which is this call when
  # prior==failing (confirming the two-of-two). But we must NOT re-fire
  # on subsequent red runs once already failing.
  #
  # Implementation: read the current bead label before calling set-state.
  # If it's already health:failing, skip the banner. Otherwise, fire it.
  ALREADY_FAILING=0
  if bd show lenny-g8y 2>/dev/null | grep -q "health:failing"; then
    ALREADY_FAILING=1
  fi

  bd set-state lenny-g8y health=failing \
    --reason "2 consecutive failures — dart exit=$DART_EXIT (ts=$TS)"

  if [[ "$ALREADY_FAILING" -eq 0 ]]; then
    # Transition healthy→failing: fire macOS banner.
    MSG="$(fs notify lenny-g8y --event build-blocked \
      --reason "2 consecutive e2e failures (ts=$TS)" 2>/dev/null || \
      echo "Lenny dogfood: 2 consecutive failures — check bd show lenny-g8y")"
    osascript -e "display notification \"$MSG\" with title \"Lenny dogfood\""
  fi
  echo "[dogfood] run complete: failing (confirmed, 2-of-2)"
else
  # current=failing but prior=healthy → single-shot red, suppress escalation.
  echo "[dogfood] run complete: failing (suppressed, 1-of-2 flake guard)"
fi

# Always exit 0 so launchd does not throttle on repeated failures.
exit 0
```

Test: `bash -n scripts/launchd/run-dogfood.sh` — exits 0 (syntax valid).
Commit: `feat(launchd): add run-dogfood.sh nightly e2e runner script`

### Step 2 — Create the LaunchAgent plist

`scripts/launchd/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist`

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN"
  "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
  <key>Label</key>
  <string>com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood</string>

  <key>ProgramArguments</key>
  <array>
    <string>/bin/bash</string>
    <string>REPO_ROOT_PLACEHOLDER/scripts/launchd/run-dogfood.sh</string>
  </array>

  <!-- Run every 6 hours (21600 seconds). -->
  <key>StartInterval</key>
  <integer>21600</integer>

  <!-- Persist across reboots. -->
  <key>RunAtLoad</key>
  <false/>

  <key>StandardOutPath</key>
  <string>HOME_PLACEHOLDER/Library/Logs/lenny-dogfood/run.log</string>

  <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
  <string>HOME_PLACEHOLDER/Library/Logs/lenny-dogfood/run.log</string>
</dict>
</plist>
```

Note: `REPO_ROOT_PLACEHOLDER` and `HOME_PLACEHOLDER` are substituted by the bootstrap step in INSTALL.md using `sed`. The plist template is committed; the installed copy is local-only (`.gitignore` pattern `~/Library/LaunchAgents/` is outside the repo so no action needed).

Test: `plutil -lint scripts/launchd/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist` — exits 0.
Commit: `feat(launchd): add com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood plist template`

### Step 3 — Write bootstrap install doc

`scripts/launchd/INSTALL.md`

```markdown
# Dogfood LaunchAgent Bootstrap

One-time setup to schedule the nightly swift-infer e2e run on your Mac.

## Prerequisites

- `dart` on PATH (via Flutter SDK)
- `bd` on PATH (beads CLI)
- `fs` on PATH (factoryskills CLI)
- swift-infer gateway running at your usual endpoint

## Step 1 — Create the secrets file

Create `~/.lenny-dogfood.env` (not committed):

    SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:8080
    SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN=sk-…

## Step 2 — Install the plist

From the repo root:

    REPO_ROOT="$(pwd)"
    sed \
      -e "s|REPO_ROOT_PLACEHOLDER|$REPO_ROOT|g" \
      -e "s|HOME_PLACEHOLDER|$HOME|g" \
      scripts/launchd/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist \
      > ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist

## Step 3 — Bootstrap the agent

    launchctl bootstrap gui/$UID \
      ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist

## Step 4 — Verify

    launchctl print gui/$UID/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood

Should show `state = waiting`.

## Manual trigger

    launchctl kickstart -k gui/$UID/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood

## Remove

    launchctl bootout gui/$UID/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood
    rm ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist

## Tracking bead

`bd show lenny-g8y` — health label and state-change history.
```

Test: `cat scripts/launchd/INSTALL.md` — renders without errors; step 2 sed command is shell-valid: `bash -c 'echo "REPO_ROOT_PLACEHOLDER HOME_PLACEHOLDER" | sed -e "s|REPO_ROOT_PLACEHOLDER|/tmp|g" -e "s|HOME_PLACEHOLDER|/tmp|g"'` — exits 0.
Commit: `docs(launchd): add INSTALL.md bootstrap guide for dogfood launchagent`

### Step 4 — Smoke-test the state machine logic (manual)

With `SWIFT_INFER_ENDPOINT` and `SWIFT_INFER_AGENT_TOKEN` set in the shell:

```bash
# Dry-run: verify the script resolves repo root correctly
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "scripts/launchd/run-dogfood.sh")" && pwd)"
echo "$SCRIPT_DIR"
```

Test: `bash -n scripts/launchd/run-dogfood.sh` — exits 0.
Commit: (no separate commit — covered by step 1)

---

## Validation Plan

- [ ] Syntax valid — `bash -n scripts/launchd/run-dogfood.sh` → exits 0.
- [ ] Plist well-formed — `plutil -lint scripts/launchd/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist` → `scripts/launchd/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist: OK`.
- [ ] No secrets in plist — `grep -i "token\|sk-\|password\|secret" scripts/launchd/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist` → no output (exits 1).
- [ ] Tracking bead closed — `bd show lenny-g8y | grep -i status` → shows `closed`.
- [ ] set-state command works on closed bead — `bd set-state lenny-g8y health=healthy --reason "validation smoke"` → exits 0; `bd show lenny-g8y | grep "health:"` → shows `health:healthy`.
- [ ] Flake guard — simulate: write `failing` to `~/Library/Logs/lenny-dogfood/last_status`; run script with `dart test` replaced by `exit 1` stub; verify `bd show lenny-g8y | grep health` → `health:failing`. Then run again with stub returning `exit 0`; verify → `health:healthy`.
- [ ] No rogue callers — `grep -rn "dogfood_e2e_test\|run-dogfood" /Users/nico/development/com.nicospencer/lenny/packages/ scripts/ 2>/dev/null` → only the e2e test file and the new scripts; no callers outside expected paths.
- [ ] Repo build clean — `cd packages/exploration_agent && flutter test --no-pub 2>&1 | tail -5` → all unit/widget tests pass (e2e skips due to missing env vars in this context).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `scripts/launchd/run-dogfood.sh` exists, is executable, sources `~/.lenny-dogfood.env`, creates `~/Library/Logs/lenny-dogfood/` if absent, runs `dart test packages/exploration_agent/test/e2e/dogfood_e2e_test.dart` from the repo root, captures exit code, writes `healthy` or `failing` to `~/Library/Logs/lenny-dogfood/last_status`, calls `bd set-state lenny-g8y health=healthy|failing --reason "<summary>"`, fires a macOS notification via `osascript` only on a healthy→failing transition, and exits 0 always so launchd does not throttle.
- [ ] `scripts/launchd/com.nicospencer.lenny.dogfood.plist` exists, references the script via absolute path using path substitution from `INSTALL.md`, sets `StartInterval=21600`, sets both `StandardOutPath` and `StandardErrorPath` to `~/Library/Logs/lenny-dogfood/run.log`, and contains NO `EnvironmentVariables` block.
- [ ] `scripts/launchd/INSTALL.md` documents the one-time bootstrap: create `~/.lenny-dogfood.env`, substitute paths in the plist template, `launchctl bootstrap gui/$UID …`, and verify with `launchctl print`.
- [ ] `bd set-state lenny-g8y health=healthy --reason "test"` exits 0 and sets the `health:healthy` label on the closed tracking bead without reopening it.
- [ ] Flake suppression: a single red run after a prior `healthy` does NOT trigger `bd set-state health=failing`; two consecutive red runs DO trigger it and call `bd set-state lenny-g8y health=failing`.
- [ ] Green recovery is immediate: a green run after any prior result writes `bd set-state health=healthy` with no hysteresis.
- [ ] The macOS notification banner fires exactly once on the first healthy→failing confirmation, not on subsequent already-failing red runs.
- [ ] The script resolves repo root from its own location via `$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../../" && pwd)` making it path-independent.

title: Rebuild per-turn prompting as chat-shape conversation
description: The exploration agent rebuilds its prompt from scratch each turn — AGENTS.md + goal + a model-rewritten running summary + last-5 action ring + full Observation + diff — placing turn-mutating content inside the system message. This invalidates the KV-cache prefix every turn against local Qwen3.6-35B-A3B served by swift-infer, drops thinking traces between turns, and pays per-turn output tokens to maintain a summary that has a compounding-error failure mode. Rebuild the prompting layer as an append-only chat-shape conversation (system = AGENTS.md + goal + tool list; user turns = observation + diff; assistant turns = thinking + tool call), with a renderer abstraction, threshold-driven trim, a hard-cut trajectory v2 schema with typed thinking, and per-provider thinking serialization. Memory tools, checkpoints, compaction, compact rendering, and viewport pruning are explicitly deferred to follow-up beads.
design:
## Architecture

Replace `PromptAssembler` + `RunningSummary` + `ActionRing` with `ConversationBuilder` — an append-only component holding a frozen system message (AGENTS.md + Goal + tool list) and `List<ConversationTurn>`. Turns are sealed: `UserTurn { observation, diff, toolResult? }` and `AssistantTurn { thinking, toolCall }`. Add `ObservationRenderer` interface with `JsonObservationRenderer` as the v1 default. Trajectory stores the typed `Observation`; the renderer runs only at prompt-build time so a future compact-text renderer slots in without breaking trajectory replay.

## Per-turn data flow

observe → diff → builder.append(UserTurn) → builder.trimIfOverBudget() → provider.render(builder) → decide (thinking + tool_call) → builder.append(AssistantTurn) → validate → execute-or-synthesize-error-toolResult → notify plugins → writer.writeTurn(TurnRecord v2).

Two control-flow changes from today: (1) rejected actions become a tool-result user turn with `{error: ...}` on the next iteration rather than triggering an in-flight schema retry — the model re-decides with the rejection in conversation context. Schema-violation retries against ActionSchema still happen synchronously inside `decide` (separate layer, unchanged). (2) No post-decide memory side effects — the assistant-turn append IS the memory update; `_summary.update` and `_actions.push` go away.

## Provider abstraction

Neutral types: `ConversationTurn`, `AssistantTurn { thinking, toolCall }`. Each provider module renders to its native wire format:
- anthropic: native thinking content blocks
- swift_infer (Qwen): `<think>...</think>` text in assistant role
- openai: thinking dropped from history (no reliable carry-forward shape today; still streamed/logged)
- frontier: per-frontier-provider

`ActionSchema` stays for output validation. The `summary_update` envelope field is removed; `rationale` and `wait_strategy` retained (matches existing semantics — they live in modelMetadata, not the next prompt).

## Long-session memory — trim

Threshold-triggered, deterministic, no model call. After each turn append the builder checks estimated prompt token count. If above `trimThreshold` (default 80% of provider context window, configurable per provider), drop `observation` from the oldest UserTurn that still has one; keep its `diff` and the following AssistantTurn. Repeat until under threshold or no trimmable turns remain. System message is never trimmed. Trim is reversible offline — trajectory still has the full Observation. Compact (model-driven fold) is deferred.

## Migration & rollout

Hard cut, no feature flag — the replacement is self-contained and worth the test-matrix savings. Trajectory v2: `TurnRecord` drops `summaryUpdate`, adds `thinking: String?`; `SessionFooter` drops `finalSummary`; header gets `schema_version: 2`; reader keeps a v1 shim for read-only loading of pre-rebuild trajectories. Provider response schema: `summary_update` removed from `ActionSchema`; all four provider response parsers updated.

## Ruled out

- Feature-flagged A/B between old and new prompting: doubles the test matrix and adds two converging code paths for a transient state.
- Compact in v1: no evidence sessions overflow trim alone; cost (synthesis prompt design + test surface) is not justified yet.
- New core memory tools (`core.plan`, `memory.remember`/`recall`) in v1: carry-forward thinking subsumes the planning use case; exact-fidelity recall has no current failure mode pinned on it.
- Compact-text observation rendering in v1: the renderer interface preserves the option without forcing the format decision now.
- `inViewport` / `rect` pruning on the semantics tree: orthogonal optimization, separate concern.

## Touch surface

`packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/{prompt,memory,loop_driver,provider,trajectory,session}/...` plus `docs/flutter_exploration_agent_prd_v0.5.md` §§10, 11, 13, 16.2, 17 (rewritten). All four provider implementations (anthropic, openai, swift_infer, frontier) updated for response-schema + serialization changes.

## Testing approach

Unit: ConversationBuilder (append, trim eligibility, threshold trigger, system-message immutability). Renderer: JsonObservationRenderer round-trip + stable key ordering. Provider: per-provider golden-file render of a known builder snapshot; thinking carry-forward verified on anthropic and swift_infer, explicitly dropped on openai. Loop driver integration: existing turn-loop tests retargeted; rejected-action → tool-result-user-turn → re-decide flow verified end-to-end. Trajectory: v2 write + v1-read-only shim covered.

## Follow-ups (separate beads)

A single bundled deferred bead covers memory tools (`core.plan`, `memory.remember`/`recall`), session checkpoints, and compact (model-driven memory fold) — created and linked as a downstream dependency. Compact-text `ObservationRenderer` impl and `inViewport`/`rect` semantics-tree pruning are noted here as additional future work but no bead is created yet — file when a concrete need surfaces.

title: Hoist session-bring-up helper shared by exploration_cli + AgentDogfoodHarness
description:
Inventory triggered: re-reading exploration_cli/lib/src/run.dart and AgentDogfoodHarness.run() revealed a narrow shared bring-up sequence both call sites duplicate verbatim — handshake walk → PluginManifestRecord construction → SessionHeader assembly → DefaultLoopHost.fromSession composition.

Earlier framing claimed '~80% overlap' between the CLI and the dogfood harness; that was inflated. The actual differences (tier multiplexing in CLI vs swift-infer-only in dogfood; live VmService vs BindingVmServiceFake; canonical-only trace vs canonical+dogfood-overlay; exit-code-int vs DogfoodRunResult) are load-bearing and SHOULD NOT be collapsed. Only the bring-up sequence is duplication.

Goal: extract that bring-up into a single helper used by both call sites. Reduces drift risk (e.g. lenny-9am's TurnRecord.providerRequestId would have needed one site update instead of two if header construction had been shared).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/session_bringup.dart` exists and exports a top-level `bringUpSession` function with the exact signature from the design.
- [ ] `bringUpSession` calls `session.start(goal, config)`, builds `List<PluginManifestRecord>` from `session.handshake.plugins`, constructs a `SessionHeader`, calls `DefaultLoopHost.fromSession`, and returns `(header: ..., host: ...)`.
- [ ] `exploration_agent.dart` barrel exports `bringUpSession` so callers import it from the package root.
- [ ] `packages/exploration_cli/lib/src/run.dart` replaces the inline bring-up block (lines 123–181) with a single `bringUpSession(...)` call, and the `writer.writeHeader(result.header)` call follows immediately after.
- [ ] `packages/exploration_agent/lib/src/dogfood/agent_dogfood_harness.dart` replaces `session.start(...)` + `DefaultLoopHost.fromSession(...)` (lines 183–193) with `bringUpSession(...)`, and passes `result.host` to the `CountingLoopHost` constructor.
- [ ] `dart analyze lib/` passes with no new issues in `packages/exploration_agent` and `packages/exploration_cli`.
- [ ] A unit test in `packages/exploration_agent/test/session_bringup_test.dart` drives `bringUpSession` against a fake `VmService`, and asserts the returned `header.plugins` list is built from the handshake manifest and the returned `host` is a `DefaultLoopHost`.
- [ ] `dart test packages/exploration_agent/test/session_bringup_test.dart` passes.
- [ ] Existing tests in `packages/exploration_agent/test/` and `packages/exploration_cli/` continue to pass after the two call-site migrations.

Two call sites confirmed: run.dart lines 123-181 and agent_dogfood_harness.dart lines 183-193. No additional callers found in the live tree. All *_test.go equivalent Dart test files in packages/exploration_agent/test/ listed: errors_test.dart, vm_service_client_fromvmservice_test.dart, session_test.dart, vm_service_client_test.dart, memory/*, integration/*, provider/*, loop_driver/*, prompt/*, trajectory/*, e2e/, validation/, dogfood/agent_dogfood_harness_test.dart. None of these tests call DefaultLoopHost.fromSession directly in a way that conflicts with the migration. dart analyze lib/ passes clean for exploration_agent (No issues found) and exploration_cli (1 pre-existing unnecessary_import info only). No sibling epic detected (bd dep list returned []). No bringUpSession symbol exists in the tree — the function is wholly new.

comments.author: deliberator
comments.text: deliberator: RESPEC. Committee respec verdict on grade-spread (A↔D, 3 letter steps). Concrete fix: Step 4 of the prior spec cycled through six contradictory API designs about whether bringUpSession calls session.start internally and whether config is a parameter, then declared a 'pragmatic resolution' (caller calls session.start; bringUpSession takes no config) but NEVER propagated that resolution back to the Step 1 artifact code or the Step 3 unit test. Rewrite Step 4 cleanly: (a) Step 1's artifact must reflect the final API (no session.start inside bringUpSession; no config parameter); (b) Step 3's unit test must call session.start explicitly before bringUpSession and omit the config argument; (c) Step 4 should be linear — show the run.dart migration without rehashing the design debate. Other rubrics (scope A, prior-art A, decisions A, concreteness B, coherence B) were clean — keep that structure, only fix the Step 4 / Step 1 / Step 3 consistency.

value:
Standing approval for the supervisor loop / inspector cascade to merge approved work. The exact post-approval action depends on .factoryskills/config merge_strategy:

merge_strategy=direct (lenny project): inspector returns APPROVED via 'fs verdict <id> approved --summary "..."' and STOPS. Do NOT run 'fs pr', do NOT run 'gh pr merge'. Before running 'fs merge', run the relevant package test suites (for Flutter/Dart packages: 'dart test' or 'flutter test' in each touched package; for repo-wide: melos run test or the project's documented suite). If any test fails, do NOT merge — surface failures to the user and pause. Only when tests are green run 'fs merge <id>' — this calls mergeDirect() in factoryskills, which squash-merges the bead branch onto main locally, deletes the branch, removes the worktree, closes the bead, and pulls main if cwd is main. One command, no GitHub round-trip, no PR ceremony. After fs merge: 'bd dolt push' (if remote configured), 'git push', done.

merge_strategy=pr (other projects): existing flow stands — 'gh pr merge <N> --squash --delete-branch --auto' (NOT --admin) so GitHub waits for required status checks before squashing. If checks fail, do NOT use --admin to bypass — pause and surface to human; admin-merging masks lint/test/CI drift. Worktree + branch cleanup after merge: 'git worktree remove --force .worktrees/<bid>'; 'git branch -D <branch>' if local copy lingers; 'git pull --rebase' (stash beads .jsonl first if dirty); then 'bd close <bid> --reason ...', commit beads, 'bd dolt push', 'git push'.

Common to both: do not wait for human ack on PR-ready / approved notifications during the loop UNLESS CI or local tests are failing.

Active gotcha: the factory inspect skill's process.md Step 5 unconditionally runs 'fs pr' on approval regardless of merge_strategy — tracked as factoryskills-wr2 (filed 2026-05-10). Until that ships, do NOT include 'fs pr' or 'open a PR' instructions in dispatch prompts to bitsmith or inspector subagents on direct-strategy projects. The bundled inspect process.md may still try to run fs pr — that's harmless duplication on top of the verdict; if it produces an orphan PR, ignore it (or close manually) and run fs merge anyway. Filed earlier: factoryskills-99f and factoryskills-vhq for the --admin bypass anti-pattern. Decision (2026-05-10): do NOT patch lenny's local copy of inspect/references/process.md — keep skills aligned with upstream and rely on this memory + factoryskills-wr2.

When user says 'autonomous discovery' or 'autonomous decomp' or asks for decomposition done on my own: do the bulk of exploration and story decomposition without asking clarifying questions. Read the source PRD/design doc, propose seams, create child stories, wire dependencies, and set priorities autonomously. Leave any unresolved design questions as blocking decision beads (type=decision) or as 'human' flags (bd human <id>) on affected stories — do not stall the decomposition waiting for answers. The user reviews the decomposition at the end, not the questions along the way.
